## REPORT 1
Title: AHL Alert - LUCK 9MFY26 EPS @ PKR 43.47/share
Created At: 2026-04-30T06:15:05.303Z
Tickers: LUCK
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 69f2f369973030436021344c
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f2f369973030436021344c.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
9MFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 43.47/share.
LUCK reported a consolidated profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 63.6bn for 9MFY26, up 11%
YoY, with EPS of PKR 43.47/share. The growth was primarily driven by strong performance
in local cement operations, which increased 9% YoY, while foreign cement operations
continued to support profitability. Additionally, the automobile and mobile phone segments
recorded improved volumes during the period. On an unconsolidated basis, EPS stood at
PKR 25.07/share in 9MFY26, reflecting a robust 34% YoY increase.
Result Highlights
•
Net revenue for 9MFY26 stood at PKR 377bn, up 13% YoY, driven primarily by higher
sales from the Local Cement, Automobile, and Mobile Phone segments. While Animal
Health (+19%) and Pharmaceuticals (+10%) recorded strong growth, revenues from
Polyester (-20%), Soda Ash (-8%), and Chemicals & Agri Sciences (-4%) declined during
the period.
•
Gross margins for 9MFY26 stood at 25%, compared to 28% in 9MFY25. Meanwhile,
unconsolidated gross margins improved to 38% from 34% in the same period last year,
driven by a 1% YoY increase in dispatches and a 14% YoY decline in international coal
prices.
•
Other income increased 5% YoY to PKR 15.3bn in 9MFY26, primarily driven by higher
cash and cash equivalents.
•
In 3QFY26, the cash and equivalents stood at PKR 178.9bn, up from PKR 134.1bn in
3QFY25. At the unconsolidated level, cash and equivalents increased to PKR 97.9bn
from PKR 64.0bn in the SPLY.
•
Finance costs in 9MFY26 declined 30% YoY to PKR 14.2bn, primarily due to the lower
interest rate environment.
•
The effective tax rate stood at 18% in 9MFY26, compared to 20% in 9MFY25. On an
unconsolidated basis, the ETR declined to 24% from 29% in the same period last year.
Recommendation
We maintain a Buy call on LUCK with a stock is currently trading at forward FY26/FY27
multiples of 6.5x/5.8x P/E.
REP-300
1
Muhammad Anas Habib
D: +92 21 3828 0256
E: anas.habib@arifhabibltd.com

## REPORT 2
Title: AHL Alert - PAEL Profit surged by 63% YoY in CY25
Created At: 2026-03-16T11:26:03.056Z
Tickers: PAEL
Sectors: 0803
Item ID: 69b7e8cb2197b61ecca3e3ea
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69b7e8cb2197b61ecca3e3ea.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Pak Elektron Limited (PAEL) announced its 4QCY25 financial results, reporting a profit
after tax of PKR 3,847mn (EPS: PKR 4.16) for CY25, reflecting a 63% YoY increase. On
a quarterly basis, the company posted PAT of PKR 796mn (EPS: PKR 0.86) in 4QCY25.
Result Highlights

The company reported a top-line of PKR 63,524mn in CY25, up 20% YoY from PKR
53,113mn in CY24. The growth was primarily driven by higher sales volumes in the
appliance division, particularly refrigerators and split ACs, in line with the LSM growth
observed in the category during Dec’25.

Export revenues stood at USD 23-26mn in CY25 (in our view), which the company
started to record in CY25.

Other income rose 55% YoY to PKR 120mn in CY25 (CY24: PKR 78mn), reflecting
improved
non-core
income
generation
from
short-term
investments
and
cash
balances.

On a quarterly basis, revenue reached PKR 16,730mn, up 42% YoY, while PAT
increased 52% YoY to PKR 796mn (EPS: PKR 0.86), supported by lower finance
costs, however margins showed a slight decline.

Distribution expenses increased 31% YoY in CY25, in line with higher sales volumes.
On a QoQ basis, distribution costs surged 50%, possibly due to higher freight costs
associated with exports to the U.S.

Finance costs declined 30% YoY to PKR 2,578mn, mainly due to lower interest rates
during the period. As a result, net margins improved to 6.1% in CY25, compared to
4.5% in CY24.

While we wait for further clarity, the company has presumably booked a deferred
taxation reversal in 4QCY25.
REP-300
AHL Research

## REPORT 3
Title: AHL Alert - ENGROH EPS clocks in at PKR 46.20 for CY25
Created At: 2026-02-27T10:09:24.044Z
Tickers: ENGROH
Sectors: 0813
Item ID: 69a16d54d33a93d06bd571d0
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69a16d54d33a93d06bd571d0.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Engro Holdings Limited (ENGROH) announced its 4QCY25 financial result with a
consolidated profit after tax of PKR 56,533mn (EPS: PKR 46.20 @1,204mn shares),
depicting a surge of 332% YoY. In 4QCY25, Company posted profit after tax of PKR
13,615mn (EPS: PKR 11.31).
Result Highlights

On the fertilizer business front, EFERT’s Earnings declined 20% YoY to PKR 22.6bn
(EPS: PKR 16.95), mainly due to a ~PKR 2bn one-off super tax, lower urea prices,
and a 46% YoY drop in DAP dispatches.

Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) registered a loss of PKR 3,898mn (LPS:
PKR 4.29) in CY25, compared to loss of PKR 362mn (LPS: PKR 0.40) in SPLY, driven
by margins contractions of the polyester segment amid import 38% import penetration
this year and global oversupply.

Frieslandcampina Engro Pakistan Limited (FCEPL) posted a profit after tax of PKR
2,691mn (EPS: PKR 3.51) in CY25 depicting a 22% YoY jump.

Post SPA cancellation of Engro Energy’s thermal assets (EPTL, EPQL, and SECMC),
the company reclassified these assets to continuing operations and reversal of one-
off impairments totalling PKR 53.8bn (PKR 26.6bn attributable to shareholders).

Total borrowings increased sharply (185% YoY) to PKR 332bn (vs. PKR 117bn in
CY24) due to funding of the telecom towers.

The finance cost increased by 121% YoY amid significant surge in long-term as well
as short-term borrowings.

The company booked effective taxation at 23.3% in CY25 vis-à-vis 40.6% in CY24.

The company also disclosed that ~723mn shares were issued, increasing Engro
Holdings’
stake
in
ECORP
from 39.97%
to
100%.
CY25
results
reflect full
consolidation, and the higher share count (1,204mn vs. 481mn last year) impacted
EPS.

## REPORT 4
Title: AHL Alert: HCAR 9MMY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 15.59/share
Created At: 2026-01-27T12:07:15.638Z
Tickers: HCAR
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 6978aa735848514eafc1ff72
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6978aa735848514eafc1ff72.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 27-Jan-2026 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited
Result Review: 9MMY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 15.59/share
REP-300
Result Highlights

Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for
9MMY26 today, posting a PAT of PKR 2,226mn (EPS: PKR 15.59), representing
YoY increase of 117%. On a quarterly basis, the company recorded a 16% YoY
increase to PKR 655mn in 3QMY26.

Net sales during 9MMY26 clocked in at PKR 84,983mn compared to PKR
50,413mn, depicting an increase of 69% YoY, mainly driven by a 69% YoY
increase in volumes to 17,559 units coupled with the impact of two new product
launches, including the HRV Hybrid in Aug’25 and the Honda City 1.5L ASPIRE
S CVT in Sep’25. During 3QMY26 revenue clocked in at PKR 33,103mn in
contrast to PKR 17,845mn in SPLY, depicting an increase of 85% YoY.

Gross margins improved marginally to 7.9% in 9MMY26 vs 7.7% in 9MMY25, amid
stronger PKR-Yen parity.

In 3QMY26, distribution expenses rose 3.1x YoY to PKR 451mn, driven primarily
by higher volumetric sales and costs associated with new product launches, while
administrative expenses increased 27% YoY to PKR 583mn.

Other income surged 97% YoY in 3QMY26 to PKR 374mn due to higher cash and
bank balances.

In 3QMY26 finance cost increased by 157% YoY to PKR 553mn given increase in
borrowings to PKR 14.3bn.

The effective tax rate stood at 43.1% in 3QMY26, compared to 33.9% in 2QMY26.

We have a target price of PKR 301.4/share with HCAR trading at MY26 P/E of
11.3x.
Menka Kirpalani | Leena Abid
UAN: (021) 3828 0256
menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com | leena.abid@arifhabib.com

## REPORT 5
Title: AHL Alert: BOP- Record high 9-Month Profit in 9MCY25
Created At: 2025-10-30T12:11:24.204Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 690355ec86a7908245f4f715
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/690355ec86a7908245f4f715.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 30-Oct-2025 
The Bank of Punjab
Result Review: Record high 9-Month Profit in 9MCY25
REP-300
Result Highlights

The Bank of Punjab (BOP) reported earnings of PKR 5.0bn (EPS: PKR 1.54) for 3QCY25,
reflecting a robust 41% YoY and a 6% QoQ increase. This took the bank’s 9MCY25
profitability to PKR 11.5bn, up 39% YoY. Alongside the results, the bank did not
announce any dividend, consistent with its historical payout trend.

On the balance sheet front, BOP’s deposits were recorded at PKR 1.9trn as of Sep’25.

Earnings momentum was primarily driven by a sharp improvement in net interest income
(NII).

In terms of core operations, the bank’s NII stood at PKR 22.6bn in 3QCY25, marking a
61% YoY increase. This growth was primarily driven by improved spreads, as interest
income declined by 28% YoY but was more than offset by a sharper 43% YoY reduction
in interest expenses, resulting in a net positive impact on income.

In 3QCY25, non-funded income declined by 4% YoY but increased by 12% QoQ,
primarily due to a 73% YoY drop in capital gains on securities, which stood at PKR 734mn
compared to PKR 2.7bn in the same period last year.

However, fee income rose 17% YoY to PKR 3.6bn, while FX income turned positive at
PKR 908mn, compared to a loss of PKR 101mn in the same period last year.

On the provisioning front, the bank recorded a provision of PKR 1.7bn in 3QCY25,
compared to a reversal of PKR 128mn in 3QCY24 and a reversal of PKR 533mn in the
prior quarter.

Operating expenses grew by 19% YoY, reaching PKR 15.4bn. Despite the increase, the
cost-to-income ratio improved to 54.4% in 3QCY25, compared to 64.9% in the same
period last year.

The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 54.5% during 3QCY25 vs 48.9% in 3QCY24.

## REPORT 6
Title: AHL Alert: OGDC - FY25 highest ever DPS of PKR 15.05
Created At: 2025-09-23T09:57:06.243Z
Tickers: AHL, OGDC
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 68d26ef26cfd11cfccc0254e
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68d26ef26cfd11cfccc0254e.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
4QFY25 earnings arrived at PKR 9.37/share, up by 6% YoY I down 15% QoQ
Oil and Gas Development Company (OGDC) announced its financial results for FY25 today,
reporting a 19% YoY decline in earnings to PKR 169.9bn (EPS: PKR 39.50), compared to PKR
208.9bn (EPS: PKR 48.59) in the same period last year. In 4QFY25, earnings remained PKR
40.3bn (EPS: PKR 9.37), marking an 6% YoY increase from PKR 37.8bn (EPS: PKR 8.81) in
4QFY24. Alongside the result, OGDC announced a 4QFY25 DPS of PKR 5.00, taking full year DPS
to highest ever levels of PKR 15.05.
Result Highlights
•
In FY25, OGDC’s sales stood at PKR 401.2bn, down 13% YoY, mainly due to a 6% decline in oil
production to 30,961 BOPD, and 9% decline in gas production to 652 MMCFD. Sales are also
down due lower oil prices. To recall, average Arab Light price stood at USD 75.8/bbl in FY25 vs.
USD 87.0/bbl in FY24. In 4QFY25, topline declined by 22% YoY and also by 14% QoQ to PKR
90.3bn. Lower YoY revenue in 4QFY25 is mainly due to lower oil and gas production.
•
Gross margins for FY25 stood at 57.7% compared to 61.1% in FY24. In 4QFY25, margins stood
at 49.1%.
•
Exploration costs increased by 49% YoY in FY25, amounting to PKR 18.8bn. In 4QFY25, these
expenses increased slightly by 1% YoY to PKR 4.1bn. Expenses increased by 49% YoY in FY25
mainly due to higher number of dry wells and higher seismic activity.
•
Other income increased by 98% YoY, totaling PKR 81.8bn in FY25, driven largely by Effective
interest income on TFCs, higher delayed payment surcharge and exchange gain in FY25
compared to losses recorded in FY24.
•
Trade Debts of OGDC improved to PKR 614bn in Jun’25 compared to 620bn in Mar’25.
•
Cash and Short term investment position of the company increased to PKR 290bn in Jun,25
compared to PKR 266bn in Mar’25.
•
The effective tax rate in FY25 stood at 39.2%, compared to 28.9% in FY24.
Recommendation
•
We reiterate “BUY” stance on OGDC, with a Jun’26 target price of PKR 312/share. The stock is
trading at FY26f PE of 6.9x.

1
Muhammad Iqbal Jawaid I Menka Kirpalani
D: +92 21 38280256
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 255
E: iqbal.Jawaid@arifhabibltd.com | menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 19-Aug-2025 
Oil Marketing Companies: Pakistan State Oil Company Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 44.5 in FY25
•
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited (PSO) announced its financial result for
FY25 posting a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 20.9bn (EPS: PKR 44.5) compared
to PKR 15.9bn (EPS: PKR 33.8) in SPLY, up by 32% YoY.
•
Alongside the result, the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR
10.00/share.
•
Net sales during FY25 contracted by 12% YoY, settling at PKR 3,149bn, amid
reduction in sales volumes of petroleum products by 5% YoY (MS, HSD, and FO
sales down by 4%, 5%, and 47% YoY, respectively) coupled with lower price of
petroleum products.
•
On a quarterly basis, the topline in 4QFY25 declined by 10% YoY to PKR 813bn
on
the back of decline in price of MS and HSD. Meanwhile, petroleum sales
increased by 3% YoY (MS and HSD up 6% and 2% YoY, respectively).
•
Although we await disclosure of detailed accounts, we estimate the revenue
contribution from RLNG segment to be ~PKR 252bn (down 13% YoY) given 6%
YoY decline in DES price, while 28 cargos were handled in 4QFY25 versus 29
cargos in SPLY.
•
During FY25, the company the company posted gross profit margins at 3.1%
compared to 2.7% in SPLY due lower inventory losses compared to last year.
During 4QFY25, the company posted gross profit margins at 2.9% compared to
1.90% in SPLY due to aforementioned reason.
•
The finance costs declined by 36% YoY to PKR 33.7bn in FY25 due to lower
interest rates. In 4QFY25, it plunged by 43% YoY to PKR 6.9bn.
•
The other income during FY25 declined by 6% to PKR 22.1bn. In 4QFY25, it
declined by 72% YoY amid decline in reimbursement of financial charges on line
fill cost, we view.
•
The company booked an effective taxation of 60.4% in FY25 and 63.6% in
4QFY25.

1
Rao Aamir Ali
D: +92 21 32461106
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 242
amir.rao@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 29-Apr-2025 
Power Generation: The Hub Power Company Limited
Result Review: Company reported earnings of PKR 26.4/share in 9MFY25

The Hub Power Company Ltd (HUBC) has released its financial results for
9MFY25, posting a net profit of PKR 34.2bn (EPS: PKR 26.40), reflecting a 31%
YoY decline. This drop is primarily attributed to the termination of the PPA for the
Hub base plant. In 3QFY25, profitability also decreased by 36% YoY to PKR
8.50/share due to the same reason. However, on a QoQ basis, earnings surged
by 162%, driven by a one-off provision of PKR 2.6bn in 2QFY25 related to a trade
debt write-off at the Narowal plant.

Sales during 9MFY25 declined by 32% YoY, again largely due to the Hub plant
PPA termination. In 3QFY25, sales dropped 46% YoY for the same reason.

Other expenses rose sharply to PKR 3.6bn in 9MFY25, 10x increase compared to
the same period last year, mainly due to the provision of PKR 2.6bn related to a
trade debt write-off at the Narowal plant.

The company’s share of profit from associates fell 13% YoY to PKR 30.4bn in
9MFY25, mainly due to reduced earnings from Prime International, we view. In
3QFY25, this profit also declined by 16% YoY.

On a positive note, finance costs dropped by 40% YoY to PKR 12.5bn in
9MFY25, primarily due to a decline in interest rates. In 3QFY25, finance costs fell
56% YoY and 29% QoQ, helped by a reduction in short-term borrowings.

HUBC reported an effective tax rate of 20.1% in 3QFY25, compared to 16.3% in
the same period last year.
•
We maintain a BUY stance on HUBC, with a target price of PKR 162.7/share by
the end of De’25. The stock is currently trading at an attractive forward P/E of 3.6x
FY26E.

## REPORT 10
Title: AHL Alert: FABL - Highest-ever profitability recorded in CY24
Created At: 2025-02-20T09:04:51.075Z
Tickers: FABL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 67b6f033881e05effab47e2c
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67b6f033881e05effab47e2c.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
20 February 2025
Profitability for CY24 recorded at PKR 23.9bn
FABL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY24 at PKR 3.5bn (EPS: PKR 2.3), depicting a
decline of 55% YoY | 48% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 5.2). This took the CY24 earnings to PKR
23.9bn (EPS: PKR 15.7) up by 18% YoY. The jump in overall earnings was mainly on the back of
growth in total income. Along with the result, FABL announced a final dividend of PKR 2.5/share
(CY24: PKR 7.0/share).
Result Highlights

Net Profit Earned by the bank clocked-in at PKR 20.6bn during 4QCY24, down by 6% YoY |
5% QoQ. With this, the total NPE for CY24 went up to PKR 80.6bn, marking an 13% YoY jump.
Profit earned saw a jump of 19% YoY while profit expensed saw a jump of 22% YoY during
CY24.

NFI depicted an increase of 9% YoY | 56% QoQ during 4QCY24, taking CY24’s total to PKR
19.6bn (+50% YoY). This jump in NFI during CY24 was primarily due to higher fee and
commission income which posted a jump of 36% YoY, clocking in at PKR 12.8bn. In addition,
the bank posted a gain on securities of PKR 1,042mn as compared to a loss of PKR 1,812mn
recorded in SPLY. Moreover the FX income of bank increased by 12% YoY to record at 5.3bn
during the year. However, FABL’s other income declined by 87% YoY during the outgoing
year.

FABL booked provisioning reversal of PKR 1.1bn during 4QCY24 (4QCY23: PKR 2.8bn). This
takes total provisioning reversal to PKR 2.5bn during CY24 compared to provisioning expense
of PKR 1.2bn in CY23.

The bank’s OPEX augmented by 21% YoY in 4QCY24 clocking-in at PKR 15.7bn (4QCY23:
PKR 13.0bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 59% in 4QCY24 against 47% same period last
year.

Effective tax rate was set at 70.9% during 4QCY24 compared to 54.2% SPLY. With this, the
effective tax for CY24 stood at 53.8% (CY23: 51.6%)
REP-300
Faysal Bank Limited
Highest-ever profitability recorded in CY24

## REPORT 11
Title: AHL Alert: FFC - Result Review: All time high profitability posted in CY24
Created At: 2025-01-29T11:47:54.069Z
Tickers: FFC
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 679a156a6ae6aa2b53adc908
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/679a156a6ae6aa2b53adc908.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
29 January 2025
Earnings clocked in at PKR 9.96/share in 4QCY24
Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited (FFC) disclosed its financial result today, marking the
first result post merger with FFBL. The merged entity posted a net profit of PKR
64,731mn (EPS: PKR 45.49) during CY24, depicting a surge of 118% YoY. On the
other hand, the profitability in 4QCY24 augmented by 90% YoY to PKR 14,171mn
(EPS: PKR 9.96). The company included FFBL’s financial result from 2HCY24 in
CY24 in announcement, while 1HCY24 and CY23 contains only FFC’s financials. The
company announced a cash dividend of PKR 21.00/share (PKR 34.86/share in CY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales climbed up by 134% YoY during CY24, on account of i) jump in urea and
DAP price by 39% and 9% YoY, respectively, and ii) growth in FFC’s urea and DAP
sales by.2% and 52% YoY, respectively. Similarly, the revenue on a quarterly basis
increased by 3.5x YoY in 4QCY24 amid hike in urea price by 12% YoY. Alongside
this, the prilled urea offtake of FFC experienced a 16% YoY increase, while granular
urea sales of FFBL witnessed a significant growth of 83% YoY. Meanwhile, locally
produced and imported DAP offtake ascended by 31% and 3x YoY, respectively.
•
The gross margins arrived at 34.05% in CY24 compared 40.29% in SPLY.
•
Financial charges increased by 16% YoY, clocking in at PKR 6,524mn in CY24
amid higher interest rates. Meanwhile, the finance charges during 4QCY24
decreased by 3% YoY to PKR 1,567mn on account of lower interest rates.
•
Other income ascended by 106% YoY, clocking in at PKR 35,231mn in CY24, given
higher dividend income from associates, and subsidiaries. Meanwhile, the other
income during 4QCY24 ascended by 48% YoY due to aforementioned reason.
•
The company recorded impairment loss on subsidiary of PKR 4,000mn in 4QCY24.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 48% in 4QCY24 vis-à-vis 47% in
4QCY23.
REP-300
Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited

## REPORT 12
Title: AHL Alert: SGF - Result Review: EPS clocked in at PKR 3.97 in 9MCY24
Created At: 2024-10-28T12:25:59.513Z
Tickers: SGF
Sectors: 0816
Item ID: 671f82d78f84bab98c11cc32
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671f82d78f84bab98c11cc32.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 October 2024
Profitability remained stagnant at PKR 1.75/share in 3QCY24
Service Global Footwear Limited (SGF) announced its 9MCY24 financial result with 
an unconsolidated profit after tax of PKR 818mn (EPS: PKR 3.97), portraying an 
increase of 3% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the unconsolidated earnings clocked in at 
PKR 357mn (EPS: PKR 1.75), remaining stagnant during 3QCY24.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 9MCY24 clocked in at PKR 12.9bn, displaying an increase of 8% 
YoY in contrast to PKR 11.9bn in SPLY. During 3QCY24, sales improved by 3% 
YoY | 25% QoQ, primarily driven by a surge in shoe sales as a result of pre-holiday 
consumer demand.
•
During 9MCY24 gross margin arrived at 16.8%, down from 22.1% during SPLY 
mainly due to increase in raw material and fuel cost. For the same reasons, gross 
margins posted a decline of 550bps during 3QCY24, reaching at 17.5%. 
•
Administrative expense rose by 15% YoY settling at PKR 203mn during 3QCY24, 
attributed to increase in employees' wages and salaries. 
•
Other income declined by 49% YoY to clock in at PKR 77mn in 3QCY24, on the 
back of lower interest earned on diminishing long-term loan to the parent company 
due to declining interest rates.  
•
The share of profit from associates, amounting to PKR 352mn in 3QCY24, 
represents the stake adjusted (18.9%) profit after tax of Service Long March 
(SLM). 
•
The company booked effective taxation at 21.4% in 3QCY24 vis-à-vis 26.9% in 
3QCY23.
REP-300

## REPORT 13
Title: AHL Alert: ILP - Result Review: EPS of PKR 11.25/share in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-26T11:59:57.637Z
Tickers: ILP
Sectors: 0829
Item ID: 66f54cbd8f84bab98cf07af6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66f54cbd8f84bab98cf07af6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
26 September 2024
Earnings declined by 56% YoY to PKR 1.87/share in 4QFY24
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced the result for FY24 today, where by the company 
posted a Profit after Tax of PKR 15,771mn (EPS: PKR 11.25) compared to PKR 
20,172mn (EPS: PKR 14.39) in FY23. On a quarterly basis, the company’s bottom-line 
arrived at PKR 2,616mn (EPS: PKR 1.87) against PKR 6,003mn (EPS: PKR 4.28) in 
4QFY23. Alongside the result the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR 
2.5/share
Result Highlights 
•
During FY24, topline clocked in at PKR 156,129mn, up by 31% YoY. Whereas, on a 
quarterly basis, net sales during 4QFY24 arrived at PKR 43,273mn, depicting a jump of 
23% YoY| 11% QoQ driven by higher export volumes and the full operationalization of 
the Apparel plant.
•
In FY24, gross margins declined to 27.9%, representing a 560bps YoY contraction, 
primarily driven by rising cotton and energy prices. In 4QFY24, gross margins further 
dropped to 22.4%, down from 33.3% in the same period last year, largely due to 
increased costs associated with the capitalization of the apparel plant's expansion 
during the quarter.
•
Admin expenses during FY24 augmented by 37% YoY, while distribution expense 
surged by 42% YoY due to inflationary pressure.
•
Finance costs climbed up by 83% YoY to PKR 10,125mn in FY24 due to elevated 
interest rates along with a jump in borrowings. Meanwhile, during 4QFY24, the finance 
cost reached PKR 2,715mn, depicting a jump of 74% YoY due to aforementioned 
reason. 
•
The company booked effective taxation at 15.2% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 6.9% in 4QFY23.
REP-300

## REPORT 14
Title: AHL Alert: LOTCHEM - Result Review: 2QCY24 EPS @ PKR 0.84
Created At: 2024-08-21T13:50:32.3Z
Tickers: LOTCHEM
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 66c5f0a8311a3f7dccd6e507
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66c5f0a8311a3f7dccd6e507.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
21 August 2024
Profitability up by 4x YoY in 2QCY24
Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LOTCHEM) announced its 1HCY24 financial result
today where the company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 2.2bn (EPS: PKR
1.43), down by 24% YoY compared to PKR 2.9bn (EPS: PKR 1.88) during SPLY. On
a quarterly basis, earnings were up by 4x YoY and 42% QoQ to PKR 1.3bn (EPS:
0.84). Alongside the result, the company’s cash dividend for 1HCY24 stood at PKR
0.50/share.
Result Highlights

In 2QCY24, net sales soared by an impressive 95% YoY, reaching PKR 32.1bn.
This significant increase was primarily driven by a substantial rise in volumetric
sales. As a result of this robust quarterly performance, net sales for the 1HCY24
rose by 67% YoY totaling PKR 64.4bn.

During 1HCY24 gross margin arrived at 6.2%, down from 16.7% during SPLY
mainly due to a 15% YoY decline in PTA margins. During 2QCY24, the company
achieved a gross profit margin of 7%, down from 12% YoY. This decline in gross
margins is largely due to reduction in international PTA margins and an increase
in gas prices.

Furthermore, in 2QCY24, the finance cost increased by 48% YoY, reaching PKR
206mn. This rise in finance costs is primarily due to lower exchange losses
compared to the previous year.

Other income fell by 39% YoY to PKR 365mn in 2QCY24, mainly due to lower
cash and cash equivalent compared to the SPLY.

Effective taxation during the quarter remained 39% compared to 85% during
2QCY24. The decline in effective taxation is witnessed due to the imposition of
retrospective super tax in SPLY.
REP-300
Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited

## REPORT 15
Title: AHL Alert: SNGP - Result Review: 9MFY24 EPS arrives at PKR 16.91
Created At: 2024-11-20T14:05:46.838Z
Tickers: SNGP
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 673decba7789f4a29ca13bea
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/673decba7789f4a29ca13bea.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
20 November 2024
3QFY24: Profitability climbs up by 62% YoY
Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited (SNGP) announced the financial result for 9MFY24
today, positing a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 10,724mn (EPS: PKR 16.91), up
28%
YoY. On a quarterly basis (3QFY24), the earnings arrived at PKR 3,700mn (PKR 5.83),
surging by 62% YoY.
Result Highlights
•
During 9MFY24, the net sales climbed up by 18% YoY, owing to revision in consumer
gas tariff by OGRA coupled with 4% YoY higher RLNG imports. However, RLNG prices
witnessed a 15% YoY decline in lieu of decline in international Brent oil price (down by
12% YoY). In addition to this, the gas production decreased by 3% YoY. In 3QFY24,
the company’s topline augmented by 16% YoY| 6% QoQ which is on account of a
revision in consumer gas tariff by OGRA tagged with 15% YoY growth in RLNG
imports.
•
The operating profit arrived at PKR 121bn in 9MFY24, depicting a surge of 110% YoY
given higher weighted average cost of capital along with reduction in charge of
unaccounted-for gas losses (UFG) due to effective UFG control in place.
•
Finance costs swelled up by 97% YoY in 9MFY24 to PKR 131bn owed to higher
interest rates.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 44% during 9MFY24 vis-à-vis 39% in
9MFY23.

Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited

## REPORT 16
Title: AHL Alert: UBL - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 25.15 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-07-31T08:17:42.878Z
Tickers: UBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 66a9f3266a3cb5cde945ba28
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a9f3266a3cb5cde945ba28.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
31 July 2024
1HCY24: Bottom-line witnessed a growth of 14% YoY
UBL announced earnings today for 2QCY24 at PKR 14.8bn (EPS: PKR 12.1), depicting an 
uptick of 17% YoY while down by 7% QoQ. For 1HCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 
30.8bn, marking a 14% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled by a jump 
in total income and provisioning reversals. The bank also announced a cash dividend of 
PKR 11.00/share along with the result taking total payout for 1HCY24 to PKR 22/share
Result Highlights 
•
The bank's NII stood at PKR 29.2bn for the 2QCY24, reflecting a YoY decline of 22%. 
However, on a QoQ basis, NII saw a modest increase of 4%. The bank experienced a 
significant increase in the interest expense, which surged by 283% YoY and by 17% QoQ. 
Moreover, the interest income also experienced substantial growth, rising by 173% YoY 
and by 16% QoQ.
•
The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 157% YoY during 2QCY24, bringing the 
total for the 1HCY24 to PKR 40.6 bn, a 142% YoY increase. This significant rise is 
primarily attributed to a gain on the sale of securities amounting to PKR 16.6bn, compared 
to a loss of PKR 637mn in the same period last year. Additionally, fee and commission 
income increased by 19% YoY to PKR 11.5bn in 1HCY24. However, foreign exchange 
income declined by 8% YoY, amounting to PKR 6.5bn during 1HCY24.
•
A provisioning reversal was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 644mn, this 
took the provisioning reversal for the 1HCY24 to PKR 2,362mn compared to a 
provisioning charge during 1HCY23 of PKR 3.0bn. 
•
The bank’s OPEX increased by 19% YoY in 1HCY24 clocking-in at PKR 40.2bn 
(2QCY23: PKR 20.4bn, +16% YoY | 2% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 42.0% in 
2QCY24 against 38.9% in same period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 47.5% during 2QCY24 compared to 51.5% SPLY.
REP-300
United Bank Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 25.15 in 1HCY24

## REPORT 17
Title: AHL Alert: LTG buy-back shares from LCI and TGL
Created At: 2024-05-30T09:50:31.089Z
Tickers: LCI, TGL
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 66584be7a7241003c6e4de34
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66584be7a7241003c6e4de34.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 May 2024
•
The Board of Directors of Lucky TG Limited (LTG) has approved the buyback of shares from 
its holding companies, Lucky Core Industries Limited (51% ownership) and Tariq Glass 
Industries Limited (49% ownership).
•
Lucky TG Limited (LTG) was initially established as a joint venture between LCI and TGL to 
develop an advanced greenfield float glass manufacturing facility with a production capacity 
of up to 1,000 tons per day. 
•
The float glass manufacturing facility project has not progressed as planned, resulting in 
significant idle funds within LTG in the form of paid-up capital.
•
The paid-up capital of LTG amounts to PKR 550mn (55mn shares), with 28mn shares owned 
by LCI and 27mn shares owned by TGL. 
•
Despite this, LTG will continue to exist as an entity, with both joint venture partners 
remaining committed to completing the project once the economic environment becomes 
more favorable.

LTG buy-back shares from LCI and TGL

## REPORT 18
Title: AHL Alert: PPL - Result Review: Profitability arrived at PKR 35.43/share in 9MFY24
Created At: 2024-04-29T12:38:35.116Z
Tickers: PPL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 662f94cbb3858eb2c7324c37
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662f94cbb3858eb2c7324c37.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
29 April 2024
Earnings declined by 16% YoY to PKR 10.16/share in 3QFY24
Pakistan Petroleum Limited (PPL) announced its financial result today, posting a net profit
of PKR 96,411mn (EPS: PKR 35.43) during 9MFY24, up by 18% YoY amid reversal of
tax provisions due to favourable judgement from the court, related to depletion allowance
in 2QFY24. On a quarterly basis, the company’s earnings settled at PKR 27,634mn (EPS:
PKR 10.16), down by 16% YoY. In addition to the result, the company announced a cash
dividend of PKR 1.00/share (PKR 3.50/share in 9MFY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 9MFY24 increased by 5% YoY, settling at PKR 224,660mn against PKR
214,175mn in SPLY on account of Pak Rupee depreciation against the greenback by
17% YoY. During 3QFY24, the topline reduced by 2% YoY, arriving at PKR 74,906mn,
owing to i) 6% and 5% YoY fall in oil and gas production, respectively, and ii) a dip in
Sui wellhead price by 7% YoY.
•
The exploration cost declined by 7% YoY to PKR 11,995mn in 9MFY24. Whereas, the
exploration cost during 3QFY24 settled at PKR 3,406mn, plummeting by 14% YoY on
account of the lower cost of the dry well (Maraab X-1) incurred during the quarter.
•
Other income clocked in at PKR 11,538mn in 9MFY24, showcasing a decline of 23%
YoY. Whereas, other income during 3QFY24 arrived at PKR 3,806mn, witnessing a
plunge of 54% YoY due to the absence of exchange gains during the quarter.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 38% in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis 35% in 3QFY23.
REP-300
Pakistan Petroleum Limited

## REPORT 19
Title: AHL Alert: DGKC - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 2.41
Created At: 2024-02-26T15:06:52.174Z
Tickers: DGKC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 65dca90c427e430fa3e64986
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65dca90c427e430fa3e64986.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability climbed by 13% YoY during 1HFY24
D.G. Khan Cement Company Limited (DGKC) announced its financial result for 1HFY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 1,054mn (EPS: PKR 2.41) compared to a PAT of PKR 932mn (EPS: 
PKR 2.13) in SPLY, up by 13% YoY. During 2QFY24 the earnings of the company dropped to 
PKR 394mn (EPS: PKR 0.9) vis-à-vis PKR 543mn (EPS: PKR 1.24), a fall of 27% YoY.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 1HFY24 arrived at PKR 34,784mn, portraying a jump of 17% YoY in contrast 
to PKR 29,761mn in SPLY,  on the back of rise in retention prices. In 2QFY24 net sales 
jumped by 13% YoY to settle at PKR 18,267mn.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY24 rose by 163bps arriving at 16% as compared to SPLY. During 
2QFY24 gross margins came at 12.8% vis-à-vis 13.5% in SPLY. This is primarily 
attributable to rise in exports as a proportion of sales.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in 1HFY24 surged by 136% YoY to arrive at PKR 
1,202mn, on the back of elevated freight charges amid higher export sales, we view. In 
2QFY24, selling and distribution expense settled at PKR 693mn vis-à-vis PKR 176mn, up 
294% due to aforementioned reason.
•
Finance costs in 1HFY24 increased by 28% YoY to clock in at PKR 4,116mn on the back of 
higher borrowing cost. In 2QFY24 finance cost arrived at PKR 2,028mn, an increase of 26% 
due to reason mentioned above.
•
The company booked an effective taxation of 40% in 1HFY24.
1

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability climbs up by 6x YoY to PKR 3.14/share in 4QCY23
Fauji Fertilizer Bin Qasim Limited announced its CY23 financial result today where the
company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 4,403mn (EPS: PKR 3.40) compared to
PKR 2,328mn (EPS: PKR 1.80) in SPLY, climbing up by 89% YoY. On a quarterly
basis, the profitability witnessed a surge of 6x YoY, arriving at PKR 4,048mn (EPS:
PKR 3.14) in 4QCY23. In addition to the result, the company announced a final cash
dividend of PKR 1.00/share in 4QCY23 (PKR 1.00/share in CY23.)
Result Highlights
•
Net sales augmented by 21% YoY to PKR 193,064mn in CY23 on account of i) a
jump in DAP offtake by 28% YoY, and ii) 3% YoY hike in DAP prices. During
4QCY23, the topline depleted by 14% YoY given a reduction in DAP and urea sales
by 19% and 47% YoY, respectively.
•
Gross margins stood at 17.15% during CY23 compared to 16.25% in SPLY owing to
higher DAP prices and DAP offtake. Whereas, the gross margins during 4QCY23
went up by 1,631 bps, settling at 28.38% amid higher DAP prices coupled with a
decline in phosphoric acid price.
•
The other income climbed up by 49% YoY to PKR 9,435mn during CY23, owing to
higher income from cash and cash balances tagged with dividend income from the
subsidiary (FPCL).
•
Finance costs of the company went up by 72% YoY to PKR 8,828mn during CY23
due to higher interest rates. Whereas, finance cost plummeted by 51% YoY to PKR
1,143mn in 4QCY23 amid decline in short-term borrowings.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 60% in 4QCY23 vis-à-vis 67% in 4QCY22.
1
AHL Alert: Fertilizer

## REPORT 21
Title: AHL Alert - ATRL Posts Record 3QFY26 Result
Created At: 2026-04-29T12:40:21.634Z
Tickers: ATRL
Sectors: 0825
Item ID: 69f1fc359730304360d08139
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1fc359730304360d08139.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
3QFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 106.4/share; no payout declared
Attock Refinery Limited (ATRL) announced the financial result for 3QFY26, posting a net
profit of PKR 11,345mn, its second highest ever compared to PKR 1,703mn (EPS: PKR
15.97) in 3QFY25, reflecting a strong 6.7x YoY increase.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales for 3QFY26 rose 18% YoY to PKR 87.7bn, driven by higher volumes and a
sharp increase in ex-refinery prices, with HSD, MS, and FO up 42.2%, 10.7%, and
19.4% YoY, respectively.
•
Gross profit clocked in at PKR 17.6bn, translating into margins of 20.1% (highest ever
in ATRL’s history), primarily driven by higher volumes and a significant expansion in
HSD crack spreads.
•
The improvement in margins is largely attributable to a notable widening in refinery
crack spreads, particularly for HSD. During the quarter, MS and HSD cracks averaged
USD 6.2/bbl and USD 57/bbl, respectively, compared to historical averages of around
USD 5/bbl for MS and USD 12/bbl for HSD, providing a strong uplift to profitability.
•
On the volumetric front, MS sales rose 15.6% YoY to 146k tons, while HSD sales
increased 24.2% YoY to 152k tons in 3QFY26. This growth was supported by reduced
curtailment and stronger demand from OMCs amid expectations of rising POL prices. In
contrast, FO volumes declined 35.4% YoY to 65k tons in Mar’26. However, the bulk of
FO output was exported, still at negative spreads, despite a recovery in international
HSFO prices.
•
Other income for 3QFY26 decline by 9% YoY, settling at PKR 2.2bn due to lower
interest rates, despite a better cash position.
•
On the balance sheet front, cash and short-term investments stand at PKR 98.3bn (PKR
922/share) vs PKR 76.8bn (PKR 721/share), with a book value of PKR 1,494/share.
•
ATRL offers attractive valuations, trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 4.7x and
6.1x, respectively.
REP-300
1

## REPORT 22
Title: AHL Alert - Result Review for Nishat Power Limited / Nishat Chunian Power Limited
Created At: 2026-02-26T10:53:00.03Z
Tickers: NCPL, NPL
Sectors: 0824
Item ID: 69a0260cd33a93d06b5c9d70
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69a0260cd33a93d06b5c9d70.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 26-Feb-2026 
Power Generation: Nishat Power Limited / Nishat Chunian Power Limited
Result Review
Zayan Babar Khan 
D: +92 21 38280256
zayan.babar@arifhabibltd.com
2QFY26: EPS at PKR 1.08 for NPL, PKR 0.94 for NCPL
Nishat Power Limited (NPL) reported 2QFY26 PAT of PKR 384mn, reversing a PKR
4,414mn loss in the same period last year, while Nishat Chunian Power Limited (NCPL)
posted PAT of PKR 347, compared to a PKR 5,245mn loss SPLY. The turnaround for
both companies was driven by provisioning for late payment surcharge (LPS) and excess
profits during the same period last year. NPL provisioned PKR 1.8bn for LPS and PKR
2.9bn for excess
profits, while
NCPL provisioned PKR 2.3bn and PKR 3.3bn,
respectively.
Result Highlights

In 2QFY26, NPL’s revenue rose to PKR 1.1bn from PKR 0.8bn YoY on improved plant
utilization of 2.7% in Dec’25 (Dec’24: 0.4%), while NCPL’s revenue increased to PKR
948mn from PKR 706mn, driven by utilization of 2.3% (Dec’24: 0.1%); however, both
companies’ utilization remained low due to weak power demand and high fuel costs,
capping ROE entitlement at 35%.

Both companies didn’t declare dividends for 2QFY26, amid ongoing investments in the
auto segment and Rafhan, in our view.

Other income remained a key contributor for both companies, with NPL recording PKR
455mn (PKR 0.96/share post-tax) and NCPL posting PKR 280mn (PKR 0.57/share
post-tax) in 2QFY26.

For both companies, elevated other income was supported by surplus cash and short-
term investments in PIBs and Sukuks, (PKR 22bn for NPL and PKR 13.5bn for NCPL
as of Dec’25), and is expected to remain a key earnings support.

We maintain a Buy stance on NPL with a Dec’26 target price of PKR 104/share.

We maintain a Buy stance on NCPL with a Dec’26 target price of PKR 83/share.

## REPORT 23
Title: AHL Alert: SAZEW 1HFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 139.64/share
Created At: 2026-01-27T12:00:42.107Z
Tickers: SAZEW
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 6978a8ea5848514eafc18505
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6978a8ea5848514eafc18505.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 27-Jan-2026 
Sazgar Engineering Works Limited
Result Review: 1HFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 139.64/share
REP-300
Result Highlights
•
Net revenue for 1HFY26 amounted to PKR 67,846mn, up 52% YoY. On a
quarterly basis, revenue surged by 85% YoY to reach PKR 34,021mn,
primarily due to higher volumetric sales of Haval. In 2QFY26, Haval sales
surged to 3,651 units.
•
During 2QFY26, gross margins were recorded at
24.2% compared to
28.4% recorded in SPLY. The decrease in gross margin is due to absence
of concessionary duties on Haval PHEV.
•
The distribution expense significantly rose YoY by 91%, and by 52% on
QoQ amid higher volumes and deliveries of Haval PHEV.
•
The admin expense also rose by 91% YoY and 25% on quarterly basis.
•
Other income inched up by 113% YoY to clock in at PKR 709mn in
2QFY26, which is mainly because of increase in cash and cash equivalent
by 90% YoY.
•
Finance cost in 2QFY26 increased by 52% QoQ due to increase in
borrowings.
•
The company declared cash dividend of PKR 15.0/share in 2QFY26, taking
the total payout to PKR 30.0/share in 1HFY26.
•
The company booked an effective tax 39% in 2QFY26 vis-à-vis 43% in
SPLY.
•
We have a BUY stance on SAZEW with Dec’26 target price of PKR 2,531.
The scrip is trading at FY26 P/E of 6.78x.

## REPORT 24
Title: AHL Alert: EFERT - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 2.17 in 1QCY25
Created At: 2025-04-22T05:59:15.95Z
Tickers: EFERT
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 680730335611029bb9a0948f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/680730335611029bb9a0948f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
22 April 2025
Lower offtake takes toll on profitability
Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) today unveiled its financial result for 1QCY25, posting a
consolidated profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 2,898mn (EPS: PKR 2.17) against PKR 7,760mn
(EPS: PKR 5.81) in 1QCY24, down by 63% YoY. Similarly, the profitability on QoQ basis
plunged by 72%. Alongside the result, the company announced an interim cash dividend of
PKR 2.25/share (PKR 8.00/share in 1QCY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 1QCY25 clocked in at PKR 30,286mn, down 59% YoY which is on account of
53% and 70% YoY contraction in urea and DAP offtake, respectively (which is due to weak
agronomics) tagged with 3% YoY decline in DAP price. On a sequential basis, the topline
plummeted by 64% QoQ which is due to 63% and 77% QoQ decline in urea and DAP
dispatches, respectively.
•
Gross margins in 1QCY25 arrived at 35.3% vis-à-vis 23.3% in 1QCY24, which is due to
higher urea price.
•
The distribution cost in 1QCY25 clocked in at PKR 3,225mn, down by 27% YoY amid lower
volumetric sales.
•
Other income plunged by 76% YoY settling at PKR 1,236mn in 1QCY25 given lower interest
income. On QoQ basis, the other income increased by 34%, owing higher income from cash
and cash balances.
•
Finance cost climbed up by 7x YoY, clocking in at PKR 1,090mn during 1QCY25 owed to
higher short term borrowings. Meanwhile, the finance cost on QoQ basis decreased by 26%
due to decline in interest rates.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 41% in 1QCY25 vs 36% in 1QCY24.
•
EFERT’s short term borrowings as of Mar’25 stood at PKR 28.6bn compared to PKR 12.5bn
in Dec’24.
•
EFERT has cash and cash balance of PKR 9.2bn in Mar’25 vis-à-vis PKR 8.0bn in Dec’24.
REP-300
Engro Fertilizers Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 2.17 in 1QCY25
AHL Alert: Fertilizer

## REPORT 25
Title: AHL Alert: AIRLINK - Result Review: Earnings rose by 7% YoY in 1QFY25
Created At: 2024-10-28T12:22:40.254Z
Tickers: AIRLINK
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 671f82108f84bab98c11afb2
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671f82108f84bab98c11afb2.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 October 2024
Profitability arrived at PKR 2.1/share in 1QFY25
Air Link Communication Limited (AIRLINK) announced its financial result for 1QFY25 
whereby the company, on consolidated basis, declared a profit after tax (PAT) of 
PKR 842mn (EPS: PKR 2.1) compared to an earnings of PKR 789mn (EPS: PKR 
2.0) in 1QFY24. On quarterly basis, net profit declined by 46% QoQ.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline of the company settled at PKR 22,052mn in 1QFY25, down by 12% YoY in 
SPLY. Moreover, on a quarterly basis, sales declined by 40% QoQ amid significant 
pre-buying of mobile phones in the previous quarter, driven by the anticipation of 
increased GST rates of 18-25% introduced in the recent budget, we view.
•
The company posted gross margins at 9.8% in 1QFY25 compared to gross 
margins of 6.4% in SPLY. The surge in margins is primarily driven by the improved 
utilisation of its mobile phone assembly plant. On a QoQ basis margins improved 
by 3.7pps.
•
Other operating income settled at PKR 210mn in 1QFY25, jump by 70% YoY. 
Whereas, on a quarterly basis, other income declined by 11% QoQ.
•
Moreover, in 1QFY25 finance costs soared by 45% YoY to settle at PKR 681mn in 
lieu of higher short-term borrowings. On a quarterly basis finance cost dipped by 
32% due to decline in policy rate.
•
The company recorded effective tax rate of 31.4% in 1QFY25 compared to 7.2% in 
SPLY.
REP-300
Air Link Communication Limited

## REPORT 26
Title: AHL Alert: KOHC - Result Review: Highest ever PAT of PKR 11.6bn in FY25
Created At: 2025-09-08T13:18:36.354Z
Tickers: KOHC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 68bed7ac04ba42474dd78134
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11900ffa16ff64800192
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68bed7ac04ba42474dd78134.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
8 September 2025
FY25: Highest ever PAT of PKR 11.6bn in FY25
Kohat Cement Company Limited (KOHC) announced its financial results for FY25 today,
reporting a 30% YoY increase in earnings to PKR 11,575mn (EPS: PKR 12.59),
compared to PKR 8,893mn (EPS: PKR 9.67) in the same period last year. In 4QFY25,
earnings remained PKR 2,354mn (EPS: PKR 2.56), marking a 1% decline from PKR
2,382mn (EPS: PKR 2.59) in 4QFY24.
Result Highlights
•
In FY25, KOHC’s sales stood at PKR 37.5bn, down 3% YoY, mainly due to a 10%
decline in dispatches, partially offset by higher retention prices. In 4QFY25, however,
topline improved slightly to PKR 8.72bn, up 1% YoY from PKR 8.68bn in 4QFY24, as
stronger retention prices outweighed a modest 1% drop in dispatches.
•
Gross margins for FY25 improved significantly to 39.2%, up from 29.1% in FY24,
mainly due to a reduction in coal prices, better power mix and higher retention prices. In
4QFY25, however gross margins declined by 60bps YoY, reaching 31.2%.
•
Selling and distribution expenses saw a 14% YoY increase in FY25, amounting to PKR
226mn. In 4QFY25, these expenses grew by 20% YoY and 33% QoQ.
•
Other income increased by 19% YoY, totaling PKR 5,281mn in FY25, driven largely by
an increase in short-term investments, which reached PKR 26.9bn by Jun’25.
In 4QFY25, other income declined by 1% YoY but increased by 41% QoQ.
•
Finance costs decreased by 48% YoY in FY25, amounting to PKR 350mn, largely due
to a reduction in interest rates. In 4QFY25, finance costs also fell by 44% YoY for the
same reason, but increased by 31% QoQ.
•
The effective tax rate in 4QFY25 stood at 33.2%, down from 37.2% in 4QFY24.
Recommendation
•
We maintain a Buy call on the stock with Jun’26 ending target price of PKR 120/share.
The scrip is trading at a forward PE of 7.9x for FY26e.
REP-300
Kohat Cement Company Limited
Result Review: Highest ever PAT of PKR 11.6bn in FY25

## REPORT 27
Title: AHL Alert: MTL - Result Review: 1HFY25 EPS clocked-in at PKR 19.01
Created At: 2025-02-19T09:52:32.828Z
Tickers: MTL
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 67b5a9e0881e05effa52325a
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67b5a9e0881e05effa52325a.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
19 February 2025
Earnings of 2QFY25 arrived at PKR 15.86/share, up by 3% YoY I 5x QoQ
Millat tractors Limited (MTL) announced its financial result for 1HFY25 today whereby the
company posted a Profit After Tax (PAT) of PKR 3,645mn (EPS: PKR 19.01), decreasing
by 31% YoY. During 2QFY25, the profitability of the company amounted to PKR 3,041mn
(EPS: PKR 15.86) increasing by 3% YoY I 5x QoQ. Alongside the result, the company
announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 45.0/share in 2QFY25 (PKR 25/share in
2QFY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales arrived at PKR 27,494mn during 1HFY25, down by 38% YoY. The decline is
attributed to lower volumetric sales to 10,107 units in 1HFY25 vis-à-vis 15,099 units in
1HFY24, down by 33% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the revenue declined by 19% YoY
amid decline in sales volumes by 5%. (2QFY25: 7,541units vs 2QFY24: 7,912 units).
However, QoQ sales increased by 3x due to green tractor scheme launched by Punjab
government.
•
During 2QFY25, gross margins were recorded at 25.5% compared to 23.7% recorded in
SPLY and 29% in last quarter. The QoQ gross margin declined due to low price tractors
sold under the tractor scheme.
•
Other income declined by 70% YoY to clock in at PKR 164mn in 2QFY25, which is
mainly because of reduction in cash and cash equivalent (including short term
investments) by 47% and declining interest rate environment during the period under
review.
•
Finance cost in 2QFY25 increased by 4.5x YoY amid increase in short-term borrowings
by 5.4x.
•
The company declared a substantial dividend of PKR 45/share, following the absence of
final dividend in FY24 due to the ongoing merger process.
•
The company booked a tax reversal of PKR 67mn in 2QFY25 vis-à-vis taxation of PKR
2,045mn in SPLY.
REP-300
Millat Tractors Limited
Result Review: 1HFY25 EPS clocked-in at PKR 19.01

## REPORT 28
Title: AHL Alert: BAFL - Result Review: CY24 EPS arrives at PKR 25.27
Created At: 2025-01-30T10:32:01.344Z
Tickers: BAFL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 679b55216ae6aa2b53fbc399
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/679b55216ae6aa2b53fbc399.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 January 2025
EPS dented in 4Q due to decline in NII and higher taxation
BAFL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY24 at PKR 4.6bn (EPS: PKR 2.94),
depicting a decline of 49% YoY | 65% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 5.74). This decline in
overall earnings was mainly on the back of subdued NII and higher OPEX. Along with the
result, BAFL announced a final cash dividend of PKR 2.5/share (CY24: PKR 8.5/share).
Result Highlights

Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 31.6bn during 4QCY24, declining by
10% YoY and 6% QoQ. With this, the total NII for CY24 went up to PKR 126.7bn,
marking a 1.0% YoY jump. Interest earned saw a jump of 23% YoY during CY24 while
interest expense was up by 33% YoY.

The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 9% YoY during 4QCY24, bringing the
total for CY24 to PKR 45.8bn, a 49% YoY increase. The increase is primarily fueled by
a 9% YoY rise in fee income, reaching PKR 17.9bn, and a jump in foreign exchange
income, which climbed to PKR 9.5bn, up by 3% YoY. Furthermore, the bank posted a
gain of PKR 14.0bn from the sale of securities, compared to a gain of PKR 296mn in
the same period last year.

Provisioning for the bank clocked in at PKR 63mn during 4QCY24 (4QCY23: PKR
1.2bn) depicting a decline of 95% on a YoY basis. This takes total provisioning to PKR
271mn during CY24 (-97% YoY).

The bank’s OPEX increased 36% YoY in 4QCY24 clocking-in at PKR 26.8bn (4QCY23:
PKR 19.7bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 61% in 4QCY24 against 43% same
period last year.

Effective tax rate was set at 72% during 4QCY24 compared to 66% in SPLY. With this,
the effective tax for CY24 stood at 53.2% (CY23: 54%)
REP-300
Bank Alfalah Limited
Result Review: CY24 EPS arrives at PKR 25.27

## REPORT 29
Title: AHL Alert: HTL - Result Review: Loss of PKR 0.99/share in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-23T08:27:38.75Z
Tickers: HTL
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 66f1267a0cbf3cea06c24560
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66f1267a0cbf3cea06c24560.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
23 September 2024
Profitability arrives at PKR 1.29/share in 4QFY24
Hi-Tech Lubricants Limited (HTL) announced its financial result for FY24 whereby the
company declared a loss after tax (LAT) of PKR 137mn (LPS: PKR 0.99) compared to
loss of PKR 247mn (LPS: PKR 1.77) in FY23. On a quarterly basis, the company
registered an earnings of
PKR 180mn (EPS: PKR 1.29) compared to a loss of PKR
67mn (LPS: PKR 0.48) in SPLY.
Result Highlights
•
Topline of the company settled at PKR 24,317mn in FY24, up by 56% YoY. The jump
in sales is due to significant jump in overall sales volumes. During 4QFY24, the net
sales witnessed a massive growth of 104% YoY to PKR 8,336mn due to jump in
petroleum volumetric sales amid increase in retail outlets coupled with improvement in
the prices of lubricant.
•
The company posted a gross profit of PKR 2,364mn with gross margins set at 9.7% in
FY24 compared to gross profit of PKR 2,427mn (15.5%) in SPLY. The decline in
margins is primarily driven by a reduction in lubricant sales, coupled with a significant
increase in white oil sales, which is a lower-margin product. Additionally, higher
discounts were offered during the quarter to attract new customers and boost sales,
further impacting margins.
•
Other operating income settled at PKR 358mn in FY24, up by 100% YoY.
•
Meanwhile, finance costs increased by 11% YoY to PKR 801mn in lieu of higher
interest rate in the period under review.
•
The company recorded effective tax reversal of PKR 169mn in FY24 compared to tax
reversal of PKR 149mn in SPLY.

Hi-Tech Lubricants Limited

## REPORT 30
Title: AHL Alert: ENGRO - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 11.67 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-08-22T10:22:11.274Z
Tickers: ENGRO
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 66c71153311a3f7dcc122305
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66c71153311a3f7dcc122305.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
22 August 2024
2QCY24: Bottom-line plummets by 63% YoY
Engro Corporation Limited (ENGRO) announced its 1HCY24 financial
result with a
consolidated profit after tax of PKR 6,261mn (EPS: PKR 11.67), depicting a decline of 42%
YoY. On a quarterly basis, the consolidated earnings clocked in at PKR 2,243mn (EPS: PKR
4.18), plunging by 63% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced dividend of PKR
8.00/share in 2QCY24 (PKR 19.00/share in 1HCY24.
Result Highlights
•
On the fertilizer business front, EFERT’s bottom-line in 2QCY24 clocked in at PKR 1,666mn
(EPS: PKR 1.25) against PKR 1,060mn (EPS: PKR 0.79) in SPLY, up by 57% YoY given
hike in urea and DAP price by 59% and 15% YoY, respectively coupled with increase in
DAP offtake by 16% YoY.
•
Meanwhile, EPCL reported a loss of PKR 688mn (LPS: PKR 0.76) in 2QCY24 compared to
a net profit of PKR 1,562mn (EPS: PKR 1.72) in SPLY, owed to lower PVC margins tagged
with higher gas prices.
•
FCEPL posted earnings of PKR 588mn (EPS: PKR 0.77), up by 75% YoY on the back of
increase in gross margins by 281bps to 17.55% amid higher sales of dairy products.
•
The other income of ENGRO depicted a jump of 72% YoY during 2QCY24, which is
attributable to a surge in income from cash and cash balances.
•
The company booked a reversal of the loss allowance on subsidy receivable from Govt.
amounting to PKR 177mn in 2QCY24 against the loss allowance on subsidiary receivable
from Govt. of PKR 72mn in 2QCY23.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 59% in 2QCY24 vis-à-vis 89% in 2QCY23.
•
In addition to the result, the company re-iterated that the financial results of thermal energy
(EPTL, EPQL, and SECMC) have been reported as discontinued operations given that
these companies have met the classification criteria of IFRS-5 related to “Non-Current
Assets Held for Sale and Discontinued Operation”

Engro Corporation Limited

## REPORT 31
Title: AHL Alert: INDU - Result Review: Earnings arrive at PKR 119.67/share in 9MFY24
Created At: 2024-04-29T12:31:31.21Z
Tickers: INDU
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 662f9323b3858eb2c732036d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662f9323b3858eb2c732036d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
29 April 2024
3QFY24: Profitability climbed up by 38% YoY, settling at PKR 56.61/share  
Indus Motors Company Limited (INDU) announced its financial result for 9MFY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 9,406mn (EPS: PKR 119.67), up by 61% YoY. During 3QFY24, the 
net profit of the company clocked in at PKR 4,449mn (EPS: PKR 56.61) compared to PKR 
3,216mn (EPS: PKR 40.92) in 3QFY23, up by 38% YoY. Alongside the result, the company 
announced a cash dividend of PKR 34.00/share.
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales during 9MFY24 clocked in at PKR 98,232mn in contrast to PKR 135,033mn in 
SPLY, depicting a decline of 27% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the topline witnessed a dip of 
2% YoY. On a sequential basis, the revenue swelled up by 159% QoQ amid 2.4x QoQ 
increase in sales volumes, which is attributable to the new-year effect and launch of 
Corolla Cross.
•
During 9MFY24 gross margin arrived at 11.9%, in contrast to 0.15% during SPLY mainly 
due to a stable PKR USD parity and an increase in overall car prices. On a quarterly 
basis, the gross margins settled at 14.7% during 3QFY24 compared to 6.30% in SPLY, 
due to the aforementioned reason.
•
Distribution expense surged by 5.6x YoY settling at 2,503mn during 3QFY24, which is 
attributable to an upsurge in marketing and distribution expenses associated with the 
Corolla Cross.
•
Other income declined by 19% YoY to clock in at PKR 9,421mn in 9MFY24, which is 
mainly because of decrease in short-term investments. While, on quarterly basis, it 
increased by 35% YoY, due to higher income from cash and cash equivalent. 
•
The company booked effective taxation at 42% in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis  34% in 3QFY23.
REP-300

## REPORT 32
Title: Result Review: POL - Earnings of PKR 61.93/share in 1HFY24
Created At: 2024-02-06T07:24:45.721Z
Tickers: POL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 65c1debd6c1b4b36b6eafc23
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65c1debd6c1b4b36b6eafc23.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY24: Bottom-line climbs up by 32% YoY to PKR 27.73/share
Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after
tax of PKR 17,579mn (EPS: PKR 61.93) during 1HFY24 compared to PKR 14,353mn
(EPS: PKR 50.57) in 1HFY23, up by 22% YoY. On a quarterly basis, earnings during
2QFY24 settled at PKR 7,871mn (EPS: PKR 27.73), up by 32% YoY. Alongside the result,
the company announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 25.00/share in 2QFY24 (PKR
20.00/share in 2QFY23).
Result Highlights
•
Topline in 1HFY24 witnessed a growth of 13% YoY, clocking-in at PKR 34,046mn
compared to PKR 29,998mn during SPLY amid a weakening of Pak Rupee. The net
sales in 2QFY24 arrived at PKR 17,364mn, showcasing a jump of 24% YoY on the
back of Pak Rupee depreciation against greenback. Whereas, realized oil price
plummeted by 1% YoY while oil and gas production reduced by 5% and 3% YoY,
respectively.
•
The exploration costs plummeted by 79% YoY in 1HFY24, arriving at PKR 1,133mn,
owed to the absence of dry well during the period. Meanwhile, the exploration costs
during 2QFY24 reached PKR 414mn, down by a 57% YoY due to aforementioned
reason.
•
The other income registered a decline of 11% YoY, settling at PKR 7,773mn during
1HFY24 on account of exchange loss on foreign currency compared to exchange gains
in SPLY. Whereas, other income during 2QFY24 arrived at PKR 2,881mn, climbing up
by 37% YoY given higher income from cash and cash equivalents.
•
The company’s effective taxation arrived at 30% in 2QFY24 vis-à-vis 36% in 2QFY23.
1

## REPORT 33
Title: AHL Alert: MARI - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 281.14
Created At: 2024-01-29T12:18:29.112Z
Tickers: MARI
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 65b79795912a41f36563e79d

--- Page 1 ---
Net Profit surge by 65% YoY to PKR 137.64/share
Mari Petroleum Company Limited (MARI) announced its financial result today, posting a
bottom line of PKR 37,505mn (EPS: PKR 281.14) during 1HFY24 against PKR
23,862mn (EPS: PKR 178.87) in 1HFY23, up by 57% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the
company’s profitability arrived at PKR 18,361mn (EPS: PKR 137.64), up by a massive
65% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced an interim cash dividend of
PKR 98.00/share in 2QFY24 (PKR 89.00 in 2QFY23).
Result Highlights
•
Topline in 1HFY24 swelled up by 54% YoY, settling at PKR 93,745mn compared to
PKR 61,002mn in SPLY amid i) 17% and 34% YoY jump in gas and oil production,
respectively, ii) 29% YoY hike in the wellhead price of Mari Gas Field, and iii) 22%
YoY Pak Rupee depreciation against the greenback. During 2QFY24, the net sales
expanded by 56% YoY, arriving at PKR 45,539mn given i) higher wellhead gas price
of Mari Gas Field, ii) 21% YoY devaluation of PKR against USD, and iii) 25% and
19% YoY growth in gas and oil production, respectively.
•
The exploration cost contracted by 47% YoY to PKR 3,256mn in 1HFY24 absence of
dry wells during the period. Meanwhile, the exploration cost during 2QFY24 arrived at
PKR 1,465mn, down by 66% YoY due to the aforementioned reason.
•
The finance income arrived at PKR 4,098mn in 1HFY24 versus PKR 2,214mn in
SPLY, up by 85% YoY on account of higher income on cash and cash balances
coupled. Whereas, finance income during 2QFY24 settled at PKR 1,606mn,
showcasing an increase of 35% YoY owing to higher interest income during the
quarter.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 40% in 2QFY24 vis-à-vis 34% in 2QFY23.

## REPORT 34
Title: AHL Alert: EPCL - Result Review: 1QCY25 LPS @ PKR 0.91
Created At: 2025-04-18T06:01:54.055Z
Tickers: EPCL
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 6801ead25611029bb9bcbd5b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6801ead25611029bb9bcbd5b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
18 April 2025
Gas price hike and lower PVC margin take toll on bottom-line
Engro Polymer & Chemicals Ltd (EPCL) disclosed its 1QCY25 financial results today,
registering a loss of PKR 825mn (LPS: PKR 0.91), compared to the loss PKR 901mn (LPS:
PKR 0.99) in the SPLY. To recall, the company posted a profit after tax of PKR 2,128mn (EPS:
PKR 2.34) in 4QCY24.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales for 1QCY25 clocked in at PKR 17.9bn, depicting jump of 8% YoY | -16% QoQ,
driven by higher volumetric sales, we view.
•
The gross profit margin for 1QCY25 stood at 7.9%, showcasing a jump from 6.4% in
1QCY24, which in our view is due to lower depreciation charged. On a sequential basis, the
gross margins observed a 617bps QoQ decline, which is majorly due to higher gas prices and
weaker PVC margins.
•
Finance costs plummeted by 10% YoY | 15% QoQ to PKR 1.5bn for 1QCY25, owing to
decline in interest rates. Similarly, the finance cost on QoQ basis tumbled by 10%, due to
aforementioned reason.
•
Other income dropped by 8% YoY | 47%, reaching PKR 161mn in 1QCY25, on account of
reduction in income from cash and cash balances.
•
Net margins for 1QCY25 were recorded at -4.6% compared to -5.4% in 1QCY25.
•
As of Mar’25, the company has cash and cash equivalents of PKR 4.4bn compared to PKR
5.1bn in Dec’24. .
•
The company recorded taxation charge of PKR 39mn in 1QCY25 vis-à-vis PKR 371mn in
1QCY24.
REP-300

## REPORT 35
Title: AHL Alert - NBP EPS @ PKR 7.58/share for 1QCY26
Created At: 2026-04-29T11:00:31.034Z
Tickers: NBP
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 69f1e4cf9730304360c86d61
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1e4cf9730304360c86d61.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 29-Apr-2026 
Commercial Bank: National Bank of Pakistan
Result Review: EPS @ PKR 7.58/share for 1QCY26
REP-300
•
NBP reported earnings of PKR 16bn (EPS: PKR 7.58) in 1QCY26, declining by
26% YoY and 17% QoQ.
•
The contraction in earnings was primarily due to lower total income, while
provisioning reversals provided some support.
•
The bank’s NII stood at PKR 51.9bn in the outgoing quarter, marking a 25% YoY,
and 1% QoQ decrease. The decline was driven by a larger decline in interest
earned, clocking-in at PKR 178bn, (-16% YoY), while interest expensed declined
by 12% YoY to reach PKR 126bn.
•
Non-funded income declined 20% YoY, largely attributable to capital losses of
PKR 469mn, along with a loss from associates of PKR 423mn and loss on
derecognized assets amounting to PKR 491mn.
•
On the provisioning front, NBP recorded a net provisioning reversal of PKR 3.5bn
in 1QCY26, compared to PKR 6.4bn in 1QCY25, and PKR 7.1bn in 4QCY25.
•
OPEX grew mildly by 6.8% YoY, however, the decline in total income took the
bank’s cost-to-income ratio at 50%, vs 35% in SPLY.
•
The effective tax rate of the bank was recorded at 53% for the quarter, (vs 53% in
1QCY25 and 46% in 4QCY25).
•
In terms of balance sheet dynamics, the bank’s deposits grew by 6% YoY to reach
PKR 4.1trn, while investments grew to their highest recorded level of PKR 5.2trn
(+8% YoY) and advances remained flat at PKR 1.3trn. This took the bank’s IDR to
126% and ADR to 31% for 1QCY26.
•
The stock is currently trading at a P/B of 0.8x, and a DY of 15% for CY26e based
on LDCP.

## REPORT 36
Title: AHL Alert - MLCF Earnings Per Share Stood at PKR 5.58 in 1HFY26
Created At: 2026-02-25T06:42:43.746Z
Tickers: MLCF
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 699e99e3d33a93d06bb02062
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699e99e3d33a93d06bb02062.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
1HFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 5.58/share
Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited (MLCF) announced its financial results for 1HFY26 today,
reporting consolidated earnings of PKR 5.84bn (EPS: PKR 5.58), showcasing a jump of
15.0% YoY. Higher earnings are due to higher Net Revenue, lower distribution expenses and
decrease in finance cost.
Result Highlights
•
During 1HFY26, revenue increased by 2% YoY to PKR 35.4bn compared to PKR 34.7bn
in 1HFY25. The inclined was driven primarily by increase in total dispatches rose 14%
YoY.
•
Gross margins decreased slightly to 34.3% in 1HFY26 (down from 36% in 1HFY25), due
to a unavailability of Afghan coal, leading to higher fuel cost but this is mitigated by rise in
total dispatches.
•
Selling and distribution expenses decreased by 33.2% YoY in 1HFY26, settling at PKR
1.59bn. Decline in distribution costs is due to company switching from prior direct
distribution model.
•
Finance costs declined sharply, down 74% YoY in 1HFY26. This is attributed to lower
interest rates and the company not having any short term borrowings on its balance sheet
in 2QFY26 compared to PKR 37.1bn in 2QFY25.
•
Other income decreased by 34% to PKR 0.79bn in 1HFY26. Decrease is due to lower
cash and short term investments of PKR 16.0bn compared to PKR 44.4bn in 2QFY25.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 34.6% in 1HFY26 vs 29.7% in 1HFY25.
We maintain a BUY stance on MLCF with a Dec’26 Target price of PKR 148/share. Currently,
the stock is trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 8.0x and 6.1x, respectively.
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 25-Feb-2026

## REPORT 37
Title: AHL Alert: APL - Result Review: Profitability arrives at PKR 41.18/share in 1HFY25
Created At: 2025-01-27T08:04:03.375Z
Tickers: APL
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 67973df36ae6aa2b53eaa2fd
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67973df36ae6aa2b53eaa2fd.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
27 January 2025
Earnings up by 8% YoY to PKR 22.01/share in 2QFY25
Attock Petroleum Limited (APL) announced the financial result for 1HFY25, whereby the
company has posted a net profit of PKR 5,123mn (EPS: PKR 41.18) against PKR 7,800mn
(EPS: PKR 62.69) in 1HFY24, down 34% YoY. During 2QFY25, the bottom-line settled at
PKR 2,739mn (EPS: PKR 22.01), up 8% YoY. Alongside the result, the company
announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 12.50/share in 2QFY25 (PKR 10.00 in
2QFY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales during 1HFY25 declined by 15% YoY, clocking in at PKR 232bn, which is owed
to i) lower average retail price of petroleum products, and ii) reduction in MS and FO
offtake by 2% and 60% YoY, respectively. On a quarterly basis, the topline settled at PKR
119bn, down 12% YoY amid fall in MS and HSD prices coupled with decline in MS and
FO dispatches by 5% and 89% YoY, respectively. Meanwhile, HSD sales improved by 6%
YoY.
•
Gross margins of the company reduced by 144bps YoY to 3.48% in 1HFY25. On the
other hand, the gross margins in 2QFY25 arrived at 3.4% vis-à-vis 2.3% in SPLY owed to
inventory gains during the quarter.
•
The operating expenses during 1HFY25 increase by 7% YoY, arriving at PKR 4,130mn
due to higher depreciation charge.
•
Finance income in 1HFY25 depicted a decrease of 8% YoY to PKR 4,058mn on the back
of reduction in income from cash and cash balances.
•
Finance cost increased by 29% YoY to PKR 997mn in 1HFY25 given a higher mark-up
charged on late payments during the period.
•
The company recorded effective taxation at 34.73% in 2QFY25 vis-à-vis 36.23% in
2QFY24.
REP-300
Attock Petroleum Limited

## REPORT 38
Title: AHL Alert: SYS - Result Review: EPS clocked in @ PKR 29.8 during CY23
Created At: 2024-03-25T06:01:27.253Z
Tickers: SYS
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 6601133745ebe0f8ff6b1d17
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6601133745ebe0f8ff6b1d17.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
4QCY23: Earnings arrive at PKR 5.3/share
Systems Limited (SYS) announced the financial result for CY23 whereby the company 
posted a PAT of PKR 8,689mn (EPS: PKR 29.8) against a PAT of PKR 6,629mn (EPS: 
22.8) during CY22, up by 31% YoY. Whereas, earnings in 4QCY23 clocked in at PKR 
1,542mn (EPS: PKR 5.3), up by 2% YoY. In addition to the result, the company announced a 
final cash dividend of PKR 6.00/share (PKR 5.00/share in CY22).
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales in CY23 clocked in at PKR 53,435mn, up by 68% YoY which is on account of 
187% and 71% YoY growth in telecommunications services and technology-related 
solutions, respectively. On a quarterly basis, the topline during 4QCY23 surged by 39% 
YoY, settling at PKR 16,102mn. 
•
Gross margins in CY23 arrived at 25.56% vis-à-vis 27.19% in CY22. This reduction in 
gross margins was predominantly driven by inflationary adjustments in compensation, 
rising energy costs, and the substantial amortization expenses associated with CLOS. The 
gross margins in 4QCY23 arrived at 22.00% (up by 240bps YoY).
•
Other income witnessed an increase of 43% YoY settling at PKR 3,194mn in CY23 due to 
significant exchange gains amid PKR depreciation. However, in 4QCY23, the other 
income came out to be PKR 177mn, down by 64% YoY, mainly due to the dividend 
income received in SPLY.
•
Finance cost elevated by 202% YoY, clocking in at PKR 869mn during CY23. In addition, 
the finance cost in 4QCY23 increased by 60% YoY, reaching PKR 215mn owed to jump in 
the short-term borrowings and higher interest rate environment.
•
Administrative and Distribution expenses grew by 72%YoY, possibly due to inflationary 
pressure and an increase in the resource pool.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 11.0% in 4QCY23 vs 4.5% in SPLY. 
1

## REPORT 40
Title: AHL Alert: BOP - Result Review: 3QCY24 EPS arrives at PKR 1.07
Created At: 2024-10-30T15:24:33.632Z
Tickers: BOP
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 67224fb18f84bab98cdd1e3e
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
30 October 2024
9MCY24: EPS up by 34% YoY due to higher NFI and provisioning reversal
BOP reported its 3QCY24 earnings at PKR 3.5bn (EPS: PKR 1.07), up by 42% on YoY
| 35% QoQ. For 9MCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 7.6bn, marking a 34% YoY
uptick. The surge in earnings on YoY basis is on the back of jump in non-funded
income and provisioning reversal.
Result Highlights

Interest earned by the bank settled at PKR 91.8bn during 3QCY24, declining by 5%
YoY | +5% QoQ while interest expense down up by 9% YoY | 2% QoQ reaching
PKR 78.4bn. With this, Net Interest Income of the bank clocked in at PKR 13.3bn in
3QCY24, depicting a jump of 25% YoY (9MCY24: PKR 29.3bn, +4.0% YoY).

The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 94% YoY during 3QCY24, bringing
the total for 9MCY24 to PKR 15.8bn, an 80% YoY increase. The increase is
primarily fueled by a gain on sale of securities, which went up by 15x YoY.
Moreover, fee income also surged by 18% YoY to settle at PKR 7.6bn. Additionally,
other income saw a 17% YoY surge to PKR 2,346mn in 9MCY24.

A provisioning reversal was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR
128mn, this took the provisioning reversal for the 9MCY24 to PKR 3,566mn
compared to a provisioning charge of PKR 296mn during 9MCY23.

The bank’s OPEX increased by 37% YoY | 4% QoQ in 3QCY24 clocking in at PKR
12.2bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 63.7% in 3QCY24 against 64.9% SPLY.

Effective tax rate during 3QCY24 stood at 49.2% compared to 49.8% SPLY.

## REPORT 41
Title: AHL Alert: ACPL - Result Review: FY24 EPS @ PKR 25.95
Created At: 2024-09-02T10:01:50.178Z
Tickers: ACPL
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 66d58d0e311a3f7dcc23daa8
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
2 September 2024
4QFY24: Earnings arrived at PKR 9.81/share
Attock Cement Pakistan Limited (ACPL) announced its financial result for FY24 today,
posting an earnings of PKR 3,567mn (EPS: PKR 25.95) compared to PKR 1,516 (EPS:
PKR 11.03) in SPLY, a jump of 135% YoY, mainly owed to gain on sale of the subsidiary
‘Saqr AL Keetan for Cement Production Company Limited’. During 4QFY24 earnings
came at PKR 1,348mn (EPS: PKR 9.81) against PKR 459mn (EPS: PKR 3.34), a surge
of 194% YoY, due to the reason stated above. Along with the result, the company also
announced a cash dividend of PKR 4.00/share (FY24: PKR 6.50/share).
Result Highlights
•
Topline during FY24 settled at PKR 28,537mn as compared to PKR 25,477mn in
SPLY, a growth of 12% YoY, due to higher retention prices along with higher
dispatches of 2,341k (up 15% YoY). In 4QFY24 net sales fell by 3% YoY to settle at
PKR 6,843mn against PKR 7,088mn in SPLY, amid fall in local dispatches by 10%
YoY for the quarter.
•
Gross margins for FY24 arrived at 19% vis-à-vis 22% in SPLY, on the back of rise in
exports. During 4QFY24 gross margins fell by 800bps to 18% as compared to SPLY,
due to the aforementioned reason.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in FY24 increased by 38% YoY, attributed to the
elevated freight charges on account of axle load factor, to settle at PKR 2,590mn, we
view. In 4QFY24, selling and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 473mn vis-à-vis
PKR 569mn, down by 17% YoY.
•
Finance costs in FY24 rose by 18% YoY to PKR 186mn on the back elevated policy
rate. In 4QFY24, the finance cost arrived at PKR 154mn.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 48% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 61% in SPLY.
REP-300
Attock Cement Pakistan Limited
