--- Page 1 ---
29 August 2024
▪
AKBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 2QCY24 at PKR 4.4bn (EPS: PKR 3.04), 
depicting an increase of +6% YoY | +19% QoQ (2QCY23 EPS: PKR 2.85). With this, 
1HCY24’s EPS settles at PKR 8.0bn (EPS: 5.58), down by 8% YoY (1HCY23: PKR 6.10).
▪
During 1HCY24, on a YoY basis, higher non-markup income and provisioning reversals 
contributed to an increase in overall profitability. 
▪
In 2QCY24, the bank reported a Net Interest Income of PKR 12bn, down 7% YoY and 7% 
from the previous quarter.
▪
Interest earned surged by 48% in 2QCY24 YoY and rose 5% from the previous quarter. 
However, interest expenses climbed by 60% compared to the same period last year and 
increased by 6% QoQ.
▪
In the outgoing quarter, the bank's improved profitability compared to the previous year 
was largely driven by a 9% YoY increase in Non-Funded Income. This boost was primarily 
due to gains from the sale of securities, which amounted to PKR 625mn, up from PKR 
117mn in the same period last year. Additionally, fee income saw an increase of 13% YoY, 
reaching PKR 1.9bn. However, FX income declined by 36%, totaling PKR 963mn, down 
from PKR 1.5bn in the corresponding period last year. 
▪
In the 2QCY24, the bank reported a reversal in provisioning of PKR 1.6bn, compared to a 
provisioning charge of PKR 675mn recorded in the same period last year. With this, total 
provisioning reversals for 1HCY24 stand at PKR 452mn against a charge of PKR 617mn 
recorded SPLY.
▪
The operating expense of the bank clocked in at PKR 8.6bn in 2QCY24, up 20% YoY | 6% 
QoQ. With this, the Cost/Income ratio stood at 54% for 2QCY24, up from 43% in the same 
period last year.
▪
The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 50.7% during 2QCY24, showing an increase 
from 52.2% in the same period last year.

## REPORT 43
Title: AHL Alert - HBL Highest DPS and PAT announced in CY25
Created At: 2026-02-18T11:56:41.038Z
Tickers: HBL
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 6995a8f92c2a6a5d4906794d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6995a8f92c2a6a5d4906794d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 18-Feb-2026 
Commercial Bank: Habib Bank Limited
Result Review: Highest DPS and PAT announced in CY25 
REP-300

HBL reported earnings of PKR 15.4bn (EPS: PKR 10.52) for 4QCY25, reflecting a
7% YoY increase, though down by 9% QoQ. This took CY25 profitability to highest
recorded level of PKR 66.7bn, up 14% YoY. Alongside the results, the bank
announced a cash dividend of PKR 6/share, bringing the cumulative payout for CY25
to PKR 20/share, compared to PKR16.25/share in CY24.

Earnings momentum was attributable to higher Net Interest Income and a lower
provisioning expense.

The bank reported its interest income at PKR 681bn (-16% YoY) and interest
expensed at PKR 405bn (-29% YoY), causing the NII to increase by 12% YoY,
settling at PKR 275bn.

Non-funded income registered a 6% growth YoY, contributed by increases in gains
booked on securities to PKR 13.6bn (+30.2% YoY), forex income to PKR 8.9bn
(+12.9% YoY) and dividend income to PKR 3.5bn (+14.5% YoY).

Meanwhile, fee income declined 5% YoY to PKR 46.8bn, while other income dropped
50% YoY to PKR 12.6bn.

On the provisioning front, the bank recognized relatively lower provisioning expenses
at PKR 9.1bn (-66% YoY) in CY25 compared to PKR 26.6bn in CY24.

OPEX grew by 4%, which brought the Cost-to-Income ratio to 56.5% in CY25 vs 57%
in CY24, while the effective tax rate was reported at 55% vs 52% in CY24.

In terms of balance sheet dynamics, deposits grew by 27% YoY to reach highest
ever level of PKR 5.5trn, while investments and advances were recorded at PKR
4.2trn (+66% YoY) and PKR 2.1trn (-15% YoY) respectively.
Valuation

The stock is currently trading at a P/B multiple of 0.9x and a Dividend Yield of 6.5%
based on LDCP.

## REPORT 44
Title: AHL Alert: HCAR- 1HMY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 11/share
Created At: 2025-11-21T04:29:36.261Z
Tickers: HCAR
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 691feab0ae4e0b8d022888ca
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/691feab0ae4e0b8d022888ca.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 20-Nov-2025 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited
Result Review: 1HMY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 11/share
REP-300
Result Highlights

Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result
for 1HMY26 today, posting a PAT of PKR 1,571mn (EPS: PKR 11.00),
representing YoY increase of 3.4x. On a quarterly basis, the company
recorded a 2.9x YoY increase to PKR 742mn in 2QMY26.

Net sales during 1HMY26 clocked in at PKR 51,880mn compared to PKR
32,567mn, depicting an increase of 59% YoY, mainly driven by a 34% YoY
increase in volumes to 11,818 units (Civic & City: 10,168 units; BRV & HRV:
1,650 units).
During 2QMY26 clocked in at PKR 25,418mn in contrast to
PKR 16,519mn in SPLY, depicting an increase of 53% YoY
primarily
supported by a 46% increase in volumes, following the launch of the HRV
Hybrid in Aug’25 and the Honda City 1.5L ASPIRE S CVT in Sep’25.

Gross margins improved to 8.1% vs 6.9% in 1HMY25. On a quarterly basis,
the margins remained stable at 7.4% in 2QMY26.

Other income surged 483% YoY in 2QMY26 to PKR 495mn due to higher
cash and bank balances which were recorded at PKR 1.5bn.

Finance cost increased by 63% YoY to PKR 315mn given increase in
borrowings.

The effective tax rate stood at 33.9% in 2QMY26, compared to 39.6% in
2QMY25.

## REPORT 45
Title: AHL Alert: Result review: MCB - EPS @ PKR 15.0 in 1QCY24
Created At: 2024-04-24T10:40:55.507Z
Tickers: MCB
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 6628e1b7a85d383ee3f42ec5
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6628e1b7a85d383ee3f42ec5.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
24 April 2024
▪
MCB announced earnings (PAT) today for 1QCY24 at PKR 17.8bn (EPS: PKR 15.04), 
depicting an increase of 28% YoY | 6% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 14.24). 
▪
On a YoY basis, higher markup and non-markup income contributed to an increase in overall 
profitability. However, on a QoQ basis, provisioning reversals and lower OPEX supported the 
earnings.  
▪
Along with the result, MCB announced a cash dividend of PKR 9.0/share (1QCY23: PKR 6.0). 
▪
During the 1QCY24, the bank recorded a Net Interest Income of PKR 42.4bn, reflecting an 
28% increase from the corresponding period in the previous year but a 8% decrease from the 
preceding quarter. 
▪
The interest earned surged by 43% compared to the previous year but slightly decreased by 
3% from the previous quarter. However, interest expenses witnessed a substantial 56% 
increase compared to the same period last year, although up only 1% QoQ.
▪
In the outgoing quarter, a significant factor influencing the bank's overall profitability on a YoY 
basis was the substantial increase in Non-Funded Income. This surge was primarily driven by 
FX Income, which soared to PKR 1.9bn, representing an impressive 86% YoY growth. 
Additionally, fee and dividend income exhibited robust increases of 46% and 22% YoY, 
respectively. However, there was a significant decline in gain on securities, plummeting by 
93% YoY to PKR 6mn.
▪
In the 1QCY24, the bank reported a reversal in provisioning of PKR 28mn, compared to a 
provisioning charge of PKR 740mn recorded in the same period last year. However, on a QoQ 
basis, the provisioning reversal declined by 96%.
▪
The operating expense of the bank clocked in at PKR 17.2bn in 1QCY24, up 21% YoY, 
however down 3% QoQ. With this, the Cost/Income ratio stood at 33.1% for 1QCY24, down 
from 35.8% in the same period last year.
▪
The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 49% during 1QCY24, showing an increase from 
43% in the same period last year. 
REP-300
MCB Bank Limited

## REPORT 46
Title: Result Review: MEBL: Profitability soars amid revenue surge
Created At: 2024-02-15T09:52:35.8Z
Tickers: MEBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 65cddee399cade535855575c
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65cddee399cade535855575c.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Highest-ever profitability recorded in CY23
MEBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY23 at PKR 26.9bn (EPS: PKR 15.00),
depicting an increase of 65% YoY | 4% QoQ (4QCY22 EPS: PKR 8.16). This jump in
overall earnings was mainly on the back of growth in total income. Along with the result,
MEBL announced a final dividend of whopping PKR 8/share (CY23: PKR 20/share).
Result Highlights
•
Net Profit Earned by the bank clocked-in at PKR 71.8bn during 4QCY23, increasing
61% YoY and 12% QoQ. With this, the total NPE for CY23 went up to PKR 226.5bn,
marking an 86% YoY jump. Profit earned and expensed both saw a jump of 86% YoY
during CY23.
•
NFI depicted an increase of 77% YoY | 10% QoQ during 4QCY23, taking CY23’s total
to PKR 24bn (+19% YoY). This jump in NFI during CY23 was primarily due to higher
fee and commission income which posted a jump of 33% YoY, clocking in at PKR
19.6bn. However, MEBL’s FX income declined by 14% YoY during the outgoing year.
In addition, the bank posted a loss on securities of PKR 698mn as compared to a loss
of PKR 100mn recorded in SPLY.
•
MEBL booked provisioning of PKR 2.9bn during 4QCY23 (4QCY22: PKR 2.4bn)
depicting a jump of 20% on a YoY basis. This takes total provisioning to PKR 7.3bn
during CY23 (CY22: PKR 4.2bn).
•
The bank’s OPEX increased 49% YoY in 4QCY23 clocking-in at PKR 21.4bn
(4QCY22: PKR 14.3bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 27% in 4QCY23 against
29.4% same period last year.
•
Effective tax rate was set at 52% during 4QCY23 compared to 49% SPLY. With this,
the effective tax for CY23 stood at 50% (CY22: 49%).

Title: AHL Alert: HBL - Result Review: EPS @ PKR 12.1 in 2QCY25

Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 688b3ed5aa3685d11835ec9f

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 31-Jul-2025 
Commercial Bank: Habib Bank Limited
Result Review: EPS @ PKR 12.1 in 2QCY25
REP-300

HBL reported earnings of PKR 17.8bn (EPS: PKR 12.1) for 2QCY25, reflecting a 24%
YoY and 7% QoQ increase. This took 1HCY25 profitability to PKR 34.4bn, up 16%
YoY. Alongside the results, the bank announced a cash dividend of PKR 4.5/share,
bringing the cumulative payout for 1HCY25 to PKR 9.0/share.

On the balance sheet front, HBL’s deposits surged to a record high, exceeding PKR
5.0trn as of Jun’25.

Earnings improvement was primarily driven by stronger net interest income (NII) and
higher non-funded income, despite a YoY drop in gross interest earned.

In the outgoing quarter of 2QCY25, the bank reported its Net Interest Income at PKR
68.9bn, marking a jump of 12% YoY compared to the SPLY

The bank witnessed a decline of 20% YoY in interest earned during 2QCY25. Interest
expenses also fell, down 34% YoY, leading to increase in net interest income.

Non-funded income also registered solid growth, increasing 17% YoY and 5% QoQ,
fueled by a 184% YoY surge in capital gains to PKR 5.3bn and a 58% YoY rise in other
income to PKR 4.3bn.

However, fee income declined 12% YoY to PKR 10.7bn, while dividend income
dropped 10% YoY and 73% QoQ to PKR 380mn

On the provisioning front, the bank booked a charge of PKR 1.9bn, lower than PKR
6.4bn in 2QCY24 and PKR 2.7bn in the preceding quarter.

Operating expenses grew by 9% YoY, reaching PKR 50.9bn. Despite the increase, the
cost-to-income ratio improved to 55.7% in 2QCY25, compared to 57.7% in the same
period last year.

The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 53.9% during 2QCY25 vs 49.5% in
2QCY24.

## REPORT 48
Title: AHL Alert - SNGP: Result Review: 9MFY23 EPS @ PKR 13.24
Created At: 2023-11-15T15:54:10.085Z
Tickers: SNGP
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 6554e9a2ea4eb5b1ba89255b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6554e9a2ea4eb5b1ba89255b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Bottom-line reduced by 11% YoY
Sui Norther Gas Pipelines Limited (SNGP) announced the financial result for 9MFY23
today, posting a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 8,399mn (EPS: PKR 13.24), down by 11%
YoY. During the 3QFY23, the company reported an earnings of PKR 2,281mn (EPS: PKR
3.60), plunging by 11% YoY.
Result Highlights 
•
During 9MFY23 sales witnessed a jump of 22% YoY, which is on account of 7% YoY
increase in RLNG prices in lieu of rising international Brent Oil price (up by 19% YoY) to
USD 93.81/bbl vs. USD 78.93/bbl in SPLY. Meanwhile, RLNG imports declined by 15%
YoY in 9MFY23. Apart from this, domestic volumes fell amid the natural decline of local
gas reserves. In 3QFY23, the company’s topline augmented by 47% YoY| 13% QoQ
which is on account of a revision in consumer gas tariff by OGRA.
•
The operating profit arrived at PKR 25.9bn in 9MFY23 vis-à-vis PKR 15.8bn in SPLY
given the reduction charge of unaccounted-for gas losses (UFG) due to effective UFG
control in place coupled with a fall in domestic volumes which absorbed the impact of a
higher weighted average cost of gas.
•
Finance costs swelled up by 87% YoY in 9MFY23 to PKR 66.8bn owed to higher interest
rates.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 39% during 9MFY23 vis-à-vis 33% in 9MFY22.
1
AHL Alert: OGMC

Title: AHL Alert: Power Cement Limited - Company recorded PAT of PKR 348mn in 9MFY25
Created At: 2025-04-25T11:56:25.753Z
Tickers: POWER
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 680b7869238b86d3f8ea6012
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/680b7869238b86d3f8ea6012.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
Rao Aamir Ali
D: +92 21 32461106
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 242
amir.rao@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 25-Apr-2025 
Cement: Power Cement Limited
Result Review: Company recorded PAT of PKR 348mn in 9MFY25

Power Cement Ltd (POWER) announced its financial results for 9MFY25, posting
a profit of PKR 348mn (EPS: PKR 0.07), compared to loss of PKR 1,187mn (LPS:
PKR 1.41).

During 3QFY25, the company recorded earnings of PKR 316mn (EPS: PKR 0.22),
compared to loss of PKR 717mn (LPS: PKR 0.77).
•
In 9MFY25, the company reported sales of PKR 21.0bn, reflecting a 16% YoY
decline, primarily driven by a 20% YoY drop in dispatches to 1.7mn tons.
•
In 3QFY25, revenue declined to PKR 7.2bn, down 11% YoY and 18% QoQ. The
decrease in revenue was mainly due to a 20% YoY and 21% QoQ reduction in
dispatches. A significant drop in export volumes, down 29% YoY and 37% QoQ,
was the primary contributor to the overall decline in dispatches, largely due to
unfavorable export pricing.
•
Gross margins improved to 27.6% in 9MFY25, up from 22.0% in the same period
last year, mainly supported by higher retention prices.
•
In 3QFY25, gross margins rose by 985bps YoY to 28.6%, benefiting from the
same favorable pricing environment.
•
Selling and distribution expenses fell by 15% YoY in 9MFY25, totaling PKR
1,993mn, and declined further by 24% YoY in 3QFY25.
•
Finance costs dropped by 35% YoY in 9MFY25 to PKR 2,513mn, while in
3QFY25, they decreased by 54% YoY. This decline was primarily due to lower
interest rates.

## REPORT 50
Title: AHL Alert: ABL - Result Review: Highest-ever quarterly PAT recorded in 2Q
Created At: 2024-08-21T07:55:19.866Z
Tickers: ABL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 66c59d67311a3f7dccc0f1cc
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66c59d67311a3f7dccc0f1cc.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
21 August 2024
2QCY24: Highest-ever quarterly PAT recorded
ABL announced earnings today for 2QCY24 at PKR 12.5bn (EPS: PKR 10.9), depicting 
an uptick of 25% YoY | 7% QoQ. For 1HCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 24.1bn, 
marking a 37% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled by a jump in 
total income and provisioning reversals. The bank also announced a cash dividend of 
PKR 4/share in 2QCY24 along with the result taking total payout for 1HCY24 to PKR 
8/share.
Result Highlights 
•
The bank’s Net Interest Income (NII) amounted to PKR 29.4bn for 2QCY24, marking a 
YoY decline of 3%. However, on a QoQ basis, NII showed a slight increase of 1%. The 
bank faced a rise in interest expenses, which increased by 6% YoY and 5% QoQ. On 
the other hand, interest income grew by 3% YoY and 4% QoQ.
•
The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 59% YoY during 2QCY24, bringing 
the total for 1HCY24 to PKR 14.2bn, a 21% YoY increase. This increase is largely 
driven by a gain of PKR 838mn from the sale of securities, up from PKR 211mn in the 
same period last year. Additionally, fee and commission income surged by 33% YoY to 
PKR 7.4bn in 1HCY24. However, foreign exchange income saw a 7% YoY decline, 
amounting to PKR 4.1bn during 1HCY24.
•
A significant provisioning reversal was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 
2.9bn, this took the provisioning reversal for the 1HCY24 to PKR 3bn compared to a 
provisioning charge during 1HCY23 of PKR 2.7bn. 
•
The bank’s OPEX increased by 18% YoY in 1HCY24 clocking-in at PKR 28.9bn 
(2QCY24: PKR 15.3bn, +19% YoY | +12% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 
41.5% in 2QCY24 against 36.7% in same period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 49.3% during 2QCY24 compared to 54.6% SPLY.
REP-300
Allied Bank Limited
Result Review: Highest-ever quarterly PAT recorded in 2Q

## REPORT 51
Title: AHL Alert: HCAR - Result Review: MY25 EPS clocked-in at PKR 18.97
Created At: 2025-05-22T08:37:33.528Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 682ee24db58a5ab2c004e421
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/682ee24db58a5ab2c004e421.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
•
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for
MY25 today, posting a PAT of PKR 2,709mn (EPS: PKR 18.97), representing YoY
increase of 16%.
•
During 4QMY25 the profitability of the company was recorded at PKR 1,682mn
(EPS: PKR 11.78) compared to a profit of PKR 1,370mn (EPS: PKR 9.60) in
4QMY24, up by 23% YoY.
•
Alongside the result, the company announced an final cash dividend of PKR
8.0/share in 4QMY25 taking the payout to PKR 8.0/share in MY25.
•
Net sales during MY25 stood at PKR 78,066mn, reflecting a 42% YoY increase from
PKR 55,071mn in SPLY. On a quarterly basis, revenue rose by 11% YoY to PKR
27,653mn compared to PKR 24,918mn in SPLY primarily driven by a 13% YoY
increase in volumes to 5,682 units (Civic & City: 5,056 units; BR-V & HR-V: 626
units).
•
Gross margins stood at 8.5% in MY25 compared to 8.2% in SPLY, supported by
stable PKR parity and consistent steel prices.
•
Other income surged by 125% YoY to PKR 370mn in 4QMY25, mainly due to short-
term investments of PKR 4,942mn, which contributed to a 4x YoY increase in cash
and cash equivalents.
•
Finance cost declined by 43% YoY to PKR 346mn in 4QMY25 from PKR 612mn in
SPLY, mainly as a result of lower borrowings and decline in interest rates.
•
The company recorded a tax reversal of PKR 214mn in 4QMY25 compared to PKR
618mn in SPLY. The effective taxation for MY25 stood at 17.3% versus 15.2% in
SPLY.

## REPORT 52
Title: AHL Alert: ILP - Result Review: EPS arrived at PKR 3.84/share in FY25
Created At: 2025-09-10T11:41:48.897Z
Tickers: ILP
Sectors: 0829
Item ID: 68c163fcf495f8900e4d195f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68c163fcf495f8900e4d195f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 10-Sep-2025 
Textile Composite: Interloop Limited
Result Review: EPS arrived at PKR 3.84/share in FY25
FY25: EPS at PKR 3.84, DPS of PKR 1/share announced
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced its financial results for FY25, wherein the company
reported a sharp 66% YoY decline in Profit after Tax (PAT) to PKR 5,377mn versus PKR
15,771mn in FY24. This translates into an EPS of PKR 3.84, compared to PKR 11.25 in the
previous year. The earnings contraction is primarily attributed to deteriorating margins,
absence of one-off surplus on acquisition of subsidiaries, and a significantly higher effective
tax rate.
Result Highlights

During FY25, topline clocked in at PKR 173,382mn, up 11% YoY. Whereas, on a
quarterly basis, net sales during 4QFY25 arrived at PKR 47,974mn, depicting a jump of
16% YoY. This increment in overall sales was mainly driven by higher export volumes
and enhanced production capacities at the Hosiery and Apparel plants..

In FY25, gross margins declined to 20.3%, compared to 27.9% in SPLY, primarily driven
ramping up of apparel plant and higher energy tariff.

Distribution expenses during FY25 augmented by 19% YoY, which can be attributed to
higher shipping costs linked to improved sales of the Apparel and Hosiery segment.

Finance costs depicted an decline of 6% YoY to PKR 9,533mn in FY25 from PKR
10,125mn in SPLY due to lower interest rates.

The company booked effective taxation at 38.8% in FY25 vis-à-vis 11.4% in FY24.

In Jun’25, ILP’s cash position stood at PKR 858mn against PKR 870mn in Jun’24.

Along with results, company announced a dividend of PKR 1.0 per share for FY25.

## REPORT 53
Title: AHL Alert - ILP: Result Review: 1QFY24 EPS @ PKR 4.31
Created At: 2023-10-30T18:37:41Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 6540b2b36156e406d25dea2d

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability climbed by 22% YoY
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced the result for 1QFY24 today, where by the company 
posted a Profit after Tax of PKR 6,042mn (EPS: PKR 4.31) compared to PKR 4,961mn 
(EPS: PKR 3.54) in 1QFY23. 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales surged by 26% YoY to PKR 38,500mn during 1QFY24 because of i) higher 
hosiery segment pricing, 2) volumetric sales increase, and 3) currency depreciation of 
23% YoY.
•
During 1QFY24, gross margins remained stable at 33.1% as compared to 33.2% in 
1QFY23. 
•
The distribution cost in 1QFY24 jumped up by 20% YoY to PKR 1,372mn against PKR 
1,416mn in 1QFY23 due to inflationary pressure.
•
Finance costs jumped by 81% YoY to PKR 2,206mn in 1QFY24 due to higher short 
term borrowing during the period.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 7.8% in 1QFY24 vis-à-vis 6.5% in 1QFY23.

Title: AHL Alert: LCI - 1QFY26 EPS @ PKR 4.66
Created At: 2025-10-23T12:03:02.165Z
Tickers: LCI
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 68fa1976d5d1fae29b016906
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 23-Oct-2025 
Chemical: Lucky Core Industries Limited
Result Review: 1QFY26 EPS @ PKR 4.66
REP-300
Earnings arrived at PKR 4.66/share in 1QFY26
Lucky Core Industries Limited announced its financial results for 1QFY26 today, reporting a
profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 2.15bn (EPS: PKR 4.66), compared to PKR 2.6bn (EPS: PKR
5.68) in the same period last year. This 18%YoY decline in profitability is primarily
attributable to weaker operating performance in the Soda Ash, Polyester, and Chemicals &
Agri Sciences segments. However, the Pharmaceuticals and Animal Health segments
contributed positively to the bottom line, further supported by a reduction in finance costs.
Result Highlights
•
During 1QFY26, net sales decreased by 7% YoY to PKR 28.6bn, while on QoQ basis,
sales witnessed a slight upside of 3%.
•
Gross margins declined by 60bps year-on-year to 21.5% in 1QFY26, primarily due to
margin compression in the Soda Ash and Polyester segments, driven by cheaper
imports and subdued demand.
•
Selling and distribution expenses fell by 14% YoY, mainly reflecting lower offtake in the
Soda Ash and Polyester businesses.
•
During 1QFY26, LCI booked an exchange loss of PKR 6mn compared to exchange gain
of PKR 5.6mn in SPLY amid a recovery in Pak Rupee against the greenback during the
period.
•
The other income of the company declined by 35% YoY to PKR 539mn during 1QFY26,
which is attributable to lower interest rates.
•
Finance costs of the company declined by 14% YoY to PKR 544mn during 1QFY26
owed to lower interest rates.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 40.2% in 1QFY26 vis-à-vis 37.9% in
1QCY25.
•
As of Sep’25, company recorded cash and short term investments of PKR 20.4bn.

## REPORT 55
Title: AHL Alert: EFERT - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 7.06 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-07-31T05:07:15.634Z
Tickers: EFERT
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 66a9c6836a3cb5cde934aa28
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a9c6836a3cb5cde934aa28.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
31 July 2024
2QCY24: Bottom-line witnessed a growth of 57% YoY
Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) today unveiled the financial result for 1HCY24 posting a 
consolidated profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 9,426mn (EPS: PKR 7.06) against PKR 5,464mn 
(EPS: PKR 4.09) in 1HCY23, up by 73% YoY. Whereas, consolidated earnings in 2QCY24 
clocked in at PKR 1,666mn (EPS: PKR 1.25), augmenting by 57% YoY. Alongside the result, 
the EFERT announced a cash dividend of PKR 3.00/share (PKR 11.00/share in 1HCY24). 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales in 1HCY24 clocked in at PKR 113,204mn, up by 37% YoY which is on account of 
62% and 19% YoY hike in urea and DAP price tagged with 54% YoY jump in DAP offtake. 
On a quarterly basis, the topline during 2QCY24 witnessed an uptick of 3% YoY, settling at 
PKR 39,421mn, which is due to 59% and 15% YoY higher urea and DAP prices, respectively 
coupled with 16% YoY jump in DAP sales. Meanwhile, urea offtake contracted by 36% YoY 
due to 58 days maintenance at EnVen plant.
•
Gross margins in 1HCY24 arrived at 21.49% vis-à-vis 26.95% in 1HCY23. Whereas, gross 
margins in 2QCY24 arrived at 18.10% (down by 1,167bps YoY) owed to lower urea offtake.
•
Other income swelled up by 75% YoY settling at PKR 2,032mn in 1HCY24 given higher 
income from cash and cash balances. Meanwhile, in 2QCY24, the other income came out to 
be PKR 745mn, up by 67% YoY due to the aforementioned reason. 
•
Finance cost climbed up by 22% YoY, clocking in at PKR 1,386mn during 1HCY24 owed to 
higher interest rates. Whereas, the finance cost in 2QCY24 swelled up by 74% YoY, 
reaching PKR 1,225mn.    
•
The company booked effective taxation at 35% in 2QCY24 vis-à-vis 85% in 2QCY23. To 
recall, during 2QCY23 an additional 6% super tax was levied on the profit before tax of CY22 
and 1HCY23, respectively.
REP-300
Engro Fertilizers Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 7.06 in 1HCY24

## REPORT 56
Title: AHL Alert: POL - Result Review: Earnings clocked in at 85.19/share in FY25
Created At: 2025-08-11T09:30:54.615Z
Tickers: AHL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 6899b84e85f4d8ff580639a9
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6899b84e85f4d8ff580639a9.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
FY25: Profitability arrived at PKR 85.2/share; DPS at PKR 50.0/share.
Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after tax
of PKR 24,182mn (EPS: PKR 85.19) during FY25, depicting a plunge of 38% YoY amid
higher exploration expense associated with a dry well during the period and absence the
reversal of the provision for depletion allowance during the quarter. On a quarterly basis,
earnings in 4QFY25 arrived at PKR 7,428mn (EPS: PKR 26.17), down 19% YoY. In addition
to this, POL announced final cash dividend of PKR 25/share (PKR 50/share in FY25).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in FY25 witnessed a decline of 13% YoY, settling at PKR 57,117mn compared
to PKR 65,290mn during SPLY amid i) 12% YoY decline in average realized oil price, and
ii) 1% YoY appreciation of the Pak Rupee against the greenback. Topline in 4QFY25
arrived at PKR 12,282mn, down 18% YoY on the back of i) 12% YoY fall in average
realized oil prices, and ii) 6% and 21% YoY reduction in oil and gas production,
respectively.
•
The exploration costs surged by 7x YoY in FY25, arriving at PKR 11,180mn, owed to
higher cost of dry well Balkassar Deep 1A incurred in 1QFY25. Meanwhile, the
exploration costs during 4QFY25 reached PKR 1,407mn, up 9x YoY due to higher
geological and geophysical costs during the period.
•
The other income registered a decrease of 12% YoY, settling at PKR 14,529mn during
FY25 given lower income from cash and cash equivalents due to decline in interest rates.
Whereas, other income during 4QFY25 arrived at PKR 3,310mn, plummeting by 31% YoY
on account of aforementioned reason.
•
The company’s effective taxation arrived at 25.1% in 4QFY25 vis-à-vis 28.1% in 4QFY24.
•
In Jun’25, POL’s cash position stood at PKR 109bn compared to PKR 103bn in Mar’25.
REP-300
1
Muhammad Iqbal Jawaid
D: +92 21 32462589

## REPORT 57
Title: Result Review: SGF - CY23 EPS @ PKR 5.75, up 3.5x YoY
Created At: 2024-02-21T15:59:01.023Z
Tickers: SGF
Sectors: 0816
Item ID: 65d61dc5c954d41aba4dee6e
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65d61dc5c954d41aba4dee6e.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability for the 4Q recorded at PKR 390mn
Service Global Footwear Limited (SGF) announced its financial result for CY23 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 1,182mn (EPS: PKR 5.75), up by 3.5x YoY. During 4QCY23 the 
profitability of the company was recorded at PKR 390mn (EPS: PKR 1.91) compared to 
a loss of PKR 284mn (LPS: PKR 1.39) in 4QCY22. Alongside the result, the company 
announced a final cash dividend of PKR 2/share.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during CY23 clocked in at PKR 15,062mn, displaying a jump of 28% YoY in 
contrast to PKR 11,753mn in SPLY, amid PKR depreciation and an increase in 
exports. During 4QCY23, sales declined by 3% YoY | 31% QoQ, due to suppressed 
demand post-holiday season.
•
During CY23 gross margin arrived at 21.9%, up from 18.6% during SPLY mainly due 
to an increase in shoe prices which were more than enough to offset the impact of 
the increase in raw material costs. Gross margins also increased by 1050bps during 
4QCY23 to 21.3%, due to the aforementioned reason.
•
Administrative expense rose by 40% YoY settling at 198mn during 4QCY23, 
attributed to increase in employees' wages and salaries.
•
Other income declined by 9% YoY to clock in at PKR 190mn in 4QCY23, on the back 
of lower interest earned on diminishing long-term loan to the parent company.
•
The share of profit from associates, amounting to PKR 474mn, represents the stake-
adjusted (18.9%) profit after tax of Service Long March (SLM) 
•
The effective tax rate was set at 6.4% during 4QCY23. With this, the effective tax for 
CY23 stood at 25.9% (CY22: 50.3%).
Recommendation 
•
The stock is currently trading at CY24E PE(x) of 5.9x along with 15.2% dividend 
yield. Currently, we have a “BUY” call on the stock with a Dec’24 TP of PKR 
76.4/share.

AHL Alert 
Oil & Gas Exploration Companies 
Oil and Gas Development Company Limited 
September 28, 2023 
4QFY23: Net profit up by PKR 15.11/share  
Oil & Gas Development Company Limited (OGDC) announced its financial result today, posting a 
highest ever profit after tax of PKR 224,619mn (EPS: PKR 52.23) during FY23 compared to PKR 
133,784mn (EPS: PKR 31.11) in FY22, showcasing a jump of 68% YoY. On a quarterly basis, 
earnings in 4QFY23 settled at PKR 64,979mn (EPS: PKR 15.11), augmenting by 3.0x YoY. Alongside 
the result, the company announced a cash dividend of PKR 2.75/share in 4QFY23 (PKR 8.55/share 
in FY23).  
 
Result Highlights 
 
• Topline in FY23 witnessed a growth by 23% YoY, arriving at PKR 413,594mn given PKR 
depreciation against USD by 28% YoY. Whereas, oil prices depicted a fall of 2% YoY. 
Meanwhile, during 4QFY23, net sales increased by 10% YoY, clocking in at PKR 104,446mn. 
The jump in revenue comes on account of weakening of the Pak Rupee against the greenback 
by 32% YoY. However, oil and gas production depicted a fall of 5% and 11% YoY, respectively 
in 3QFY23, while oil prices declined by 29% YoY. 
• The exploration cost settled at PKR 19,024mn in FY23, up by 22% YoY owed to ascend dry well 
expenses. Whereas, in 4QFY23, exploration costs arrived at PKR 8,129mn, climbing up by 38% 
YoY given higher cost of dry well (Chak 20-1) compared to dry well (Bewato) in SPLY. 
• Other income in FY23 showcased a hefty jump of 2.2x YoY, clocking-in at PKR 154,692mn owing 
to hefty exchange gain during the period. Whereas, the other income in 4QFY23 depicted hefty 
jump 5.2x YoY, reaching PKR 89,508mn. We await disclosure of detailed accounts in this regard.  
• The company booked effective taxation at 50% in 4QFY23 vis-à-vis 65% in 4QFY22. The 
taxation during the quarter includes an additional 6% super tax levied on the profit before tax of 
FY23.

## REPORT 59
Title: AHL Alert - ATRL Profitability arrives at PKR 33.5/share in 2QFY26
Created At: 2026-02-24T06:21:47.372Z
Tickers: ATRL
Sectors: 0825
Item ID: 699d437bd33a93d06b2f6470
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699d437bd33a93d06b2f6470.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 33.5/share; payout of PKR 2.5/sh declared
Attock Refinery Limited (ATRL) announced the financial result for 2QFY26, whereby the
company has posted a net profit of PKR 3,568mn (EPS: PKR 33.5) against PKR 3,563mn
(EPS: PKR 33.4) in 2QFY25, remaining stable on a YoY basis. The company announced a
payout of PKR 2.5/sh, lower than expectations.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales for 2QFY26 dropped 3% YoY to PKR 78bn, primarily due to lower volumes as
explained below and lower MS and FO ex-refinery prices with both down 0.2% and
5.1%, respectively.
•
Gross profit clocked in at PKR 4,041mn (gross margins of 5.2%). The YoY rise in gross
profit was driven by improving MS and HSD spreads, despite lower volumes and weak
local and export margins on higher FO offtake, mostly exported.
•
International MS and HSD spreads surged 143.5% YoY to USD 11.62/bbl and 61.0%
YoY to USD 20.69/bbl, respectively (Bloomberg). In contrast, HSFO prices declined
23% YoY to USD 333/ton and remained pressured by north–south freight costs. Ex-
refinery FO prices averaged PKR 108,000/ton, below benchmark oil prices, signaling
continued negative margins.
•
MS sales declined 14.1% YoY to 125k tons and HSD sales fell 3.2% YoY to 154k tons in
2QFY26, primarily due to forced crude production curtailments at certain fields amid
high system pressure on the SNGPL network. Meanwhile, FO volumes jumped 272.4%
YoY to 90k tons in Dec’25, largely driven by exports from ATRL.
•
Other income for 2QFY26 decline by 32% YoY, settling at PKR 2.4bn due to lower
interest rates.
•
On the balance sheet front, cash and short-term investments stand at PKR 92bn (PKR
860/share), with a book value of PKR 1,390/share.
•
ATRL offers attractive valuations, trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 7.2x and
4.4x, respectively.
REP-300
1

zayan.babar@arifhabibltd.com

AHL Alert 
Cement 
Kohat Cement Company Limited 
August 31, 2023 
 
 
Kohat Cement Company Limited (KOHC) announced its financial result for FY23 today, posting a profit 
after tax (PAT) of PKR 5,821mn (EPS: PKR 28.98) compared to PKR 5,024mn (EPS: PKR 25.01) in 
FY22, depicting a growth of 16% YoY. The profit after tax in 4QFY23 clocked in at PKR 458mn (EPS: 
PKR 2.28) vis-à-vis PKR 394mn (EPS: PKR 1.96) in SPLY, up by 16% YoY.  
    Result Highlights 
 
 Topline during FY23 settled at PKR 38,922mn, up by 18% YoY led by a nearly 40% hike in retention 
prices which offset the impact of a 16% decline in dispatches to 2,990k tons. In 4QFY23, total 
dispatches dropped by 17% YoY settling at 683k tons vs. 824k tons in SPLY. 
 Gross margin during FY23 arrived at 27% vs. 30% last year on the back of volumetric decline, higher 
electricity tariff and PKR depreciation which countered the impact of topline growth. On quarterly 
basis, margins fell slightly to 27.0% in contrast to 27.6% last year. 
 It is pertinent to note that in 4QFY23 finance cost grew by 45% YoY on the back of high interest 
rates, with this FY23 finance cost increased to 740mn (up by 38% YoY). 
 Other income escalated by 202% YoY to PKR 2,086mn in FY23 mainly due to higher return on short 
term investments. 
 The company booked effective taxation at 44% in FY23.

## REPORT 61
Title: AHL Alert - EFERT - 1HCY23 PAT up by 1% YoY
Created At: 2023-07-27T12:30:25Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 64d5de46d806004e2b350090
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=300&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
 
P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Fertilizer 
Engro Fertilizers Limited                                    
July 27, 2023 
Bottom-line settles at PKR 0.79/share in 2QCY23 
The management of Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) today disclosed the financial result for 1HCY23 
posting a consolidated profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 5,464mn (EPS: PKR 4.09) against PKR 5,413mn 
(EPS: PKR 4.05) in 1HCY22, uptick of 1% YoY. Whereas, consolidated earnings in 2QCY23 clocked in 
at PKR 1,060mn (EPS: PKR 0.79) compared to a loss of PKR 98mn (LPS: PKR 0.07) in SPLY. In addition 
to the result, the company announced a cash dividend of PKR 3.00/share (PKR 6.50/share in 1HCY23).  
Financial Result Highlights 
 
 Net sales in 1HCY23 clocked in at PKR 82,366mn, up by 10% YoY amid a surge in urea and DAP 
prices by 44% and 6% YoY, respectively. Meanwhile, urea and DAP offtake went down by 6% and 
27% YoY, respectively. On a quarterly basis, topline during 2QCY23 remained stable at PKR 
38,375mn owing to a jump in urea and DAP prices by 43% and 2% YoY, respectively, offsetting the 
12% and 2% YoY decline in urea and DAP sales, respectively. 
 Gross margins in 1HCY23 settled at 26.95% compared to 30.01% in SPLY on account of revision 
in prices of feed and fuel gas (PP12) and delay in passing on the impact via higher urea prices in 
1QCY23. Whereas, gross margins in 2QCY23 arrived at 29.77% (down by 74bps YoY) owed to i) 
lower urea offtake, and ii) higher repair and maintenance costs given the closure of the base plant 
for one month. 
 Other income depicted an increase of 6% YoY settling at PKR 1,164mn in 1HCY23 owed to higher 
income from cash and cash balances. However, in 2QCY23, the other income came out to be PKR 
447mn, showcasing a fall of 24% YoY owing to a decline in short-term investments.  
 Finance cost ascended by 8% YoY to PKR 1,138mn during 1HCY23 amid higher interest rates. 
Whereas, the finance cost in 2QCY23 swelled by 21% YoY, reaching PKR 702mn given the 
aforementioned reason.   
 
The company booked effective taxation at 85% in 2QCY23 vis-à-vis 101% in 2QCY22. The taxation 
during the quarter includes an additional 6% super tax levied on the profit before tax of CY22 and 
1HCY23, respectively coupled with deferred tax liability adjustment.

## REPORT 62
Title: AHL Alert: HUBC - Result Review: Company reported earnings of PKR 35.6/share in FY25
Created At: 2025-09-03T12:05:57.852Z
Tickers: HUBC
Sectors: 0824
Item ID: 68b82f25d3c226148cf3c7c9
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11900ffa16ff64800192
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68b82f25d3c226148cf3c7c9.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Research
D: +92 21 32461106
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 242
research@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 03-Sep-2025 
Power Generation: The Hub Power Company Limited
Result Review: Company reported earnings of PKR 35.6/share in FY25
4QFY25 earnings arrived at PKR 9.16/share; 4QFY25 DPS at PKR 10.00/share

The Hub Power Company Ltd (HUBC) has released its financial results for FY25,
posting a net profit of PKR 46.1bn (EPS: PKR 35.56/share), reflecting a 34% YoY
decline. This drop is primarily attributed to the termination of the PPA for the Hub
base plant.

In 4QFY25, profitability also decreased by 42% YoY to PKR 11.9 bn (EPS: PKR
9.16/share) due to the same reason. However, on a QoQ basis, earnings increased
by 8% mainly due to increase in revenue by 10% QoQ and higher Share of profit
from associates. Alongside the result, company announced a DPS of PKR
10.00/share which takes total FY25 DPS to PKR 15.00/share.
Result Highlights

Sales during FY25 declined by 36% YoY, again largely due to the Hub plant PPA
termination. In 4QFY25, sales dropped 47% YoY due to the same reason.

Other expenses rose sharply to PKR 3.85bn in FY25, a 57% increase compared to
the same period last year, mainly due to the provision of PKR 2.6bn related to a
trade debt write-off at the Narowal plant.

The company’s share of profit from associates fell 16% YoY to PKR 41.3bn in FY25,
mainly due to reduced earnings from Prime International, in our view. In 4QFY25,
this profit also declined by 25% YoY.

On a positive note, finance costs dropped by 43% YoY to PKR 15.2bn in FY25,
primarily due to a decline in interest rates. In 4QFY25, finance costs fell 54% YoY
and 4% QoQ, helped by lower interest rates.

HUBC reported an effective tax rate of 19.5% in FY25, compared to 16.3% in the
same period last year.

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Automobile Assembler 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited 
May 26, 2023 
 
  Lower volumetric sales and higher other expenses led to a decline in profitability 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for MY23 on 26th May’23 
whereby the company posted a Profit After Tax (PAT) of PKR 260mn (EPS: PKR 1.82), representing a 
YoY decline of 90%. The significant decline in profit was attributed to lower volumetric sales and higher 
finance cost, which were recorded up by 6.5x on YoY basis. During 4QMY23, the company posted a Loss 
after Tax (LAT) of PKR 824mn (LPS: PKR 5.77) vs a Profit after Tax of PKR 197mn (EPS: 1.38) in 
4QMY22.  
Result Highlights 
 
 
Net sales declined to PKR 95,087mn during MY23, down by 12% YoY, owing to lower volumetric 
sales (-38% YoY) and the impact of import restrictions imposed by the SBP, which was partially 
offset by higher car prices.  
 
Gross margins stood at 7.5% during MY23, as compared to 5.1% in SPLY, depicting an improvement 
of 240bps. This was attributed to augmented car prices coupled with lower commodity prices, 
especially steel. For similar reasons, margins jumped up over last year (MY22: 5.1%).  
 
Other income depicted a jump of 16% YoY during MY23, as higher interest was earned on the cash 
and short-term investments.  
 
Other expenses were recorded at PKR 4.9bn, up by 5x on YoY basis, mainly due to company 
recording a exchange loss amid PKR depreciation. 
 
The company booked effective taxation at 86.9% during MY23. 
Result Highlights 
Currently, we have a ‘Hold’ stance on the stock with Dec’23 target price of PKR 109/share. 
 
Result Review: MY23 EPS clocked-in at @ PKR 1.82

## REPORT 64
Title: AHL Alert: SAZEW - 1QFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 73.07
Created At: 2025-10-15T12:10:05.437Z
Tickers: SAZEW
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 68ef8f1dd5d1fae29bce4b2a
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68ef8f1dd5d1fae29bce4b2a.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
•
Sazgar Engineering Works Limited (SAZEW) announced its financial result for
1QFY26 today, posting a PAT of PKR 4,417mn (EPS: PKR 73.07) compared to PKR
4,217mn (EPS: PKR 69.77) in SPLY, up by 5% YoY.
•
Alongside the result, the company announced dividend of PKR 15.00/share in 1QFY26
compared to the dividend of PKR 10.0/share in SPLY. In 4QFY25, the company
announced a final dividend of PKR 20.0/share.
•
Net sales stood at PKR 33,825mn in 1QFY26, depicting a 28% YoY increase, primarily
driven by higher Haval volumes, which rose by 37% YoY to 3,557 units. The increase in
Haval sales is attributed to the launch of the Haval PHEV in Aug’25. Additionally, three-
wheeler sales increased by 13% YoY to 5,346 units during the quarter.
•
The company’s distribution expenses rose by 30% YoY to PKR 964mn, primarily driven by
higher distribution and marketing costs associated with the launch of the new Haval PHEV.
•
In 1QFY26, gross margins stood at 25.2%, compared to 28.9% in the same period last year
and 25.1% in 4QFY25. The YoY decline in margins is mainly attributable to the absorption
of the carbon levy imposed in Budget FY26 and the absence of concessionary duties on the
newly launched Haval PHEV.
•
Other income increased by 27% YoY to PKR 514mn, mainly due to increase in cash and
bank balances which increased by 1.9x. YoY to PKR 31,336mn.
•
The company recorded an effective taxation of 39.0% in 1QFY26 vis-à-vis 36.3% in the
same period last year. The company recorded an effective taxation of 38.9% in 4QFY25.
•
We maintain our BUY stance on SAZEW, which is currently trading at an FY26 P/E multiple
of 5.9x and 6.3x.

## REPORT 65
Title: AHL Alert: FFC - Result Review: EPS clocked in at PKR 20.49 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-07-30T11:04:43.076Z
Tickers: FFC
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 66a8c8cb0ef1688f8bd844eb
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a8c8cb0ef1688f8bd844eb.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 July 2024
All time high quarterly profit posted during the 2QCY24
Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited (FFC) disclosed its financial result today where the
company posted net profit of PKR 26,071mn (EPS: PKR 20.49) during 1HCY24 against
PKR 13,079mn (EPS: PKR 10.28) in 1HCY23, up by 99% YoY. On the other hand, the
profitability in 2QCY24 augmented by 2.9x YoY to PKR 15,549mn (EPS: PKR 12.22). In
addition to this, FFC announced a cash dividend of PKR 10.00/share in 2QCY24 (PKR
15.50/share in 1HCY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales climbed up by 61% YoY during 1HCY24, settling at PKR 115,574mn on
account of a jump in urea and DAP offtake by 3% and 26% YoY, respectively tagged with
62% and 19% YoY higher urea and DAP prices. On a quarterly basis, the revenue in
2QCY24 surged by 61% YoY amid an increase in DAP sales by 55% YoY coupled with
59% YoY higher urea prices. Whereas, urea sales remained stable during the quarter.
•
The gross margins during 1HCY24 arrived at 41.90% compared to 43.40% in SPLY on
account higher gas prices coupled with high cost of imported urea in 1QCY24. Whereas,
gross margins in 2QCY24 arrived at 54.46% (up by 762bps YoY) amid better urea and
DAP margins.
•
Financial charges increased by 9% YoY, clocking in at PKR 2,886mn in 1HCY24 amid
higher interest rates. Meanwhile, the finance charges went up by 16% YoY to PKR
1,380mn.
•
Other income ascended by 2.5x YoY, clocking in at PKR 15,770mn in 1HCY24, given i)
higher dividend income from associates, and subsidiaries such as AKBL and PMP, and ii)
increase in income from cash and cash balances.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 43% in 2QCY24 vis-à-vis 61% in 2QCY23.

## REPORT 66
Title: AHL Alert - FABL - 1QCY23 EPS @ PKR 2.18
Created At: 2023-04-28T14:05:02Z
Tickers: Not stated

AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
Faysal Bank Limited 
April 28, 2023 
FABL’s bottom line increased by 50% YoY 
FABL announced earnings today for 1QCY23 at PKR 3.3bn (EPS: PKR 2.18), depicting an uptick of 50% 
YoY, while a decline of 9% QoQ (4QCY22 EPS: PKR 2.38). The rise in YoY earnings is primarily due to 
a jump in total income. 
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net Profit Earned by the bank settled at PKR 13bn during 1QCY23, remaining stagnant on a QoQ 
basis while increasing by 82% YoY. The profit expense was up 115% YoY, and interest earned too 
showed an increase of 101% YoY during 1QCY23. This jump in net profit earned is primarily on the 
back of policy rate hikes, we view.  
• 
NFI depicted an increase of 19% YoY primarily due to healthy fee and commission during the quarter 
of PKR 2.1bn (+31% YoY). In addition, during 1QCY23, the foreign exchange posted a 2.3x YoY 
increase owed to PKR depreciation against the greenback. However, during 1QCY23, the bank 
booked a loss on securities of PKR 888mn against a loss of PKR 113mn during the same period last 
year. 
• 
During 1QCY23, the bank booked provisioning of PKR 1.4bn against a reversal of PKR 147mn 
recorded SPLY. 
• 
The bank’s OPEX rose 34% YoY to PKR 8bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 51% in 1QCY23 
compared to 54% in SPLY  
• 
Effective tax rate was set at 48.1% during 1QCY23 compared to 51.1% recorded in last quarter of 
CY22. 
.

## REPORT 67
Title: AHL Alert: MARI Earnings inclined by 15% YoY in 2QFY26
Created At: 2026-01-26T10:06:17.666Z
Tickers: MARI
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 69773c995848514eaf1cd26f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69773c995848514eaf1cd26f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY26: Bottomline arrived at PKR 10.66/share
Mari Energies Limited (MARI) announced its financial results today, reporting earnings of
PKR 12,800mn (EPS: PKR 10.66) for 2QFY26, reflecting a 15% YoY increase. Alongside
the results, the company announced an interim dividend, returning to its historic payout
level of 35%, with a cash dividend of PKR 8.30/share. Earnings rose due to a 6%
increase in oil production, a 20% decline in operating expenses, and a 50% drop in
exploration costs YoY.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 2QFY26 rose 8% YoY to PKR 44,770mn. Oil production increased 6%
YoY to 1,298 bopd, while gas production fell. Among MARI’s key gas fields, HRL
dropped 2.8% YoY due to ATA at FFC’s plant, limiting incremental volumes. Goru B
stayed stable, reflecting only two months’ data, while Shewa output surged 102% QoQ
to 53 mmcfd.
•
Royalty charges in 2QFY26 rose 33% YoY to PKR 10,648mn, mainly due to
incremental royalty on Mari D&P Lease wellhead revenue.
•
Exploration costs in 2QFY26 dropped 50% YoY to PKR 1,864mn and 16% QoQ,
driven by reduced prospecting expenditure.
•
Finance income stood at PKR 1,001mn in 2QFY26, declining by 56% YoY due to
lower interest rates.
•
The company recorded an effective tax rate of 35% in 2QFY26 (25% SPLY).
•
MARI’s cash position declined to PKR 60,811mn in Dec’25 (Jun’25: PKR 76,895mn),
while trade receivables increased up to PKR 88,765mn (Jun’25: PKR 86,581mn).
MARI is currently trading at a forward FY26/FY27 multiples of 15.8x/11.8x P/E and
3.2x/2.7x P/B, offering a dividend yield of 2.6%/3.4%.

1

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Automobile Assembler 
Pak Suzuki Motor Company Limited 
March 20, 2023 
 
 Bottom-line turns red 
  Pak Suzuki Motor Company Limited (PSMC) announced its financial result for 4QCY22 whereby the 
company posted a loss after tax (LAT) of PKR 3,830mn (LPS: PKR 46.5) against a profit after tax (PAT) 
of PKR 489mn (EPS: PKR 5.9) during 4QCY21, mainly due to an enormous jump (+13x YoY) in the 
finance cost along with huge tax charge (+15x YoY). This took the loss in CY22 to PKR 6,337mn (LPS: 
PKR 77) vs. a PAT of PKR 2,679mn (EPS: PKR 32.6) during CY21. 
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net sales jumped up by 37% YoY to PKR 60bn in 4QCY22, mainly due to higher car prices which 
offset the impact of slowdown in offtake. In addition, the PSMC plant was shut down in the month 
of Oct’22 due to lower availability of the raw material on the back of SBP’s restrictive measures. 
• 
Gross margins arrived at 9.85% during 4QCY22 vis-à-vis 3.55% in SPLY, given strong topline 
growth as increase in car prices managed to counter the impact of hike in other costs and 
volumetric decline.  
• 
Other income declined by 37% YoY compared to SPLY, majorly due to declining cash and cash 
equivalents.  
• 
During 4QCY22, finance cost increased by 13x YoY to PKR 4.9bn, whereby the company has 
potentially recognized an exchange loss of PKR 3.55bn on outstanding foreign liabilities of USD 
184mn (up to 31st Dec’22). In addition, PSMC booked markup paid on account of late delivery of 
vehicles. 
• 
Company charged a massive tax of PKR 3.4bn in CY22; we await guidance from the management 
for this massive increase.  
• 
In another notice today to the PSX, PSMC has mentioned that it has an outstanding foreign 
liability of USD 218mn as of now, and the company has unrealized loss of ~PKR 9bn, which may 
possibility hurt the profitability, and hence the equity of the company during CY23. 
 
 
Result Review: 4QCY22 LPS @ PKR 46.5

## REPORT 69
Title: AHL Alert - AIRLINK 9MFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 9.23/share
Created At: 2026-04-29T12:55:41.835Z
Tickers: AIRLINK
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 69f1ffcd9730304360d12a87
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1ffcd9730304360d12a87.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 29-Apr-2026 
Air Link Communication Limited
Result Review: 9MFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 9.23/share
REP-300
3QFY26 earnings arrived at PKR 1.50/share, up by 11% YoY
Air Link Communication Limited (AIRLINK) announced the financial result for
9MFY26 today, whereby the company has posted a PAT of PKR 3,647mn (EPS:
PKR 9.23) compared to profit of PKR 2,854m (EPS: PKR 7.22) in 9MFY25. On a
quarterly basis, the company recorded PAT of PKR 595mn (EPS:PKR 1.5) in
3QFY26 up by 11% YoY.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales for 9MFY26 declined by 20% YoY to PKR 68,370mn. On a quarterly
basis, the topline in 3QFY26 contracted by 31% YoY to PKR 19,599mn. The
decline in revenue was primarily attributable to a contraction in volumetric sales
of
Xiaomi
from
Select
Technologies,
which
weighed
on
overall
sales
performance during the period.
•
Gross profit margin rose to 13.6% in 9MFY26 vs 9.8% in 9MFY25. On a
quarterly basis, the gross margins stood at 10.4% in 3QFY26 compared to
10.4% in SPLY and 16% in 2QFY26. The QoQ decline is due to decline in
volumes.
•
The other income arrived at PKR 207mn in 3QFY26 compared to other income
of PKR 94mn in SPLY due to increase in cash and short term investments.
•
The finance costs decreased by 77% YoY to PKR 322m in 3QFY26 due to
decline in long term loan to PKR 639mn compared to PKR 959mn in SPLY.
•
The company reported an effective tax rate of 44.1% in 3QFY26 compared to
39.1% in SPLY.
•
We have a BUY stance on AIRLINK, the stock is trading at FY26 and FY27
multiple of 9.7 and 7.2x.
Menka Kirpalani

menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
National Bank of Pakistan 
February 28, 2023 
Jump in income uplifts profitability 
NBP announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY22 at PKR 11.6bn (EPS: PKR 5.46), depicting an 
increase of 186% YoY | 66% QoQ (4QCY21 EPS: PKR 1.91). This jump in earnings during 4QCY22 
was mainly led by higher total income. Moreover, provisioning of the bank in the out-going quarter 
was up significantly QoQ.  
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 36.4bn during 4QCY22, increasing 44% YoY and 
32% QoQ. With this, total NII for CY22 went up to PKR 117bn, marking a 20% YoY jump. While 
interest expense of the bank in 4QCY22 was up 236% YoY, interest income too remained high 
162% YoY depicting asset-repricing impact in the out-going quarter. 
• 
NFI depicted an increase of 15% YoY to PKR 11.9bn during 4QCY22 taking CY22’s total to PKR 
39.3bn (+1% YoY). This jump in CY22 is primarily due to a FX gains of PKR 8.2bn (+21% YoY) 
on the back of PKR depreciation. In addition, fee and commission income posted a 17% YoY 
jump, clocking-in at PKR 22.6bn during CY22. Further, bank booked a PKR 1.1bn gain on security 
during CY22 compared to a gain of PKR 6.2bn SPLY. 
• 
Provisioning for the bank clocked-in at PKR 13.1bn during CY22 (4QCY22: PKR 9.5bn), depicting 
a jump of 12% on YoY basis. 
• 
The bank’s OPEX increased 30% YoY in CY22 clocking-in at PKR 79.6bn (4QCY22: PKR 23.8bn, 
+79% YoY | 23% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 49.4% in 4QCY22 against 37.4% same 
period last year.  
• 
Effective tax rate was set at 21.6% during 4QCY22 compared to 37.4% SPLY.

## REPORT 71
Title: AHL Alert: LUCK - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 46.79
Created At: 2024-01-26T07:42:46.979Z
Tickers: LUCK
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 65b36276ebc5f774fdc4c3e0
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
Lucky Cement Limited
Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 46.79
AHL Alert: Cements
26-Jan-24
REP-300
2QFY24: Earnings settled at PKR 23.14/share
Lucky Cement Limited (LUCK) announced its financial result for 1HFY24 today, posting an 
unconsolidated earnings of PKR 13,710mn (EPS: PKR 46.79), showcasing a hefty jump of 
92% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the unconsolidated net profit arrived at PKR 6,781mn 
(EPS: PKR 23.14) compared to PKR 3,276mn (EPS: PKR 10.13) in SPLY, depicting a 
surge of 107% YoY. That said, consolidated earnings ascended by 69% YoY, settling at 
PKR 17,633mn (EPS: PKR 60.18) in 2QFY24 vs PKR 10,464mn (EPS: PKR 32.36) in 
SPLY. The increase in consolidated earnings is attributable to higher earnings from 
cement operations, LEPCL, and LCI.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 2QFY24 clocked-in at PKR 30,499mn, displaying a jump of 19% YoY in 
contrast to PKR 25,590mn in SPLY, mainly due to rise in volumetric sales in tandem 
with better retention prices. During 2QFY24 total dispatches rose by 13% YoY reaching 
2.25mn vs 1.99mn in SPLY, mainly due to rise in exports to 0.6mn, up by 95% YoY.
•
Gross margins for 2QFY24 settled at 36% vis-a-vis 25% as compared to the same 
period last year, amid rise in cement prices coupled with a fall in coal prices.
•
Finance costs climbed by 18% YoY to clock in at PKR 424mn in 2QFY24, on the back of 
elevated interest rates.
•
Other income saw an uptick of 263% YoY clocking in at PKR 3,045mn on the back of 
PKR 1,400mn dividends received from LMC.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 33% in 2QFY24.

## REPORT 72
Title: AHL Alert: EPCL - Result Review: 3QCY24 LPS @ PKR 0.77
Created At: 2024-10-22T08:44:41.42Z
Tickers: EPCL
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 671765f98f84bab98c5c2dad
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671765f98f84bab98c5c2dad.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
22 October 2024
Bottom-line depicted a loss of PKR 2,288mn during 9MCY24
Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) announced its 9MCY24 financial result today 
where the company posted a loss of PKR 2,288mn (LPS: PKR 2.52) compared to profit of 
PKR 5,386mn (EPS: PKR 5.93) in SPLY. Whereas, loss in 3QCY24 clocked in at PKR 
698mn (LPS: PKR 0.77).
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales in 9MCY24 clocked in at PKR 54.5bn, down by 12% YoY. Moreover, topline 
declined by 20% YoY to PKR 20.1bn during 3QCY24. This decline is attributable to 
decline in PVC prices which went down by 3% YoY in USD terms.
•
The gross profit margin in 9MCY24 arrived at 6.6% compared to 25.6% during SPLY 
due to higher gas prices and subdued PVC margins. The gross margins during the 
quarter declined to 5.5%, down by 20pps YoY due to aforementioned reasons
•
The finance cost climbed up by 59% YoY, arriving at PKR 1,964mn during 3QCY24 
amid jump in short-term borrowings. On a sequential basis, the finance cost declined 
by 8% YoY, which is attributable to a decline in interest rates. 
•
Other income plummeted by 48% YoY to PKR 194mn during 3QCY24 due to lower 
short-term investments.
•
The company booked a tax reversal of PKR 749mn in 3QCY24 compared to taxation 
of PKR 1,769mn in 3QCY23.
Outlook
•
PVC margins are expected to remain constrained due to the global economic 
slowdown and sluggish construction activity. This trend poses a downside risk to 
EPCL’s profitability, as it faces headwinds from lower product pricing due weaker 
demand fundamentals.

## REPORT 73
Title: AHL Alert: NCPL - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 6.60
Created At: 2024-02-26T10:33:03.025Z
Tickers: NCPL
Sectors: 0824
Item ID: 65dc68dfc954d41aba01b190
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
NCPL: Profitability in 1HFY24 rose by 32% YoY
Nishat Chunian Power Limited (NCPL) announced its 1HFY24 financial result today
where the company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 2,425mn (EPS: PKR 6.60),
up by 32% YoY compared to PKR 1,838mn (EPS: PKR 5.00) in the SPLY. In 2QFY24
the company posted earnings of PKR 1,040mn (EPS: PKR 2.83) vis-à-vis PKR 878mn
(EPS: PKR 2.39) in corresponding period, an increase of 18% YoY.
Result Highlights
•
In 1HFY24 topline fell by 17% YoY arriving at PKR 8,723mn as compared to PKR
10,567mn, on the back of lower dispatches, amid overall drop in electricity demand
for FO-based power generation due to the induction of relatively cheaper power
plants. During 2QFY24, net sales witnessed a decrease of 3% YoY to clock in at PKR
1,917mn as compared to PKR 1,980mn, due to aforementioned reason.
•
During 1HFY24 gross margins settled at 30% as compared to 23% in SPLY, whereas
in 2QFY24, the gross margins of the company clocked in at 58% vis-à-vis 60% in
2QFY23.
•
In 2QFY24 finance cost decreased by 71% YoY, despite higher interest rates, to
arrive at PKR 78mn during the period compared to the same period last year. The
decline is mainly attributable to a reduction in short-term borrowings to PKR 3,889mn
as of Sep’23. This took 1HFY24 finance cost to settle at PKR 219mn, a decline of
53% YoY.
Recommendation
•
We have a BUY call on the script with a target price of PKR 29.6/share.
1

## REPORT 74
Title: AHL Alert: POL - Result Review: EPS settled at PKR 35.73 in 1HFY25
Created At: 2025-01-27T10:44:13.563Z
Tickers: POL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 6797637d6ae6aa2b53028f81
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6797637d6ae6aa2b53028f81.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
27 January 2025
Profitability decreased by 4% YoY to PKR 26.68/share in 2QFY25
Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after
tax of PKR 10,142mn (EPS: PKR 35.73) during 1HFY25, depicting a plunge of 42% YoY
amid higher exploration expense associated with a dry well during the period. On a
quarterly basis, net profit during 2QFY25 arrived at PKR 7,573mn (EPS: PKR 26.68),
down 4% YoY. In addition to the result, the company announced an interim cash dividend
of PKR 25.00/share in 2QFY25 (PKR 25.00/share in 2QFY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 1HFY25 witnessed a decline of 11% YoY, settling at PKR 30,283mn
compared to PKR 34,046mn during SPLY amid i) decline in oil and gas production by
5% and 9% YoY, respectively, ii) 14% YoY decline in average realized oil prices, and
iii) 3.3% YoY appreciation of the Pak Rupee against the greenback. Topline in 2QFY25
arrived at PKR 14,832mn, down 15% YoY on the back of i) 17% YoY fall in average
realized oil prices and ii) 4% and 14% YoY reduction in oil and gas production,
respectively.
•
The exploration costs surged by 7x YoY in 1HFY25, arriving at PKR 8,361mn, owed to
higher cost of dry well Balkassar Deep 1A incurred in 1QFY25. Meanwhile, the
exploration costs during 2QFY25 reached PKR 626mn, up 51% YoY due to higher
geological and geophysical costs during the period.
•
The other income registered a growth of 8% YoY, settling at PKR 8,372mn during
1HFY25 given higher income from cash and cash equivalents due to improvement in
cash position. Whereas, other income during 2QFY25 arrived at PKR 4,626mn,
depicting a surge of 61% YoY on account of aforementioned reason.
•
The company’s effective taxation arrived at 37% in 2QFY25 vis-à-vis 39% in 2QFY24.
REP-300
Pakistan Oilfields Limited

## REPORT 75
Title: AHL Alert: Result Review: MTL posted highest ever net sales of PKR 91.5bn in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-09T10:43:42.677Z
Tickers: MTL
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 66ded15e0cbf3cea06302320
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
9 September 2024
4QFY24: Profitability augmented by 64% YoY to PKR 10.72/share
Millat Tractors Limited (MTL) announced its financial result for FY24 today, posting a PAT of
PKR 9,917mn (EPS: PKR 51.7) compared to PKR 3,378mn (EPS: PKR 17.61) in SPLY, up
by 2.9x YoY. During 4QFY24 earnings clocked in at PKR 2,057mn (EPS: PKR 10.72),
showcasing a significant jump of 64% YoY. No final cash dividend was announced, with the
full-year payout remaining at PKR 25.0/share.
Result Highlights
•
Topline during FY24 surged by 107% YoY clocking at PKR 91,509mn driven by a 62%
YoY rise in volumetric sales to 30.2k units. During 4QFY24 net sales augmented by 58%
YoY to settle at PKR 21,928mn in contrast to PKR 13,893mn in SPLY.
•
Gross margins for FY24 arrived at 22.6% vis-à-vis 20.0% in SPLY primarily driven by an
increase in tractor prices, effectively offsetting high production costs. During 4QFY24,
gross margins were recorded at 20.7% compared to 25.4% recorded in SPLY.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in FY24 climbed up by 77% YoY to clock in at PKR
1,898mn amid inflationary pressure and increase in volumetric sales, we view. In 4QFY24,
selling and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 311mn, up by 42% YoY.
•
Other income surged by 2.2x YoY in FY24 to clock in at PKR 1,036mn, due to higher
income from cash and cash balances. However, in 4QFY24, the other income arrived at
PKR 40mn, depicting a decline of 61% YoY.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 42.6% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 50.6% in 4QFY23.
Other information
•
The merger of Millat Tractors Limited with Millat Equipment Limited is pending approval
from the Lahore High Court, during which MTL cannot pay further dividends. As a result,
for FY24, the total cash dividend is limited to the already paid interim DPS of PKR 25.
REP-300
Millat Tractors Limited

## REPORT 76
Title: AHL Alert: FFBL - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 8.18 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-07-25T10:09:44.846Z
Tickers: FFBL
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 66a224689332006adf623b20
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a224689332006adf623b20.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 July 2024
Profitability augmented by 13x YoY to PKR 4.84/share in 2QCY24
Fauji Fertilizer Bin Qasim Limited announced its 1HCY24 financial result today 
where the company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 10,560mn (EPS: PKR 
8.81) compared to a loss of PKR 4,950mn (LPS: PKR 3.83) in SPLY. On a quarterly 
basis, the profitability witnessed a surge of 13x YoY, arriving at PKR 6,254mn (EPS: 
PKR 4.84) in 2QCY24. 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales swelled up by 45% YoY to PKR 96,856mn in 1HCY24 on account of i) 
jump in urea and DAP offtake by 19% YoY, each, and ii) increase in urea and 
DAP prices by 62% and 19% YoY, respectively. During 2QCY24, the topline 
surged by 43% YoY given 63% and 7% growth in urea and DAP sales tagged 
with 59% and 15% YoY increase in urea and DAP prices, respectively. 
•
Gross margins stood at 20.76% during 1HCY24 compared to 9.91% in SPLY 
owing to a decline in phosphoric acid price and higher DAP price and offtake. 
Similarly, the gross margins during 2QCY24 went up by 982 bps, settling at 
22.51% due to the aforementioned reason.
•
The other income climbed up by 3x YoY to PKR 8,684mn during 1HCY24, owing 
to higher income from cash and cash balances tagged with dividend income from 
the associate company (AKBL) and joint venture. On the a quarterly basis, the 
other income ascended by 83% YoY on account dividend income from PMP.  
•
Finance costs plummeted by 60% YoY to PKR 2,122mn during 1HCY24 due to 
decline in short-term borrowing. Whereas, finance cost plummeted by 61% YoY 
to PKR 1,102mn in 2QCY24 owed to the above stated reason.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 44% in 2QCY24 vis-à-vis 76% in 
2QCY23.

Fauji Fertilizer Bin Qasim Limited

## REPORT 77
Title: AHL Alert: Result review: LOTCHEM - 1QCY24 EPS @ PKR 0.59
Created At: 2024-04-18T13:29:08.611Z
Tickers: LOTCHEM
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 662120240270a7c404e022f6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662120240270a7c404e022f6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
18 April 2024
Profitability contracts by 65% YoY in 1QCY24
Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LOTCHEM) announced its 1QCY24 financial result today where 
the company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 897mn (EPS: PKR 0.59), down by 65% YoY 
compared to PKR 2,542mn (EPS: PKR 1.68) during SPLY. On a sequential basis, earnings were 
up by 4x QoQ. Alongside the result, the company announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 
0.50/share.
Result Highlights 
▪
 Net sales during 1QCY24 climbed up by 46% YoY to PKR 32,277mn amid increase in PTA 
volumes tagged with Pak Rupee depreciation. On a QoQ basis, the topline surged by 66% 
YoY due to higher volumetric sales as the plant remained operational throughout the quarter 
while a 26-day plant shutdown was observed in 4Q. 
▪
The gross profit margin in 1QCY24 arrived at 5.2% compared to 20.1% during SPLY. The fall 
in margins was witnessed owing to 18% YoY decline in international PTA margins and an 
increase in gas prices.
▪
The company booked a finance cost of PKR 223mn during 1QCY24, showing a hefty decline 
of 65% YoY on account of the absence of exchange loss in the quarter.
▪
Other income reduced by 40% YoY to PKR 351mn during 1QCY24 due to lower short-term 
investments.
▪
The company booked effective taxation at 39% in 1QCY24 vis-à-vis 33% in 1QCY23.
REP-300
Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited

Title: Daily Call – Oil and Marketing Companies - Result Review of FY25
Created At: 2025-07-24T09:43:20.78Z
Tickers: AHL
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 688200384bcb380fce12bf3f

Daily Call 
OMCs 
Earnings Preview 
July 24, 2025 
PSO: Earnings to grow by 33% YoY to PKR 45.11/share in FY25 
Pakistan State Oil Company Ltd (PSO) is expected to announce FY25 financial result soon, 
where we expect the company to post a profit after tax of PKR 21,176mn (EPS: PKR 45.11),  up 
by 33% YoY. During the period, the net sales is anticipated to decline by 11% YoY amid reduction 
in sales volumes of petroleum products by 5% YoY (MS, HSD, and FO sales down by 4%, 5%, 
and 47% YoY, respectively) coupled with lower price of petroleum products. On a quarterly basis, 
the company is expected to report earnings of PKR 5,907mn (EPS: PKR 12.58) compared to a 
profit of PKR 2,465mn (EPS: PKR 5.25) in SPLY. Topline is projected to tumble by 6% YoY on 
the back of decline in price of MS and HSD. Meanwhile, petroleum sales increased by 3% YoY  
(MS and HSD up 6% and 2% YoY, respectively). Furthermore, revenue contribution from RLNG 
segment is projected to be PKR 252bn (down 13% YoY) amid 3% YoY lower quantity (28 cargos 
handled in 4QFY25 versus 29 cargos in SPLY), coupled with 6% YoY decline in DES price during 
the quarter. Gross margins are expected to settle at 2.26% in 4QFY25 amid lower inventory 
losses during the quarter (clocking in at ~PKR 4.6bn) compared to SPLY. The finance cost is 
forecasted to plummet by 45% YoY owed to lower short term borrowings and lower interest rates. 
The company is expected to give final dividend of PKR 13.00/share in FY25 compared to PKR 
10.00/share in SPLY.

APL: Profitability to decline by 29% YoY to PKR 79.01/share in FY25 
Attock Petroleum Limited (APL) is expected to announce its FY25 financial result soon, where 
we anticipate the company to post a Profit after tax of PKR 9,830mn (EPS: PKR 79.01) in FY25 
compared to PKR 13,822mn (EPS: PKR 111.09), down 29% YoY. Net sales are expected to 
dwindle by 9% YoY amid i) lower average retail price of petroleum products, and ii) reduction in 
MS and FO offtake by 2% and 48% YoY, respectively. On a quarterly basis, the net profit is 
expected to arrive at PKR 2,235mn (EPS: PKR 17.96), down 25% YoY. Topline of the company 
is projected to depict an increase of 5% YoY owed to increase in dispatches by 20% YoY. The 
gross margin in 4QFY25 is projected to be 3.5% compared to 3.7% in SPLY, amid effective 
inventory management by the company, we view. Alongside the result, APL is expected to 
announce a final cash dividend of PKR 14.00/share in 4QFY25 (PKR 26.50/share in FY25).

Description

SELL 
Upside* of subject security(ies) is less than -15% from last closing of market price(s)

## REPORT 79
Title: AHL Alert: HCAR - Result Review: Earnings arrive at PKR 16.34/share in MY24
Created At: 2024-05-27T12:09:17.273Z
Tickers: HCAR
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 665477eda7241003c6822a73
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/665477eda7241003c6822a73.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
27 May 2024
MY24: Profitability surged by 9x YoY, settling at PKR 16.34/share. 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for MY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 2,334mn (EPS: PKR 16.34), up by 9x YoY. During 4QMY24, the net 
profit of the company clocked in at PKR 1,370mn (EPS: PKR 9.59) compared to net loss of  
PKR 824mn (LPS: PKR 5.77) in 4QMY23. Alongside the result, the company announced a 
cash dividend of PKR 6.50/share.
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales during MY24 clocked in at PKR 55,071mn in contrast to PKR 95,087mn in 
SPLY, depicting a decline of 42% YoY primarily due to reduced customer purchasing 
power. On a quarterly basis, the topline went up by 12% YoY to record at PKR 24.9bn. On 
a sequential basis, the revenue swelled up by 101% QoQ amid surge in sales volumes by 
112% QoQ to 5,044 units vis-à-vis 2,375 units, which is attributable to the new-year effect. 
•
During MY24 gross margin arrived at 8.2%, in contrast to 7.5% during SPLY mainly due to 
a stable PKR-USD parity. On a quarterly basis, the gross margins settled at 8.4% during 
4QMY24 compared to 12.5% in SPLY, amid cost-push inflationary pressures and currency 
depreciation.
•
Other income declined by 3% YoY to clock in at PKR 2,252mn in MY24, which is mainly 
because of decrease in cash and cash equivalent. While, on quarterly basis, it plummeted 
by 76% YoY  due to the same aforementioned reason. 
•
Other expenses declined by 92% YoY to clock in at PKR 394mn in MY24, primarily due to 
the absence of significant exchange losses.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 15% in MY24 vis-à-vis  87% in SPLY.
REP-300
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited

## REPORT 80
Title: AHL Alert: MLCF - Result Review: Gross Margins in 2QFY25 at 32 quarters high
Created At: 2025-02-20T06:02:30.507Z
Tickers: MLCF
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 67b6c576881e05effa93af21
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67b6c576881e05effa93af21.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
20 February 2025
2QFY25: Earnings arrived at PKR 3.57/share
Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited (MLCF) announced its financial results for 1HFY25
today, reporting consolidated earnings of PKR 5,078mn (EPS: PKR 4.85), reflecting a
31% YoY increase compared to earnings of PKR 3,869mn (EPS: PKR 3.69) in the same
period last year. In 2QFY25, earnings amounted to PKR 3,735mn (EPS: PKR 3.57), up
67% YoY and 178% QoQ, compared to PKR 2,243mn (EPS: PKR 2.14) in 2QFY24.
Result Highlights
•
In 1HFY25, sales remained steady at PKR 34.7bn, unchanged from the previous year.
In 2QFY25, the topline grew to PKR 19.0bn, marking a 5% YoY increase from PKR
18.1bn in the same period last year. The sales growth was primarily driven by higher
retention prices, although dispatches in 2QFY25 declined by 3% YoY. However, a
21% QoQ increase in sales was observed, driven by a 21% QoQ rise in dispatches.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY25 improved to 36.1%, up from 33.4% in 1HFY24, mainly due
to the decline in Afghan coal prices and higher retention prices. In 2QFY25, gross
margins up by 450bps YoY to 39.8%, making the highest level since 2QFY17.
•
Selling and distribution expenses fell by 14% YoY in 1HFY25, totaling PKR 2,390mn.
In 2QFY25, these expenses dropped by 37% YoY and 23% QoQ.
•
Other income surged 8.3 times YoY to PKR 1,213mn in 1HFY25, largely driven by an
increase in short-term investments, which reached PKR 42.7bn by Dec’24. In
2QFY25, other income also rose 16.4x YoY for the same reason.
•
Finance costs grew by 12% YoY in 1HFY25, amounting to PKR 2,091mn, due to
higher borrowings, which reached PKR 37.1bn. In 2QFY25, finance costs increased
by 55% YoY and 110% QoQ, driven by the same factor.
•
The company reported an effective tax rate of 27.1% in 2QFY25, compared to 23.3%
in the same period last year.
REP-300
Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited
