## REPORT 81
Title: AHL Alert - OGDC Record Dividend Payout PKR 11/share in 9MFY26
Created At: 2026-04-29T12:50:06.997Z
Tickers: OGDC
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 69f1fe7e9730304360d0f956
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1fe7e9730304360d0f956.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
3QFY26 earnings arrived at PKR 9.82/share, down by 10% YoY I DPS at PKR 3.25
Oil and Gas Development Company (OGDC) reported a 10% YoY decline in 3QFY26
earnings to PKR 42.2bn (EPS: PKR 9.82), down from PKR 47.1bn (EPS: PKR 10.96),
primarily due to a 36% drop in other income. The company declared a dividend of PKR
3.25/share, lifting the payout ratio to 33% (vs. 27% in 3QFY25). This brings 9MFY26 DPS
to a record PKR 11/share, with the payout ratio rising to 41%, supported by improved
liquidity following receivable recoveries, including TFCs.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales rose 3% YoY to PKR 107bn, driven by higher oil and gas output, which
increased 1% and 3% YoY, respectively. Production growth was observed across key
fields, including Uch (+9% YoY, supported by strong power demand), Nashpa (+13%
YoY), TAL (+16% YoY), and KPD (+15% YoY), while Qadirpur declined by 10% YoY.
•
Exploration costs declined 12% YoY to PKR 6.0bn in 3QFY26, as no dry well was
reported during the quarter.
•
Other income fell 36% YoY to PKR 11.5bn in 3QFY26, mainly due to lower finance
income in a declining interest rate environment. The decline was partially offset by the
remaining unwinding of TFC-related losses.
•
During 3QFY26, OGDC’s recovery ratio declined to 86%, resulting in a rise in trade
receivables to PKR 598.7bn, compared to PKR 583.8bn in 2QFY26.
•
Meanwhile, cash and short-term investments slightly declined to PKR 252.1bn (PKR
59/share) in Mar’25, compared to PKR 255.5bn (PKR 59/share) in Dec’25.
•
The effective tax rate in 3QFY26 stood at 35%, compared to 30% in 3QFY25.
•
On the Reko Diq and PIOL front, OGDC made an additional investment of PKR 11.2bn
in Mar’26 (vs PKR 11.7bn), bringing total 9MFY26 investment to PKR 25.3bn,
compared to PKR 17.0bn in 9MFY25. In our view, following PPL’s results, a major
portion of future investment is likely to be directed toward the Reko Diq project.
Recommendation
We maintain a Buy stance on OGDC, with a Dec’26 target price of PKR 347/share. The
stock is trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 8.6x and 7.4x, respectively.
REP-300
Zayan Babar Khan | Muhammad Anas Habib

Title: AHL Alert: ENGRO - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 22.33 in 9MCY24
Created At: 2024-10-29T05:19:42.019Z
Tickers: ENGRO
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 6720706e8f84bab98c39c3f1

--- Page 1 ---
29 October 2024
3QCY24: Bottom-line plunges by 53% YoY
Engro Corporation Limited (ENGRO) announced its 9MCY24 financial result with a
consolidated profit after tax of PKR 11,983mn (EPS: PKR 22.33), depicting a decline of 48%
YoY. On a quarterly basis, the consolidated earnings clocked in at PKR 5,722mn (EPS: PKR
10.66), plunging by 53% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced dividend of PKR
5.00/share in 3QCY24 (PKR 24.00/share in 9MCY24).
Result Highlights
•
On the fertilizer business front, EFERT’s earnings settled at PKR 8,554mn (EPS: PKR
6.41), declining by 11% YoY during 3QCY24 which is due to i) 33% and 14% YoY fall in
urea and DAP offtake, respectively, and ii) higher finance cost amid jump in short term
borrowings.
•
Meanwhile, Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) registered a loss of PKR 698mn
(LPS: PKR 0.77) compared to net profit of PKR 2,642mn (EPS: PKR 2.91) due to higher
gas prices and subdued PVC margins.
•
FCEPL posted earnings of PKR 766mn (EPS: PKR 1.00), up 3x YoY on the back of
increase in gross margins by 190bps to 15.40% amid higher sales of dairy products.
•
The other income of ENGRO depicted a jump of 53% YoY during 3QCY24, which is
attributable to a surge in income from cash and cash balances.
•
The finance cost of the company augmented by 41% YoY on the back of higher short term
borrowings.
•
The company booked gain on allowance on subsidy receivable from Govt. amounting to
PKR 464mn in 3QCY24 against the gain allowance on subsidiary receivable from Govt. of
PKR 1mn in 3QCY23.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 39.95% in 3QCY24 vis-à-vis 40.54% in 3QCY23.

Engro Corporation Limited

## REPORT 83
Title: AHL Alert: PSO - Result Review: Company reported earnings of PKR 26.4/share in 9MFY25
Created At: 2025-04-29T11:47:01.741Z
Tickers: PSO
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 6810bc351c371b6b18f102b6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6810bc351c371b6b18f102b6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
Muhammad Iqbal Jawaid I Menka Kirpalani
D: +92 21 38280256
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 255
E: iqbal.Jawaid@arifhabibltd.com | menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 29-Apr-2025 
Oil Marketing Companies: Pakistan State Oil Company Limited
Result Review: Company reported earnings of PKR 26.4/share in 9MFY25
•
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited (PSO) announced the financial result for
9MFY25 today, whereby the company has posted a net profit of PKR 15.3bn
(EPS: PKR 32.52) compared to PKR 13.4bn (EPS: PKR 28.54) in 9MFY24, up
14% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the company booked a profit of PKR 4bn
(EPS:PKR 8.71) in 3QFY25 against a profit of PKR 5.6bn (EPS: PKR 12.03) in
3QFY24.
•
Net sales during 9MFY25 contracted by 13% YoY, settling at PKR 2,337bn, which
comes on the back of reduction in sales volume of petroleum products (MS, HSD,
and FO down by 8%, 7%, and 47% YoY, respectively). On a quarterly basis, the
topline in 3QFY25 declined by 16% YoY to PKR 711bn amid decline in MS and
HSD prices. The overall sales volume declined by 14% YoY in 3QFY25, MS and
HSD sales declined by 15% and 13% YoY, respectively. Although we await
disclosure of detailed accounts, we estimate the revenue contribution from RLNG
segment to be ~PKR 250bn (down 1% YoY) given 9% YoY decline in DES price,
while 27 cargos were handled in 3QFY25 (which is similar to last year).
•
During 3QFY25, the company posted gross profit was recorded at PKR 22.5bn
down by 9% YoY. The decline was due to the inventory loss incurred by the
company during the quarter compared to inventory gains in SPLY.
•
The finance costs plunged by 49% YoY to PKR 7.7bn in 3QFY25 due to 11% YoY
fall in short-term borrowings (PKR 361bn in Mar’25 versus PKR 408bn in Mar’24).
•
The other income climbed up by 2x YoY amid higher interest on delayed payment
tagged with financial charges on line fill cost, we view.
•
The company booked an effective taxation of 64.3% in 3QFY25.
•
We maintain a BUY stance on PSO, with a target price of PKR 473.7/share by the
end of Dec’25. The stock is currently trading at an attractive forward P/E of 4.7x

## REPORT 84
Title: AHL Alert: INDU - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 191.76 in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-02T08:35:20.826Z
Tickers: INDU
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 66d578c8311a3f7dcc1be8cf
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66d578c8311a3f7dcc1be8cf.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2 September 2024
FY24: Profitability climbed up by 56% YoY, settling at PKR 191.76/share
INDU announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QFY24 at PKR 5.7bn (EPS: PKR 72.09), depicting
an increase of 48% YoY (4QFY23 EPS: PKR 48.61). With this, FY24’s PAT settles at PKR
15.1bn (EPS: 191.76), up by 56% YoY (FY23: PKR 122.96). Alongside this, the company
announced a final cash dividend of PKR 43.0/share (PKR 114.7/share in FY24).
Result Highlights

Topline during FY24 clocked in at PKR 152.5bn in contrast to PKR 177.7bn in SPLY,
depicting a decline of 14% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the topline witnessed a an increase of
27% YoY on the back of 28% higher volumetric sales (4QFY24: 7,069 units compared to
4QFY23: 5,512 units) and the substantial contribution of the Corolla Cross to the sales mix.

During FY24 gross margin arrived at 12.7%, in contrast to 4.46%% during SPLY mainly due
to a stable PKR USD parity, some easing in import restrictions and the addition of the
Corolla Cross, which benefited from reduced customs duties and sales tax.

Distribution expense surged by 3.3x YoY settling at PKR 5.5bn during FY24, which is
attributable to an increase in marketing and distribution expenses.

Other income declined by 4% YoY to clock in at PKR 13.7bn in FY24, which is mainly
because of decrease in short-term investments. While, on quarterly basis, it increased by
68% YoY, due to higher income from cash and cash equivalent.

The company booked effective taxation at 32% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 57% in 4QFY23.
REP-300
Indus Motor Company Limited

## REPORT 86
Title: AHL Alert: DGKC - Result Review: 1HFY25 EPS @ PKR 8.04
Created At: 2025-02-19T12:39:46.42Z
Tickers: DGKC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 67b5d112881e05effa649c40
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67b5d112881e05effa649c40.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
19 February 2025
2QFY25: Earnings arrived at PKR 6.21/share
D.G. Khan Cement Company Limited (DGKC) announced its financial results for 1HFY25
today, reporting earnings of PKR 3,523mn (EPS: PKR 8.04), reflecting a 234% YoY
increase compared to PKR 1,054mn (EPS: PKR 2.41) in 1HFY24. In 2QFY25, earnings
reached PKR 2,719mn (EPS: PKR 6.21), marking a 591% YoY increase compared to
PKR 394mn (EPS: PKR 0.90) in 2QFY24.
Result Highlights
•
During 1HFY25, sales reached PKR 37.0bn, reflecting a 6% YoY growth from PKR
34.8bn in the same period last year, driven by an 8% YoY increase in dispatches. In
2QFY25, the topline rose to PKR 21.7bn, marking a 19% YoY increase from PKR
18.3bn in the same period last year, due to a 15% YoY rise in dispatches.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY25 stood at 22.8%, up from 15.9% in the same period last
year, primarily due to higher capacity utilization. In 2QFY25, gross margins also
increased by 1238bps YoY to 25.1%, driven by the same factors.
•
Selling and distribution expenses rose by 63% YoY to PKR 1,956mn in 1HFY25,
mainly attributed to higher sales volume. In 2QFY25, these expenses also surged by
64% YoY and 39% QoQ.
•
Other income up by 3% YoY to PKR 2,227mn in 1HFY25, driven by higher dividend
income. In 2QFY25, other income decreased by 11% YoY but rose by 15% QoQ.
•
Finance costs fell by 36% YoY to PKR 2,640mn in 1HFY25, benefiting from lower
interest rates. In 2QFY25, finance costs also declined by 48% YoY and 34% QoQ, due
to the same factor.
•
The company recorded an effective tax rate of 30.0% in 2QFY25, compared to 40.3%
in the same period last year.
REP-300
D.G Khan Cement Company Limited

## REPORT 87
Title: AHL Alert: FABL - Result Review: 2QCY24 EPS arrives at PKR 4.6
Created At: 2024-08-28T09:44:02.984Z
Tickers: FABL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 66cef162311a3f7dcc3aaaa4
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66cef162311a3f7dcc3aaaa4.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 August 2024
2QCY24: EPS arrives at PKR 4.6 fueled by higher other income
FABL announced earnings today for 2QCY24 at PKR 6.9bn (EPS: PKR 4.6), depicting an
uptick of 60% YoY | 5% QoQ. For 1HCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 13.6bn,
marking a 77% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled by a jump in
total income. The bank also announced a cash dividend of PKR 2/share in 2QCY24 along
with the result taking total payout for 1HCY24 to PKR 3/share.
Result Highlights
•
The bank’s Net profit amounted to PKR 19.6bn for 2QCY24, marking a YoY surge of
11%. However, on a QoQ basis, NP showed a slight increase of 5%. The bank faced a
rise in profit expensed, which increased by 35% YoY while down by 2% QoQ. On the
other hand, profit earned grew by 26% YoY while remained stagnant on QoQ basis.
•
The bank’s Other income surged by 100% YoY during 2QCY24, bringing the total for
1HCY24 to PKR 9.7bn, a 91% YoY increase. The increase is primarily fueled by a 38%
YoY rise in fee income, reaching PKR 6.0bn, and a significant boost in foreign
exchange income, which climbed to PKR 2.8bn from PKR 1.8bn in the same period last
year. Furthermore, the bank posted a gain of PKR 639mn from the sale of securities,
compared to a loss of PKR 1,535mn in the same period last year.
•
A provisioning reversal was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 834mn,
this took the provisioning reversal for the 1HCY24 to PKR 798mn compared to a
provisioning charge during 1HCY23 of PKR 2.3bn.
•
The bank’s OPEX increased by 24% YoY in 1HCY24 clocking-in at PKR 22.6bn
(2QCY24: PKR 11.6bn, +14% YoY | +5% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 47.6%
in 2QCY24 against 50.7% in same period last year.
•
Effective tax rate was set at 48.9% during 2QCY24 compared to 51.0% SPLY.
REP-300

Sectors: 0807

--- Page 1 ---
31 July 2024
1HCY24: Bottom-line witnessed a growth of 14% YoY
UBL announced earnings today for 2QCY24 at PKR 14.8bn (EPS: PKR 12.1), depicting an 
uptick of 17% YoY while down by 7% QoQ. For 1HCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 
30.8bn, marking a 14% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled by a jump 
in total income and provisioning reversals. The bank also announced a cash dividend of 
PKR 11.00/share along with the result taking total payout for 1HCY24 to PKR 22/share
Result Highlights 
•
The bank's NII stood at PKR 29.2bn for the 2QCY24, reflecting a YoY decline of 22%. 
However, on a QoQ basis, NII saw a modest increase of 4%. The bank experienced a 
significant increase in the interest expense, which surged by 283% YoY and by 17% QoQ. 
Moreover, the interest income also experienced substantial growth, rising by 173% YoY 
and by 16% QoQ.
•
The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 157% YoY during 2QCY24, bringing the 
total for the 1HCY24 to PKR 40.6 bn, a 142% YoY increase. This significant rise is 
primarily attributed to a gain on the sale of securities amounting to PKR 16.6bn, compared 
to a loss of PKR 637mn in the same period last year. Additionally, fee and commission 
income increased by 19% YoY to PKR 11.5bn in 1HCY24. However, foreign exchange 
income declined by 8% YoY, amounting to PKR 6.5bn during 1HCY24.
•
A provisioning reversal was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 644mn, this 
took the provisioning reversal for the 1HCY24 to PKR 2,362mn compared to a 
provisioning charge during 1HCY23 of PKR 3.0bn. 
•
The bank’s OPEX increased by 19% YoY in 1HCY24 clocking-in at PKR 40.2bn 
(2QCY23: PKR 20.4bn, +16% YoY | 2% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 42.0% in 
2QCY24 against 38.9% in same period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 47.5% during 2QCY24 compared to 51.5% SPLY.

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 29-Apr-2025 
E&P: Pakistan Petroleum Limited
Result Review: Earnings settled at PKR 26.72/share in 9MFY25

Pakistan Petroleum Ltd (PPL) announced its financial result today, posting a net profit of
PKR 72.7bn (EPS: PKR 26.72) during 9MFY25, down 25% YoY. On a quarterly basis,
earnings settled at PKR 21.8bn (EPS: PKR 8.01), down 21% YoY. In addition to the
result, PPL announced a DPS of PKR 1.00 in 3QFY25 (9MFY25: DPS PKR 5.00).

Net sales in 9MFY25 depicted a decline of 15% YoY, settling at PKR 190,723mn
against PKR 224,660mn in SPLY on the back of i) a 9% and 8% YoY fall in oil and gas
production, respectively, ii) lower oil prices (down 10% YoY), and iii) lower wellhead
price of Sui. During 3QFY25, the topline plummeted by 15% YoY, arriving at PKR
63,825mn, owing to 7% and 6% YoY reduction in oil and gas production, respectively,
along with 6% fall in Arab Light price.

The exploration cost declined by 3% YoY to PKR 11,621mn in 9MFY25. Whereas, the
exploration cost during 3QFY25 settled at PKR 4,919mn, augmenting by 44% YoY. We
await disclosure of detailed accounts in this regard.

Other income clocked in at PKR 19,474mn in 9MFY25, displaying surge of 68% YoY.
Whereas, other income during 3QFY25 arrived at PKR 4,232mn, witnessing a jump of
11% YoY owed to higher income from cash and cash balances.

The company booked effective taxation at 38% in 3QFY25, which is similar to SPLY.

The trade debts in Mar’25 stood at PKR 592bn versus PKR 576bn in Dec’24.

As of Mar’25, the company’s cash and cash equivalent reached PKR 133bn compared
to PKR 140bn in Dec’24.

The pay-out ratio has reached 18.7% in 9MFY25 compared 9.9% in SPLY.

We have a “BUY” stance on the scrip, with Dec’25 target price of PKR 210.6/share. The
scrip is trading at forward P/E of 4.2x for FY26E

## REPORT 90
Title: AHL Alert: HTL - Result Review: Earnings settled at PKR 0.14/share in 1QFY25
Created At: 2024-10-28T05:45:21.012Z
Tickers: HTL
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 671f24f18f84bab98cda9a89
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671f24f18f84bab98cda9a89.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 October 2024
Profitability arrived at PKR 20mn in 1QFY25
Hi-Tech Lubricants Limited (HTL) announced its financial result for 1QFY25 whereby the 
company, on consolidated basis, declared a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 20mn (EPS: 
PKR 0.14) compared to loss of PKR 57mn (LPS: PKR 0.41) in 1QFY24. 
Result Highlights 
•
Topline of the company settled at PKR 7,103mn in 1QFY25, up by 74% YoY in SPLY. 
The jump in sales is due to significant growth in volumetric sales on the back of 
increase in fuel stations coupled with improvement in the prices of lubricants. On a 
quarterly basis sales decline by 15% YoY amid fall in demand for white oil.
•
The company posted gross margins at 9.0% in 1QFY25 compared gross margins of  
13.4% in SPLY. The decline in margins is primarily driven by a reduction in lubricant 
sales, coupled with a significant increase in white oil sales, which is a lower-margin 
product. Additionally, higher discounts were offered during the quarter to attract new 
customers and boost sales, further impacting margins. On a QoQ basis margins 
decline by a 55bps.
•
Other operating income settled at PKR 81mn in 1QFY25, down by 27% YoY. Whereas, 
on a quarterly basis, other income rose by 116% QoQ.
•
Meanwhile, in 1QFY25 finance costs fell by 12% YoY to PKR 177mn in lieu of lower 
interest rate in tandem with fall in debt. On a quarterly basis finance cost dipped by 
20% due decline in policy rate.
•
The company recorded effective tax reversal of PKR 18mn in 1QFY25 compared to tax 
charge of PKR 37mn in SPLY.
REP-300
Hi-Tech Lubricants Limited
Result Review: Earnings settled at PKR 0.14/share in 1QFY25

Title: AHL Alert: KOHC - Result Review: FY24 EPS @ PKR 45.41; Buyback of 12mn shares
Created At: 2024-09-05T10:58:03.895Z
Tickers: KOHC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 66d98ebb50f122f1ba22cdb0
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
5 September 2024
4QFY24: Profitability augmented by 5x YoY to PKR 12.16/share
Kohat Cement Company Limited (KOHC) announced its financial result for FY24 today,
posting a PAT of PKR 8,893mn (EPS: PKR 45.41) compared to PKR 5,821mn (EPS: PKR
28.98) in SPLY, up by 53% YoY. During 4QFY24 earnings clocked in at PKR 2,382mn (EPS:
PKR 12.16), showcasing a significant jump of 5x YoY.
Result Highlights
•
Topline during FY24 slightly declined by 1% YoY clocking at PKR 38,648mn. During
4QFY24 net sales plummeted by 5% YoY to settle at PKR 8,676mn in contrast to PKR
9,097mn in SPLY.
•
Gross margins for FY24 arrived at 29% vis-à-vis 27% in SPLY. During 4QFY24, the gross
margins climbed by 466bps to 32% owing to higher cement prices in tandem with better
coal management.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in FY24 climbed up by 12% YoY to clock in at PKR
197mn amid introduction of the axle load factor, we view. In 4QFY24, selling and
distribution expenses arrived at PKR 60mn, down by 1% YoY.
•
Other income surged by 2x YoY in FY24 to clock in at PKR 4,454mn, due to higher
income from cash and cash balances. In 4QFY24, the other income arrived at PKR
1,309mn, depicting a massive growth of 76% YoY due to the aforementioned reason.
•
Finance costs in FY24 declined by 9% YoY to PKR 677mn. In 4QFY24, the finance cost
contracted by 38% YoY, which is due to fall in the long-term finances.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 37% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 83% in 4QFY23.
Buyback announced
•
Along with the result, the company has announced a buyback of 12mn ordinary shares
(6% of the total outstanding shares and 20% of the total free-float). The buyback period
will commence from 23rd Oct'24 till 12th Apr’25
REP-300
Kohat Cement Company Limited

Title: AHL Alert - HBL EPS clocked-in at 11/share for 1QCY26
Created At: 2026-04-17T13:33:49.678Z
Tickers: HBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 69e236bd1f328d6e653eba82
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69e236bd1f328d6e653eba82.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 17-Apr-2026 
Commercial Bank: Habib Bank Limited
Result Review: EPS clocked-in at 11/share for 1QCY26
REP-300

HBL reported earnings of PKR 16.1bn (EPS: PKR 11.00) for 1QCY26, reflecting a
decline of 3% YoY increase and a growth of 5% QoQ. Alongside the results, the bank
announced a cash dividend of PKR 6/share for the quarter, with a payout ratio of
~55%.

Yearly dip in earnings was largely attributable to higher provisioning expense for the
quarter, although quarter
wise momentum was continued by higher NII and
meaningful contributions from Non-funded income sources.

The bank reported its net interest income at PKR 71bn (4% YoY, 5% QoQ). Higher NII
was on the back of higher QoQ growth in interest earned, which was recorded at PKR
184bn (18% YoY, 4% QoQ), compared to interest expensed at PKR 113bn (+28%
YoY, 3% QoQ).

Non-funded income registered a decline of 5% YoY, due to lower gains booked on
securities, reported at PKR 297mn, vs PKR 4.2bn in 1QCY25, and a loss of PKR
1.4bn in 4QCY25. Other income declined by 22% YoY to reach PKR 1.8bn, while fee
income and dividend income both grew by 8.6% YoY and 158% YoY to reach PKR
12.6bn and PKR 3.6bn respectively.

On the provisioning front, the bank recognized relatively higher provisioning expenses
at PKR 4.3bn, growing by 58% YoY in 1QCY26 compared to PKR 2.7bn in 1QCY25.

OPEX grew by 5.5% YoY, taking the bank’s cost to income ratio to 58% for 1QCY26
vs 56% in 1QCY25. Moreover, a lower effective tax rate was recorded for the quarter
at 52%, compared to 57% for 1QCY25.

In terms of balance sheet dynamics, deposits grew by 21% YoY to reach PKR 5.4trn,
while investments and advances were recorded at PKR 4.8trn (+70% YoY) and PKR
2.0trn (+4% YoY), bringing the ADR and IDR to 38% and 90% respectively.

The stock is currently trading at a P/B multiple of 0.9x for CY26e and a Dividend Yield
of 7.8% based on LDCP.
Arham Ahmed

Created At: 2024-04-25T13:05:57.455Z

Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 662a5535b3858eb2c783697d

--- Page 1 ---
25 April 2024
3QFY24: Earnings arrived at PKR 1.30/share
Attock Cement Pakistan Limited (ACPL) announced its financial result for 9MFY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 2,219mn (EPS: PKR 16.14) compared to PKR 1,057 (EPS: PKR 7.70) 
in SPLY, a jump of 110% YoY, mainly owed to gain on sale of the subsidiary ‘Saqr AL Keetan 
for Cement Production Company Limited’. During 3QFY24 earnings came at PKR 178mn 
(EPS: PKR 1.30) against PKR 497mn (EPS: PKR 3.62), a fall of 64% YoY.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 9MFY24 settled at PKR 21,694mn as compared to PKR 18,390mn in SPLY, 
a growth of 18% YoY, due to higher retention prices along with higher dispatches of 1,771k 
(up 21% YoY). In 3QFY24 net sales fell by 12% YoY to settle at PKR 7,056mn against 
PKR 8,017mn in SPLY, amid fall in total offtake by 18% YoY for the quarter.
•
Gross margins for 9MFY24 arrived at 19% vis-à-vis 21% in SPLY, on the back of rise in 
exports. During 3QFY24 gross margins fell by 334bps to 18% as compared to SPLY, due 
to the aforementioned reason.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in 9MFY24 increased by 61% YoY, attributed to the 
elevated freight charges amid rise in exports, to settle at PKR 2,117mn, we view. In 
3QFY24, selling and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 701mn vis-à-vis PKR 671mn, up 
by 4% YoY.
•
Finance costs in 9MFY24 fell by 40% YoY to PKR 186mn on the back of repayment of 
debt. In 3QFY24, the finance cost arrived at PKR 96mn, showcasing a jump of 3% YoY.
•
The company booked an effective taxation at 42% in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis 32% in 3QFY23.

## REPORT 94
Title: AHL Alert - NBP Highest ever PAT and DPS in CY25
Created At: 2026-02-25T06:12:28.562Z
Tickers: NBP
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 699e92ccd33a93d06ba881ae
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699e92ccd33a93d06ba881ae.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 25-Feb-2026 
Commercial Bank: National Bank of Pakistan
Result Review: Highest ever PAT and DPS in CY25
REP-300
•
NBP reported record earnings of PKR 85bn (EPS: PKR 39.9) in CY25, growing by
227% YoY. On a quarterly basis the earnings were recorded at PKR 19.3bn, down by
13% YoY and 16% QoQ. The bank also announced its highest ever dividend of PKR
35/share in CY25, compared to PKR 8/share in CY24.
•
The sharp improvement in earnings was mainly driven by strong growth in NII and
lower OPEX.
•
The bank’s NII stood at PKR 248.6bn in CY25, marking a 45% YoY increase. This
growth was primarily driven by a larger interest expense decline of 42% YoY while
interest income declined by 28% YoY, resulting in a net positive impact on income.
•
Non-funded income declined by 1% YoY. Major contributors included lower gains on
securities at PKR 13.5bn (-52.1% YoY) and mild dividend income at PKR 5.3bn (-
11.7% YoY). Meanwhile Fee Income grew to PKR 34.7bn (+18.9% YoY) and FX
income to PKR 15.8bn (+167.8% YoY), providing key support.
•
On the provisioning front, NBP posted provisioning expenses of PKR 9.2bn in CY25,
compared to PKR 4.5bn in CY24.
•
Meanwhile OPEX contracted by 28% YoY to settle at PKR 129.3bn, taking the annual
cost-to-income ratio to 41% for CY25 a significant reduction from 74.5%, which
included a one-off pension charge in CY24.
•
The effective tax rate of the bank was recorded at 52% in CY25 compared to 53.8% in
CY24.
•
In terms of balance sheet dynamics, the deposits grew by 15% to reach PKR 4.4trn,
while investments and advances settled at PKR 4.9trn (+7% YoY) and PKR 1.3trn
(-5% YoY). This took the bank’s IDR to 111% and ADR to 30.2% for CY25.
•
We maintain a BUY call on the stock, with a P/E of 7.5x and a P/B of 1.1x for CY26e
based on LDCP.

## REPORT 95
Title: AHL Alert: FCCL - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 2.15
Created At: 2024-02-26T13:16:37.555Z
Tickers: FCCL
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 65dc8f35427e430fa3e40442
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65dc8f35427e430fa3e40442.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability rose by 4% YoY during 1HFY24
Fauji Cement Company Limited (FCCL) announced its financial result for 1HFY24 today, posting 
a PAT of PKR 5,274mn (EPS: PKR 2.15) compared to a PAT of PKR 5,079mn (EPS: PKR 2.07) 
in SPLY, up by 4% YoY. During 2QFY24 the profitability of the company dropped to PKR 
2,660mn (EPS: PKR 1.08) vis-à-vis PKR 2,764 (EPS: PKR 1.13), a fall of 4% YoY.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 1HFY24 clocked-in at PKR 40,352mn, displaying a jump of 20% YoY in 
contrast to PKR 33,673mn in SPLY, amid rise in retention prices. In 2QFY24 net sales 
climbed by 6% YoY to settle at PKR 20,039mn.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY24 grew by 400bps arriving at 32% as compared to the same period 
last year. During 2QFY24 gross margins are recorded at 33% vis-à-vis 27% in SPLY. This is 
primarily attributable to increase in cement prices in tandem with better inventory 
management.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in 1HFY24 soared by 39% YoY to arrive at PKR 1,640mn, 
on the back of general uptick in inflation. In 2QFY24, distribution expense settled at PKR 
936mn vis-à-vis PKR 676mn.
•
Finance costs in 1HFY24 increased by 215% YoY to clock in at PKR 2,005mn on the back of 
higher long term loan in tandem with elevated borrowing cost. In 2QFY24 finance cost arrived 
at PKR 935mn, a jump of 136% due to reason mentioned above.
•
The company booked an effective taxation of 35% in 1HFY24.
Additional information
•
The company successfully commissioned its greenfield project on 30th Nov’23, having a 
capacity of 6,500 tons per day.
•
The Board approved enhancement in renewable energy capacity through installation of 
additional Solar plant of 12.5MW taking total capacity to 52.6MW.
1

## REPORT 96
Title: AHL Alert: MEBL - Highest-ever profitability recorded in CY24
Created At: 2025-02-13T10:03:21.864Z
Tickers: MEBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 67adc3698ad2dd7a1e17a2ac
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67adc3698ad2dd7a1e17a2ac.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
13 February 2025
Profitability for CY24 crossed PKR 100bn mark
MEBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY24 at PKR 24.9bn (EPS: PKR 13.66), depicting a
decline of 8% YoY | 5% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 14.97). This took the CY24 earnings to PKR
103.2bn (EPS: PKR 57.28) up by 20% YoY. The jump in overall earnings was mainly on the back
of growth in total income. Along with the result, MEBL announced a final dividend of PKR 7/share
(CY24: PKR 28/share).
Result Highlights

Net Profit Earned by the bank clocked-in at PKR 72.3bn during 4QCY24, up by 1% YoY while
down by 6% QoQ. With this, the total NPE for CY24 went up to PKR 287.0bn, marking an 27%
YoY jump. Profit earned saw a jump of 14% YoY while profit expensed saw a jump of 1% YoY
during CY24.

NFI depicted an increase of 50% YoY | 76% QoQ during 4QCY24, taking CY24’s total to PKR
32bn (+33% YoY). This jump in NFI during CY24 was primarily due to higher fee and
commission income which posted a jump of 32% YoY, clocking in at PKR 25.8bn. In addition, the
bank posted a gain on securities of PKR 3,512mn as compared to a loss of PKR 698mn
recorded in SPLY. However, MEBL’s FX income declined by 73% YoY during the outgoing year.

MEBL booked provisioning of PKR 7.3bn during 4QCY24 (4QCY23: PKR 2.9bn) depicting a
jump of 2.5x on a YoY basis. This takes total provisioning to PKR 9.2bn during CY24 (CY23:
PKR 7.3bn).

The bank’s OPEX declined 13% YoY in 4QCY24 clocking-in at PKR 18.7bn (4QCY23: PKR
21.4bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 22.4% in 4QCY24 against 27.0% same period last year.

Effective tax rate was set at 58.2% during 4QCY24 compared to 51.7% SPLY. With this, the
effective tax for CY24 stood at 54.2% (CY23: 50.0%)
Balance sheet update:

Net advances surged by 58% YoY, primarily driven by the bank's focus on meeting the 50%+
ADR threshold, leading to an increase in ADR to 59%, we believe. Additionally, the bank
achieved impressive deposit growth of 17% YoY.
REP-300
Meezan Bank Limited
Highest-ever profitability recorded in CY24

## REPORT 97
Title: AHL Alert - LUCK: 1HFY23 EPS @ PKR 22.15
Created At: 2023-01-30T06:40:11Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 64910eb37cf4c3f30fb88613
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=400&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/64910eb37cf4c3f30fb88613.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
P a g e  1 | 2 
 
AHL Alert 
Cement 
Lucky Cement Limited 
January 30, 2023 
 
  
 LUCK’s bottom-line jumped up by 32% YoY during the quarter 
Lucky Cement Limited (LUCK) announced the financial result for 2QFY23 today whereby unconsolidated 
earnings arrived at PKR 3,276mn (EPS: PKR 10.18; based on adjusted number of shares of 321.8mn 
since buy back is underway) against PKR 2,491mn (EPS: PKR 7.74) last year and PKR 3,852mn (EPS: 
PKR 11.97) in 1QFY23. This took the 1HFY23 profitability to PKR 7,129mn (EPS: PKR 22.15), up by 
23% YoY  
    Result Highlights 
 
 During 2QFY23, sales arrived at PKR 25.6bn (+24% YoY), as a result of over 40% jump in local 
retention prices which countered the impact of an 18% YoY decline in offtake to 1,994k tons given 
slowdown in the economy. Whereas on a QoQ basis, revenue underwent a growth of 30% primarily 
due to a 46% surge in dispatches (1QFY23: 1,577k tons owed to floods across the country) coupled 
with a slight uptick in prices. During 1HFY23, revenue witnessed an increase of 21% amid higher 
retention prices which offset the impact of a 24% YoY dip in offtake to 3,572k tons. 
 The gross margins in 2QFY23 settled at 25.4% vis-à-vis 22.6% in SPLY owed to strong retention 
prices which offset the impact of volumetric decline, jump in coal prices, higher energy tariff, and 
PKR depreciation. Albeit, they remained lower than expectations as we believe company had to 
majorly rely on FO / National grid compared to natural gas historically. On a QoQ basis, gross 
margins compressed from 30.6% in 1QFY23 led by augmented coal inventory price, and change 
in power mix towards FO / National grid from gas, which offset the impact of volumetric growth and 
higher retention prices. During the half year, margins arrived at 27.6% compared to 24.7% last 
year, owed to robust retention prices.  
 Other income grew by 12% YoY to PKR 838mn in 2QFY23 owed to augmented markup income on 
short term investments, and dividend income from Yunas Energy Limited (PKR 0.1bn). 
 Finance cost arrived at PKR 360mn during the quarter under review, up by 4x YoY / 43% QoQ, 
attributable to augmented borrowing and higher interest rates.  
 The company booked effective taxation at 30% in 2QFY23 vs. 24% in SPLY, amid recognition of 
super tax to the tune of 4%.  
 Consolidated profit remained higher than expected at PKR 10,464mn (EPS: PKR 32.51) vs. PKR 
6,496mn (EPS: PKR 20.09) in 2QFY23. This took the profit in 1HFY23 to 15,913mn (EPS: PKR 
49.32) against PKR 13,147mn (EPS: PKR 40.66) last year owed to profitability of Lucky Electric 
Power Company Limited compared to a loss it posted in SPLY, as well as augmented profits of 
JV’s and associates (combined earnings of Congo, Iraq and Younus Energy), which offset the 
impact of a decline in earnings of auto business (Lucky Motor Corporation) and chemical business 
(Lucky Core Industries – previously ICI).

P a g e  2 | 2 
 
AHL Alert 
Cement 
Lucky Cement Limited 
January 30, 2023 
Other information: 
 The company’s brownfield cement expansion of 3.15mn tons at Pezu achieved COD in Dec’22, 
taking the total capacity of the company to 15mn tons per annum.  
 A 34MW solar power project also commissioned at Pezu in Dec’22. While LUCK’s 25.3MW solar 
power project at its Karachi plant has completed commercial negotiations, and is expected to come 
online in 2QFY24.  
 Plant availability of Lucky Electric Power Company Limited in 1HFY23 improved to 92.0% (aimed 
at 100% going forward) vs. 78.4% last year, as management took remedial actions to resolve initial 
teething issues. The company also observed its first maintenance shutdown during the quarter, 
while average fuel cost of electricity arrived at PKR 15.16/KwH, cheapest in the country.  
 Demand in Iraq and Congo remained robust despite global challenges. The company’s subsidiary 
in Najmat-Al-Samawah (Iraq) switched to natural gas from furnace oil to fire its kiln, which aided 
the profitability of the venture. Going forward, strong demand in Congo is projected to aid earnings 
however, depreciating currency of Iraq and high input costs may keep margins under pressure.  
 Volumes of the auto and phone business continued to post a decline due to slowdown in demand 
and LC opening issues, which curtailed availability of raw material. The company is focusing on 
implementing cost-saving measures as well as improve localization so as to shield its profitability.  
 The company’s chemical subsidiary – Lucky Core Industries – submitted a bid to acquire 75.01% 
shares of Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited. After signing a share purchase agreement, the 
transaction is now subject to approval from the Competition Commission of Pakistan.   
 For the Polyster, Soda Ash and Chemicals segment, the management views further threats to 
demand in upcoming quarters emanating from high inflationary pressure amid high energy prices, 
imposition of additional taxes, monetary tightening, and compressed govt spending.  
 The company’s shares buyback of 10mn shares at spot prices is also underway, till 27th Mar’23. By 
the end of Dec’22 quarter, 3.6mn shares had been purchased from the market. The shares 
purchased will then be canceled by the company, improving earnings per share.

## REPORT 98
Title: AHL Alert: BAFL - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 13.9 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-08-01T09:11:27.821Z
Tickers: BAFL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 66ab513f6a3cb5cde9804864
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66ab513f6a3cb5cde9804864.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
1 August 2024
2QCY24: Gain on securities fuels earnings
BAFL announced earnings (PAT) today for 2QCY24 at PKR 11.9bn (EPS: PKR 7.59), 
depicting an increase of 53% YoY | 20% QoQ (2QCY23 EPS: PKR 4.97). In addition, the 
profit before tax was also up by 31% YoY | 14% QoQ during the quarter. During 2QCY24, 
the earnings were enhanced by gain on securities. With this, total earnings  for 1HCY24 
increased to PKR 21.9bn, marking a 18% YoY increase. Alongside the result, BAFL 
announced a DPS of PKR 2.0. 
Result Highlights 
•
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 30.6bn during 2QCY24, down by 2% YoY , 
while remained stagnant on QoQ basis. With this, total NII for 1HCY24 went up to PKR 
61.3bn, marking an 4% YoY jump. Interest earned saw a jump of 26% YoY while Interest 
expense saw jump of 39% YoY, in the out-going quarter. 
•
NFI increased by 76% YoY, taking 1HCY24’s total NFI to PKR 20.3bn (+48% YoY). This 
jump in 1HCY24 is primarily due to a huge fee and commission income of PKR 9.5bn 
(+35% YoY) possibly on the back increase in trade volumes. In addition, dividend income 
posted a 94% YoY, clocking-in at PKR 905mn during 1HCY24. Further more, during the 
1HCY24, the bank recorded extraordinary gain on securities of PKR 2.4bn vs a loss on 
securities of PKR 247mn in the SPLY.
•
Provisioning reversal for the bank clocked in at PKR 25mn during 2QCY24 compared to a 
provisioning charge of PKR 4.5bn in SPLY. This takes total provisioning reversal to PKR 
232mn during 1HCY24. 
•
The bank’s OPEX increased by 25% YoY in 1HCY24 clocking-in at PKR 39.5bn 
(2QCY24: PKR 20.3bn, 24% YoY | 6% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 47.4% in 
2QCY24 against 43.1% same period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 47% during 2QCY24 compared to 54% SPLY.
REP-300
Bank Alfalah Limited

HCAR: 2QMY24 EPS clocked-in at @ PKR 4.7 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for 
2QMY23 on 22nd Nov’23, whereby the company posted a Profit After Tax (PAT) of 
PKR 675mn (EPS: PKR 4.7),  vs a loss after tax of PKR 385mn in SPLY. The surge 
in profit was attributed to improved margins, which surged by 750bps on a YoY 
basis. This brought the profit in 6MMY24 to PKR 821mn (EPS: PKR 5.7) as 
compared to a PAT of PKR 273mn (EPS: PKR 1.9) during 6MMY23. 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales declined to PKR 13,956mn during 2QMY24, down  by 29% YoY, owing 
to lower volumetric sales (down 55% YoY) and the impact of import restrictions 
imposed by the SBP, which was partially offset by higher car prices. 
•
Gross margins stood at 10.9% during 2QMY24, as compared to 3.4% in SPLY, 
depicting an improvement of 750bps. This was attributed to augmented car prices 
coupled with lower commodity prices, especially steel. 
•
Other income depicted a jump of 14% YoY during 2QMY24, amid increase in 
short-term investments held by the company as compared to SPLY. 
•
The company booked effective taxation at 55% during 2QMY24.

## REPORT 100
Title: AHL Alert: MCB - Result Review: 3QCY24 EPS arrives at PKR 15.3
Created At: 2024-10-23T10:57:16.663Z
Tickers: MCB
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 6718d68c8f84bab98ccf4d71
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6718d68c8f84bab98ccf4d71.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
23 October 2024
3QCY24: EPS led by higher total income and lower provisioning
MCB reported its 3QCY24 earnings at PKR 18.1bn (EPS: PKR 15.3), down by 8% 
YoY while up by 8% QoQ. For 9MCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 52.7bn, 
marking a 9% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled by a jump in 
total income and decline in provisioning. The bank also announced a cash dividend of 
PKR 9.0/share in 3QCY24 along with the result taking total payout for 9MCY24 to PKR 
27.0/share.
Result Highlights 
▪
Interest earned by the bank settled at PKR 110.8bn during 3QCY24, surging by 10% 
YoY and 4% QoQ while interest expense was up by 19% YoY | 1% QoQ reaching 
PKR 65.9bn. With this, Net Interest Income of the bank clocked in at PKR 44.9bn in 
3QCY24, remaining stagnant on YoY basis while up by 9% QoQ (9MCY24: PKR 
128.5bn, +8% YoY). 
▪
The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 6% YoY during 3QCY24, bringing 
the total for 9MCY24 to PKR 29.2bn, a 19% YoY increase. The increase is primarily 
fueled by a 18% YoY rise in fee income, reaching PKR 18.7bn, and a jump in foreign 
exchange income, which climbed to PKR 7.8bn, up by 22% YoY. Additionally, other 
income saw a 74% YoY surge to PKR 417mn in 9MCY24. However, the gain on 
sale of securities was recorded at PKR 174mn, down by 14% YoY.
▪
A provisioning charge was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 258mn, 
this took the provisioning charge for the 9MCY24 to PKR 818mn compared to a 
provisioning charge of PKR 1.8bn during 9MCY23.
▪
The bank’s OPEX increased by 16% YoY | 8% QoQ in 3QCY24 clocking in at PKR 
19.3bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 35.4% in 3QCY24 against 30.8% SPLY. 
▪
Effective tax rate during 3QCY24 stood at 49.1% compared to 48.8% SPLY.
REP-300
MCB Bank Limited
Result Review: 3QCY24 EPS arrives at PKR 15.3

P a g e  1 | 1 
 
December 23, 2022 
AHL Alert 
Power Generation and Distribution 
The Hub Power Company Limited 
 
As per PSX notice, an encashment notice that was served by China Power Hub Generation Company 
(CPHGC) on November 23rd, 2022, has been withdrawn and the Standby Letter of Credit (SBLC) has been 
extended on mutually agreed terms. We believe that the development is material positive for HUBC as the 
uncertainty with respect to the encashment of SBLC has been put to rest.  
To recall, the Hub Power Company Limited (HUBC) had provided SBLC an aggregate amount of USD 
150mn to guarantee an investment in the form of equity or subordinated debt to satisfy the funding shortfall, 
if any, in CPHGC;  
 
a) To achieve completion of the project to the satisfaction of the lenders; and  
b) To repay all principal, interest, fees or any other amounts that may fall due by CPHGC under the 
finance documents to the finance parties. 
Moreover, shares held by Hub Power Holding Limited (HPHL) in CPHGC were pledged in favor of the 
security trustee in order to secure the company and HPHL’s obligations under the financing documents of 
CPHGC. 
 
 
Standby Letter of Credit (SBLC) Extended   
 
REP- 300 
Rao Aamir Ali
D: +92 21 32461106
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 242
amir.rao@arifhabibltd.com

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Chemicals 
Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited                
November 29, 2022 
  
As per a PSX notice, Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) has entered into an interim 
agreement with Sui Southern Gas Company Limited (SSGC) for the supply of gas till 28th Feb’23. 
The price will be charged to EPCL at the rate on industrial/commercial pricing as notified by the Oil 
and Gas Regulatory Authority (OGRA). 
 
Currently, EPCL is being charged PKR 1,087/mmbtu while the current (Nov’22) rate of RLNG notified 
by OGRA is USD 14.81/mmbu (PKR 3,318/mmbtu).  
 
However, to manage the expected gas shortage in the winter season, the company has already 
planned an annual turnaround during Dec’22 for 30 days. Normally, the company holds one month’s 
inventory (15-20K tons).  
 
On the basis of the aforementioned development, we have calculated the potential financial impact 
on EPCL. As per our calculations, the company will have to increase PVC prices by USD 285/ton to 
fully pas on the impact of higher gas prices.  
 
However, due to lower international PVC prices, we assume EPCL will only pass on half of the 
impact of higher gas prices, translating to a negati ve earnings impact of PKR 0.91/share (assuming 
40,000 tons of PVC production during the RLNG pricing period). Albeit, if the company absorbs the 
full impact of higher gas prices, the profitability will decline by PKR 1.82/share. 
 
 
EPCL to buy RLNG till 28th Feb-2023 
REP- 300 
Rao Aamir Ali

UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 242
amir.rao@arifhabibltd.com

## REPORT 103
Title: AHL Alert: AKBL - Result review: EPS @ PKR 2.56 in 1QCY24
Created At: 2024-04-30T10:20:45.085Z
Tickers: AKBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 6630c5fdb3858eb2c7717f40
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6630c5fdb3858eb2c7717f40.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 April 2024
▪
AKBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 1QCY24 at PKR 3.7bn (EPS: PKR 2.56), depicting 
a decrease on both YoY and QoQ basis by 21% and 46%, respectively (1QCY23 EPS: PKR 
3.25). 
▪
The decrease in overall profitability was mainly caused by increased provisioning compared to 
the reversals recorded in the same period previous year. QoQ, the decline in markup income, 
coupled with higher provisioning, led to the overall earnings decrease.  
▪
In the 1QCY24, the bank recorded a Net Interest Income of PKR 12.9bn, reflecting an 8% rise 
compared to the same period in the previous year, but experiencing a 32% decrease QoQ. 
▪
Interest earned saw a significant surge of 76% compared to the previous year and a 13% 
increase from the previous quarter. However, interest expenses witnessed a whopping 94% 
increase compared to the same period last year, along with a 25% rise QoQ.
▪
During the outgoing quarter, Non-Markup income experienced a 23% YoY growth and a 19% 
QoQ jump. This YoY surge was mainly propelled by a 9% growth in Fee Income, a 13% rise 
in FX income, and a gain on the sale of securities amounting to PKR 274mn, compared to a 
loss of PKR 91mn recorded in the same period last year. However, on a QoQ basis, Fee 
income decreased by 10%, and gains on the sale of securities decreased by 51%.
▪
In the 1QCY24, the bank reported total provisioning of PKR 1.17bn, compared to a 
provisioning reversal of PKR 58mn recorded in the same period last year. Additionally, on a 
QoQ basis, provisioning witnessed a significant increase of 325%.
▪
The bank's operating expenses during 1QCY24 totaled PKR 8.2bn, reflecting a 21% increase 
compared to the same period last year, while experiencing a meagre 1% jump from the 
previous quarter. With this, the Cost/Income ratio stood at 49% for 1QCY24, up from 45% in 
the same period last year.
▪
The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 49% during 1QCY24, showing an increase from 
44% in the same period last year. 
REP-300
Askari Bank Limited
 Result review: EPS @ PKR 2.56 in 1QCY24

## REPORT 104
Title: AHL Alert: AGHA - 1QFY23 EPS @ PKR 0.25
Created At: 2022-10-28T11:11:06Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 64914c737cf4c3f30fbf0378
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=400&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/64914c737cf4c3f30fbf0378.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
P a g e  1 | 2 
 
AHL Alert 
Engineering 
Agha Steel Industries Limited 
October 28, 2022 
 
  
Agha Steel Industries Limited (AGHA) announced the financial result for 1QFY23 today whereby earnings 
arrived at PKR 153mn (EPS: PKR 0.25) against PKR 558mn (EPS: PKR 0.92) last year, down by 73% YoY. 
Whereas bottom-line posted a 33% jump QoQ primarily since in the last quarter State Bank restrictions to 
open LC’s forced the company to rely on domestic scrap, which was more expensive compared to imported 
DRI.  
    Result Highlights 
 
 During 1QFY23, sales showed a decline of 23% YoY as a result of a significant dip in sales of rebars 
and billets (estimated at ~27k tons) given monsoon rains and floods across the country which offset the 
impact of hike in rebars and billets prices. The decline was more noteworthy on a QoQ basis; we believe 
offtake was cut by over 40% from 4QFY22, which countered the impact of higher prices (rebar prices 
estimated at under PKR 175k/ton in 1QFY23). Albeit, we await management guidance.  
 Gross margins in 1QFY23 settled at 21.7% vis-à-vis 23.2% in 1QFY22 given higher energy tariff, PKR 
depreciation and augmented scrap prices. Albeit, the QoQ improvement in margins (4QFY22: 17.6%) is 
owed to procurement of imported DRI, which is much cheaper compared to domestic scrap that the 
company used in the last quarter due to restrictions by the SBP on LC opening. 
 Finance cost settled at PKR 697mn during the quarter under review, up by 55% YoY, on account of 
higher interest rates.  
 The company booked effective taxation at 18% in 1QFY23 vs. 25% in SPLY, despite recognition of 4% 
super tax this year as the company continues to avail some tax credits.

## REPORT 105
Title: Result Review: ABL- 4QCY23 EPS @ PKR 10.72
Created At: 2024-02-13T09:40:05.449Z
Tickers: ABL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 65cb38f5a4708ea5a39af96f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65cb38f5a4708ea5a39af96f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Fueled by surge in NII, ABL posts record profitability in CY23
ABL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY23 at PKR 41.3bn (EPS: PKR 36.07), 
depicting an increase of 94% YoY | 8% QoQ (4QCY22 EPS: PKR 7.51). This jump in overall 
earnings was mainly on the back of growth in total income. Along with the result, ABL 
announced a final dividend of PKR 4/share (CY23: PKR 12/share). 
Result Highlights 
•
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 32.3bn during 4QCY23, increasing 52% 
YoY and 7% QoQ. With this, the total NII for CY23 went up to PKR 112.9bn, marking a 
69% YoY jump. Interest earned saw a jump of 66% during CY23 while interest expense 
was up by 64%.
•
NFI depicted an increase of 73% YoY | 47% QoQ during 4QCY23, taking CY23’s total to 
PKR 25.6bn (+19% YoY). This jump in CY23 is primarily due to higher fee, FX and 
dividend income which were up 32% YoY, 15% YoY and 15% YoY, respectively. 
However, the gain on securities declined by 40% YoY during the same period, clocking-in 
at PKR 845mn. 
•
Provisioning for the bank clocked in at PKR 445mn during 4QCY23 (4QCY22: PKR 
436mn) depicting an increase of 2% on a YoY basis. This takes total provisioning to PKR 
~3bn during CY23 (CY22 reversals: PKR 261mn).
•
The bank’s OPEX increased 6% YoY in 4QCY23 clocking-in at PKR 12.4bn (4QCY22: 
PKR 11.7bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 30.8% in 4QCY23 against 45.3% same 
period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 57% during 4QCY23 compared to 50% last quarter. With 
this, the effective tax for CY23 stood at 52% (CY22: 55%).
1

## REPORT 106
Title: AHL Alert - ILP EPS arrives at PKR 6.66/share in 9MFY26
Created At: 2026-04-29T12:02:55.177Z
Tickers: ILP
Sectors: 0829
Item ID: 69f1f36f9730304360ce3418
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1f36f9730304360ce3418.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 29-Apr-2026
Textile Composite: Interloop Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 6.66/share in 9MFY26
9MFY26: EPS arrives at PKR 6.66/share
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced its financial results for 9MFY26, wherein the
company reported a 245% YoY jump in Profit after Tax (PAT) to PKR 9,342mn,
compared to PKR 2,710mn in 9MFY25. This translates into an EPS of PKR 6.66
versus PKR 1.93 in the SPLY. The recovery in earnings was driven by improved gross
margins, significantly higher other income, and a decline in finance costs.
Result Highlights

During 9MFY26, net revenue reached PKR 127,291mn, up 2% YoY, while 3QFY26
sales stood at PKR 39,872mn, declining 4% YoY. Overall, topline growth remained
modest.

Gross profit increased 24% YoY to PKR 30,605mn in 9MFY26, with gross margins
expanding to 24.0% from 19.6% in SPLY, supported by lower yarn prices and a
reduction in losses within the apparel segment. On a quarterly basis, 3QFY26
gross margins improved to 25.0%, compared to 20.1% in 3QFY25.

Distribution expenses declined 19% YoY to PKR 3,997mn in 9MFY26, while
administrative expenses rose 7% YoY to PKR 7,653mn.

Meanwhile, other income surged to PKR 2,407mn (5x YoY) from PKR 467mn in
SPLY, primarily driven by forward bookings (derivative-related income), which
provided a meaningful uplift to overall profitability.

Finance cost declined 37% YoY to PKR 4,802mn in 9MFY26 (SPLY: PKR
7,572mn), further supporting the earnings recovery.

As a result, PAT surged 245% YoY to PKR 9,342mn in 9MFY26, while on a
quarterly basis, PAT rose 130% YoY to PKR 3,074mn in 3QFY26.

The company reported an effective tax rate of 39.2% in 9MFY26, compared to
39.6% in 9MFY25.

The company's cumulative DPS stands at PKR 2.00/share for 9MFY26, with no
dividend declared in 3QFY26, implying a payout ratio of 30.0% for 9MFY26.
Leena Abid
UAN: (021) 3828 0264
leena.abid@arifhabibltd.com

▪
BOP announced earnings (PAT) today for 1QCY24 at PKR 1.7bn (EPS: PKR 0.50), depicting 
an increase of +44% YoY | -69% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 1.61). 
▪
On a YoY basis, higher markup and non-markup income contributed to an increase in overall 
profitability. However, on a QoQ basis, provisioning reversals and lower taxation supported 
the earnings.  
▪
During the 1QCY24, the bank recorded a Net Interest Income of PKR 8.6bn, reflecting an 
10% increase from the corresponding period in the previous year but a 33% decrease from 
the preceding quarter. 
▪
The interest earned surged by 66% compared to the previous year but decreased by 18% 
from the previous quarter. However, interest expenses witnessed a substantial 76% increase 
compared to the same period last year, while down 16% QoQ.
▪
In the outgoing quarter, a major contributor to the bank's improved profitability compared to 
the previous year was a 34% rise in Non-Funded Income. This increase was mainly propelled 
by a 21% YoY growth in Fee Income and gains from the sale of securities amounting to PKR 
702mn, significantly higher than the PKR 60mn gain recorded in the corresponding period last 
year. However, dividend income and FX income experienced declines of 16% and 73% YoY, 
respectively.
▪
In the 1QCY24, the bank reported a reversal in provisioning of PKR 1.8bn, compared to a 
provisioning charge of PKR 10mn recorded in the same period last year. Moreover, on a QoQ 
basis, the provisioning reversal increased by 389%.
▪
The operating expense of the bank clocked in at PKR 10.5bn in 1QCY24, up 25% YoY | 6% 
QoQ. With this, the Cost/Income ratio stood at 86% for 1QCY24, up from 80% in the same 
period last year.
▪
The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 52% during 1QCY24, showing an increase from 
43% in the same period last year. 
REP-300
The Bank of Punjab
 Result review: EPS @ PKR 0.50 in 1QCY24

Title: AHL Alert: LCI - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 54.58
Created At: 2024-01-25T06:29:44.437Z
Tickers: LCI
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 65b1ffd8ebc5f774fd99c10b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65b1ffd8ebc5f774fd99c10b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
LCI: 2QFY24 earnings at PKR 27.10/share, up by 104% YoY
Lucky Core Industries Limited announced its 1HFY24 financial result today where the 
company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 5.0bn (EPS: PKR 54.58), compared to 
PAT of PKR 3.0bn (EPS: PKR 32.76) during SPLY. The rise in earnings is witnessed 
due to higher gross margins and a 9x YoY rise in other income. Along with the result, 
the company also announced a cash dividend of PKR 27/share.
Result Highlights 
•
During 1HFY24, net sales surged up 21% YoY to PKR 60.1bn due to higher sales 
across all segments, supporting the overall jump. During 2QFY24, sales also 
increased by 23% YoY. 
•
Gross margins went up by 324bps YoY to 21.4% during 1HFY24. The rise in gross 
margins was led by lower coal prices (-59% YoY), we view.
•
During 2QFY24, LCI booked an exchange gain of PKR 98mn amid a recovery in PKR 
against the greenback.
•
The other income of the company increased by 9x YoY to PKR 1,810mn during 
1HFY24, the rise in earnings is witnessed due to the return on short-term investment.
•
Finance costs of the company went up by 89% YoY to PKR 1,989mn during 1HFY24 
due to augmented short-term borrowing along with higher interest rates.
Recommendation 
•
We have a BUY call on the scrip with a Dec’24 target price of PKR 937.1/share.

## REPORT 110
Title: AHL Alert - AIRLINK 1HFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 7.72/share
Created At: 2026-02-25T08:49:38.626Z
Tickers: AIRLINK
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 699eb7a2d33a93d06bcd822b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699eb7a2d33a93d06bcd822b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 25-Feb-2026 
Air Link Communication Limited
Result Review: 1HFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 7.72/share
REP-300
Result Highlights
•
Air Link Communication Limited (AIRLINK) announced the financial result for
1HFY26 today, whereby the company has posted a PAT of PKR 3,052mn (EPS:
PKR 7.72) compared to profit of PKR 2,319m (EPS: PKR 5.87) in 1HFY25. On a
quarterly basis, the company recorded PAT of PKR 1,469mn (EPS:PKR 3.72) in
2QFY26 down by 1% YoY. The contraction in profitability was primarily
attributable to lower volumetric sales leading to reduced net revenue and higher
finance costs.
•
Net sales for 1HFY26 declined by 15% YoY to PKR 48,771mn. On a quarterly
basis, the topline in 2QFY26 contracted by 31% YoY to PKR 24,369mn. The
reduction in revenue was primarily driven by a contraction in volumetric sales.
Locally manufactured device volumes stood at 7.43mn units in 2QFY26, down
14% YoY, amid low demand for local manufacturing devices due to higher tax
rates.
•
Gross profit margin rose to 14.9% in 1HFY26 (9.5% SPLY) and 16% in 2QFY26
(9.2% prior year) as the company’s existing inventory, purchased at lower costs,
was sold at higher prices. Furthermore, higher sales of TVs, which have better
margins, also contributed to the increase in margins as per our channel checks.
•
The other income arrived at PKR 113mn in 2QFY26 compared to other income
of PKR 133mn in SPLY due to decline in interest rates.
•
The finance costs increased by 9% YoY to PKR 1,122m in 2QFY26 due to
higher short-term borrowings to PKR 29,324mn in Dec’25.
•
The company reported an effective tax rate of 27.1% in 2QFY26 compared to
18.8% in SPLY.
•
We have a “BUY” stance on AIRLINK, the stock is trading at FY26 and FY27
multiple of 8.9 and 6.8x.

## REPORT 111
Title: AHL Alert: HUBC - Result Review: Company resumes dividends with a PKR 5 payout
Created At: 2025-02-26T07:18:06.86Z
Tickers: HUBC
Sectors: 0824
Item ID: 67bec02e881e05effa4a02be
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67bec02e881e05effa4a02be.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
26 February 2025
2QFY25: Earnings arrived at PKR 3.25/share
The Hub Power Company Ltd (HUBC) announced its financial results for 1HFY25 today,
reporting a profit of PKR 23.3bn (EPS: PKR 17.99), down by 28% YoY, compared to PKR
32.4bn (EPS: PKR 24.95) in SPLY. In 2QFY25, the company posted earnings of PKR
4.2bn (EPS: PKR 3.25), reflecting a 72% YoY decrease and a 78% QoQ drop. Along with
the results, HUBC declared a cash dividend of PKR 5.00/share.
Result Highlights
•
In 1HFY25, the company reported sales of PKR 47.5bn, reflecting a 25% YoY decline,
largely due to the termination of the PPA for the Hub base plant. In 2QFY25, sales
also saw a significant drop, falling by 48% YoY and 52% QoQ, primarily driven by the
same factor.
•
The company incurred a distribution expense of PKR 496mn in 1HFY25, compared to
no such expense in the previous year.
•
Other expense of PKR 3.6bn was recorded in 2QFY25, marking a 22x increase
compared to the previous year. This increase in other expenses is attributed to the
provision for LPS disallowance related to the Narowal plant, in our view.
•
The share of profit from associates declined by 11% YoY, amounting to PKR 20.2bn in
1HFY25. This decline is mainly due to lower income from Prime International, in our
view.
•
Finance costs decreased by 32% YoY to PKR 9.6bn in 1HFY25, largely due to a
reduction in interest rates. In 2QFY25, finance costs also saw a decline of 41% YoY
and 25% QoQ, due to same reasons.
•
The company reported an effective tax rate of 38.7% in 2QFY25, up from 15.7%
2QFY24. Further clarification is required regarding the higher effective tax rate.
REP-300
The Hub Power Company Limited
Result Review: Company resumes dividends with a PKR 5 payout

## REPORT 112
Title: AHL Alert: FFC - Result Review: CY23 EPS @ PKR 23.32
Created At: 2024-01-26T11:44:50.928Z
Tickers: FFC
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 65b39b32ebc5f774fdcfd4a6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65b39b32ebc5f774fdcfd4a6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability climbs up by 43% YoY to PKR 5.86/share in 4QCY23
Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited (FFC) disclosed its financial result today where the 
company posted net profit of PKR 29,673mn (EPS: PKR 23.32) during CY23 against 
PKR 20,050mn (EPS: PKR 15.76) in CY22, up by 48% YoY. On the other hand, the 
profitability in 4QCY23 swelled up by 43% YoY to PKR 7,459mn (EPS: PKR 5.86). 
Alongside the result, FFC announced a final cash dividend of PKR 4.10/share in 
4QCY23 (PKR 15.49/share in CY23).
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales witnessed a jump of 46% YoY during CY23, settling at PKR 159,472mn on 
account of a jump in urea and DAP offtake by 3% and 51% YoY, respectively. On a 
quarterly basis, the topline during 4QCY23 increased by 44% YoY amid an increase 
in DAP sales by 109% YoY coupled with 64% YoY higher urea prices. Meanwhile, 
urea sales declined by 5% YoY in 4QCY23.
•
The gross margins during CY23 arrived at 35.78% compared to 32.34% in SPLY on 
account of higher urea offtake and urea prices. Whereas, gross margins in 4QCY23 
arrived at 44.66% (up by 1,250bps YoY) owing to a hike in urea prices.  
•
The company booked a loss allowance on subsidy receivable by PKR 2,900mn in 
CY23, depicting a jump of 4x YoY. Major jump in loss allowance was recorded in 
4QCY23, settling at PKR 2,300mn (up by 7x YoY). 
•
Financial charges increased by 16% YoY, clocking in at PKR 5,624mn in CY23 amid 
higher interest rates. 
•
Other income ascended by 18% YoY, clocking in at PKR 17,097mn in CY23, given i) 
higher dividend income from associates, and subsidiaries and ii) increase in income 
from cash and cash balances. 
•
The company booked effective taxation at 47% in 4QCY23 vis-à-vis 30% in 4QCY22.
1

Other information 
•
Phase II of Nodal Compression project is about to commence, which will have a 
capital outlay of USD 100mn.
•
Urea production climbed up by 5% YoY in CY23, settling at 2,521K tons.
•
In order to ease out availability of urea and resolve pricing issues, FFC and other 
fertilizer companies have devised a plan with government to import urea and 
distribute it during CY24. 
•
During CY23, the FFC sold urea at PKR 200/bag -PKR 500/bag lower compared to 
other urea manufacturers. Towards the end of CY23, the urea prices stood at PKR 
3,400/bag.

Title: AHL Alert: SYS - Result Review: Company recorded PAT of PKR 2.5bn during 1QCY25
Created At: 2025-04-28T13:33:45.674Z
Tickers: SYS
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 680f83b983dc41f4304b4aef

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
Muhammad Abrar
D: +92 21 38280256
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 255
E: muhammad.abrar@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 28-Apr-2025 
Technology: Systems Limited
Result Review: Company recorded PAT of PKR 2.5bn during 1QCY25

Systems Limited (SYS) announced the financial result for 1QCY25 whereby the
company posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 2,502mn (EPS: PKR 8.54) in
contrast to PKR 1,571mn (EPS: 5.39) during 1QCY24, up by 59% The increase
was primarily driven by higher technology services exports and improved gross
margins.

Net sales in 1QCY25 clocked in at PKR 18,080mn, up by 19% YoY which is on
account of 29.4%, 24.7%, and 6.2% YoY growth in telecommunications services,
technology-related solutions, and, Banking Financial Services & Insurance (BFSI),
respectively.

Gross margins in 1QCY25 improved to 25.2%, compared to 23.7% in 1QCY24,
driven by higher productivity, improved billing efficiency, and cost optimization
initiatives during the quarter.

Administrative and Distribution expenses augmented by 27% YoY, which is due to
inflationary pressure.

The company booked an expense of PKR 49mn in 1QCY25, dropping by 11% YoY.

Finance costs dropped by 40% YoY, settling at PKR 90mn during 1QCY25,
primarily due to a decline in interest rates.

The company booked effective taxation at 7% in 1QCY25 vis-à-vis 11% in 1QCY24.
Recommendation

We maintain our Buy recommendation on Systems Limited, with a Dec'25 target
price of PKR 661/share. The stock is currently trading at forward P/E multiples of
14.7x for CY25 and 13.0x for CY26.

## REPORT 114
Title: AHL Alert: LUCK - Result Review: 1HFY25 EPS @ PKR 47.24
Created At: 2025-01-30T12:25:14.007Z
Tickers: LUCK
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 679b6faa6ae6aa2b5301d5b6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/679b6faa6ae6aa2b5301d5b6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 January 2025
2QFY25: Earnings arrived at PKR 24.84/share
Lucky Cement Limited (LUCK) announced its financial result for 1HFY25 today, posting
an unconsolidated earnings of PKR 13,840mn (EPS: PKR 47.24), showcasing a increase
of 1% YoY as compared to earnings of PKR 13,710mn (EPS:PKR 46.79) in SPLY. During
2QFY25 earnings arrived at PKR 7,278mn (EPS: PKR 24.84) against PKR 6,781mn
(EPS: PKR 23.14), an increase of 7% YoY I 11% QoQ.
Result Highlights
•
During 1HFY25, sales reached PKR 64.3bn, reflecting a 7% YoY growth from PKR
59.9bn in the corresponding period last year, driven by higher dispatches, which rose
by 9% YoY. In 2QFY25, topline stood at PKR 34.5bn, marking a 13% YoY increase
from PKR 30.5bn in SPLY due 16% YoY rise in dispatches.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY25 stood at 34.2%, down from 36.4% in SPLY, mainly due to
higher energy prices and an increased share of exports in total dispatches. In
2QFY25, gross margins declined by 77bps YoY to 35.3% for the same reasons.
•
Selling and distribution expenses surged by 31% YoY in 1HFY25, reaching PKR
4,906mn, which we attribute to the axle load factor. In 2QFY25, these expenses
increased by 27% YoY and 9% QoQ.
•
Other income grew by 21% YoY to PKR 7,479mn in 1HFY25, primarily driven by
higher short-term investments, which reached PKR 49.9bn as of Dec’24. In 2QFY25,
other income also rose by 16% YoY for the same reason.
•
Finance costs grew by 3% YoY in 1HFY25, amounting to PKR 827mn, driven by
higher borrowings which has increased to PKR 13.5bn. In 2QFY25, finance costs
increased by 10% YoY and 30% QoQ due to the same factor.
•
The company booked effective taxation of 35.5% in 2QFY25 as compared to 33.0%
during SPLY.
REP-300
Lucky Cement Limited
Result Review: 1HFY25 EPS @ PKR 47.24

## REPORT 115
Title: AHL Alert: EFERT - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 7.06 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-07-31T05:06:18.768Z
Tickers: EFERT
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 66a9c64a6a3cb5cde93496ea
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a9c64a6a3cb5cde93496ea.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
31 July 2024
2QCY24: Bottom-line witnessed a growth of 57% YoY
Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) today unveiled the financial result for 1HCY24 posting a 
consolidated profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 9,426mn (EPS: PKR 7.06) against PKR 5,464mn 
(EPS: PKR 4.09) in 1HCY23, up by 73% YoY. Whereas, consolidated earnings in 2QCY24 
clocked in at PKR 1,666mn (EPS: PKR 1.25), augmenting by 57% YoY. Alongside the result, 
the EFERT announced a cash dividend of PKR 3.00/share (PKR 11.00/share in 1HCY24). 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales in 1HCY24 clocked in at PKR 113,204mn, up by 37% YoY which is on account of 
62% and 19% YoY hike in urea and DAP price tagged with 54% YoY jump in DAP offtake. 
On a quarterly basis, the topline during 2QCY24 witnessed an uptick of 3% YoY, settling at 
PKR 39,421mn, which is due to 59% and 15% YoY higher urea and DAP prices, respectively 
coupled with 16% YoY jump in DAP sales. Meanwhile, urea offtake contracted by 36% YoY 
due to 58 days maintenance at EnVen plant.
•
Gross margins in 1HCY24 arrived at 21.49% vis-à-vis 26.95% in 1HCY23. Whereas, gross 
margins in 2QCY24 arrived at 18.10% (down by 1,167bps YoY) owed to lower urea offtake.
•
Other income swelled up by 75% YoY settling at PKR 2,032mn in 1HCY24 given higher 
income from cash and cash balances. Meanwhile, in 2QCY24, the other income came out to 
be PKR 745mn, up by 67% YoY due to the aforementioned reason. 
•
Finance cost climbed up by 22% YoY, clocking in at PKR 1,386mn during 1HCY24 owed to 
higher interest rates. Whereas, the finance cost in 2QCY24 swelled up by 74% YoY, 
reaching PKR 1,225mn.    
•
The company booked effective taxation at 35% in 2QCY24 vis-à-vis 85% in 2QCY23. To 
recall, during 2QCY23 an additional 6% super tax was levied on the profit before tax of CY22 
and 1HCY23, respectively.
REP-300
Engro Fertilizers Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 7.06 in 1HCY24

## REPORT 116
Title: AHL Alert: SAZEW - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 131.29 in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-23T12:35:54.182Z
Tickers: SAZEW
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 66f160aa0cbf3cea06d4fc67
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66f160aa0cbf3cea06d4fc67.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
23 September 2024
FY24: Profitability climbed up by 8x YoY to PKR 131.29/share
Sazgar Engineering Works Limited (SAZEW) announced its financial result for FY24 today,
posting a PAT of PKR 7,936mn (EPS: PKR 131.29) compared to PKR 995mn (EPS: PKR 16.46)
in SPLY, up by 8x YoY. During 4QFY24 earnings clocked in at PKR 3,487mn (EPS: PKR 57.69),
showcasing a significant jump of 7.4x YoY. Final cash dividend of 12.0/share was announced,
bringing the total annual payout at PKR 20.0/share.
Result Highlights
•
Topline during FY24 surged by 3.2x YoY clocking at PKR 57,642mn. This growth was driven
by a 3x increase in Haval sales YoY, totaling 5,374 units, as well as a 1.6x YoY rise in three-
wheeler sales to 15,014 units. During 4QFY24 net sales augmented by 4.1x YoY to settle at
PKR 23,058mn in contrast to PKR 5,581mn in SPLY.
•
For FY24, gross margins improved to 27%, up from 14% in the same period last year. This
increase was primarily driven by a concessionary customs duties and GST on Haval vehicles,
coupled with stability in PKR parity. During 4QFY24, gross margins were recorded at 29%
compared to 23% recorded in SPLY.
•
Other income surged massively by 27x YoY in FY24 at PKR 832mn, due to higher income
from cash and cash equivalents. In 4QFY24, the other income arrived at PKR 481mn,
augmented by 38x YoY.
•
Other expenses rose significantly by 9.8x to PKR 1,000mn, while on the quarterly basis it went
up by 9.4x to PKR 456mn.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 39.6% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 35.0% in 4QFY23.
Other information
•
The company plans to invest PKR 4.5bn in its expansion, to be financed through internal cash
reserves. This investment includes the construction of new warehousing facilities, expansion of
the paint shop, installation of a solar system, and the establishment of manufacturing facilities
for the local assembly of new energy vehicles, pending approval from the relevant regulatory
authorities.
REP-300
Sazgar Engineering Works Limited

## REPORT 117
Title: SNGP - Result Review: FY23 EPS up 2% YoY / 1QFY24 EPS up 44% YoY
Created At: 2024-07-26T13:15:47.263Z
Tickers: SNGP
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 66a3a1838db87d2f86117e73
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a3a1838db87d2f86117e73.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
26 July 2024
Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited (SNGP) announced the financial result for
FY23 and 1QFY24 today. SNGP during FY23 posted a profit after tax (PAT) of
PKR 10,564mn (EPS: PKR 16.66), up by 2% YoY. On a quarterly basis (4QFY23),
the earnings arrived at PKR 2,165mn (PKR 3.41), swelling up by 2x YoY.
Additionally, the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR 4.50/share
(PKR 4.00/share in FY22). Moreover, in 1QFY24 the bottom-line settled at PKR
3,706mn (EPS: PKR 5.84), up by 44% YoY.
Result Highlights
•
In FY23, net sales climbed up by 13% YoY, owing to revision in consumer gas
tariff by OGRA. However, RLNG prices witnessed a 5% YoY decline in lieu of
decline in international Brent oil price (down by 4% YoY). In addition to this,
RLNG imports declined by 16% YoY in FY23 while gas production reduced by
4% YoY. Whereas, during 1QFY24, the company’s topline increased by 13%
YoY amid 12% YoY higher RLNG imports coupled with hike in consumer gas
rates. Whereas, local gas production tumbled by 2% YoY.
•
The operating profit arrived at PKR 136.3bn in FY23, depicting a surge of 87%
YoY given the reduction charge of unaccounted-for gas losses (UFG) due to
effective UFG control in place, coupled with a fall in domestic volumes which
absorbed the impact of a higher weighted average cost of gas. On the other
hand, operating profit during 1QFY24 depicted an massive jump of 95% YoY on
account of increase in Return on Assets (ROA).
•
Finance costs swelled up by 2x YoY in FY23 to PKR 120.5bn owed to higher
interest rates. Similarly, finance costs in 1QFY24 went up by 2x YoY.
•
The company booked effective taxation remained unchanged at 33% during
FY23. Whereas, SNGP’s effective taxation arrived at 39% in 1QFY24 vis-à-vis
40% in 1QFY23.
REP-300
Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited
Result Review: FY23 EPS up 2% YoY / 1QFY24 EPS up 44% YoY

## REPORT 118
Title: AHL Alert: Result review: EPCL- Loss of PKR 0.99/share in 1QCY24
Created At: 2024-04-22T06:41:35.096Z
Tickers: EPCL
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 6626069f0270a7c4045bf1b6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6626069f0270a7c4045bf1b6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
22 April 2024
Higher gas prices and a decline in PVC margins result in a loss after tax
Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) announced its 1QCY24 financial result today where the
company posted a loss of PKR 901mn (LPS: PKR 0.99) compared to PKR 1,183mn (EPS: PKR 1.30)
and PKR 3,951mn (EPS: PKR 3.90) in 1QCY23 and 4QCY24, respectively.
Result Highlights

Net sales during 1QCY24 reduced by 8% YoY to PKR 16,572mn amid decline in PVC volumes.
On a QoQ basis, the topline tumbled by 14% YoY due to the aforementioned reason.

The gross profit margin in 1QCY24 arrived at 6.4% compared to 20.0% during SPLY due to higher
gas prices and subdued PVC margins.

The finance cost climbed up by 42% YoY, arriving at PKR 1,668mn during 1QCY24 amid higher
interest rates and a jump in short-term borrowings. On a sequential basis, the finance cost surged
by 7x YoY, which is attributable to higher short-term borrowings.

Other income plummeted by 61% YoY to PKR 175mn during 1QCY24 due to lower short-term
investments.

The other expenses plunged by 97% YoY to PKR 23mn in 1QCY24, which is mainly on the back
of absence on exchange loss in the quarter.

The company booked a tax reversal of PKR 371mn in 1QCY24 compared to taxation of PKR
489mn in 1QCY23.

Source (s): Company Financials, AHL Research
FY25: EPS at PKR 2.88, DPS of PKR 7.00/share announced
Kot Addu Power Company (KAPCO) announced its financial results for FY25, wherein the
EPS arrived at PKR 2.88 (-41% QoQ) for FY25 with the major contribution from other
income from conventional and Islamic mutual funds with different AMCs, as KAPCO's PPA
expired in Jun-21. However, it entered into a 3-year Tri-Partite PPA with CPPA-G and
NGPCL, effective from 04-Jun-25, with only a one-month impact for this quarter.
Result Highlights

In FY25, revenue reached PKR 1.6bn, entirely recognized in 4QFY25 (vs. PKR Nil in
the prior quarter and SPLY), with no PPA in place.

The company paid dividend of PKR 7.00/share in FY25.

The company earnings were primarily driven by interest income as well as penal income
(PKR 8.03/share in FY25).

Finance costs amounted to PKR 248mn in FY25 (-52% YoY), as the borrowings have
been significantly eliminated (PKR Nil in Jun’25 vs PKR 9.9bn in Jun’24), likely funded
by the receipt of trade debts (PKR 1.8bn in Jun’25 vs PKR 10.9bn in Jun’24).

KAPCO resumed operations in 4QFY25 after an 18-month gap, with 15.1% utilization of
its switchyard (SY) LSFO plant. The 500 MW SY plant will operate under a hybrid take-
and-pay model, ensuring fixed cost coverage and a 25% minimum ROE, with an upside
tied to additional generation. Additionally, KAPCO, along with Fauji Foundation, has
issued a PAI to acquire joint control of ACPL, with each expected to hold 42.03%.
Other Information

It has also bid for two solar projects (150MW & 120MW), pending NEPRA approval.
These investments may limit future payouts.
