## REPORT 120
Title: AHL Alert - POL EPS settled at PKR 27.5 in 3QFY26
Created At: 2026-04-30T06:11:55.944Z
Tickers: POL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 69f2f2ab973030436020431a
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f2f2ab973030436020431a.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 April 2026
Profitability increased by 18% YoY to PKR 27.5/share in 3QFY26
Pakistan Oilfields Limited announced its 3QFY26 results, posting a profit after tax of PKR
7,797mn (EPS: PKR 27.5), up 18% YoY, driven by higher revenue and a lower effective tax
rate (ETR). Cumulatively, 9MFY26 net profit stood at PKR 19,516mn (EPS: PKR 68.75),
reflecting a 16% YoY increase. The company declared no dividend, in line with historical
trends.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 3QFY26 increased 5% YoY to PKR 15,259mn (vs. PKR 14,551mn SPLY),
driven by slightly higher oil prices (up 1% YoY) and increased gas production. Among
key oil fields, output declined YoY at Adhi (down 11%), Jhandial (down 79%), and
Mamikhel South (down 8%), partially offset by significant gains at Makori Deep (up 76x)
and Makori East (up 3%). On the gas side, production growth remained strong, primarily
driven by Makori Deep (up 70x), along with incremental volumes from the newly added
Razgir field. In contrast, gas output from Jhandial declined by 70% YoY.
•
Exploration costs declined 53% YoY to PKR 4,574mn in 9MFY26, primarily due to the
absence of dry well expenses, compared to a dry well at Balkassar in the same period
last year.
•
Other income declined 5% YoY to PKR 2,691mn in 3QFY26, mainly due to lower interest
rates and a slight reduction in cash balances. Cash and cash equivalents stood at PKR
101bn (PKR 359/share) versus PKR 103bn (PKR 361/share) in the same period last
year. Consequently, 9MFY26 other income fell 39% YoY to PKR 6,812mn, reflecting the
same underlying trends.
•
POL’s earnings were supported by a lower ETR of 21.6% in 3QFY26 vs 29.8% in
3QFY25.
We maintain a Buy stance on POL, with a Dec’26 target price of PKR 633/share. The stock
is currently trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 8.7x and 10.2x, respectively.
REP-300
E&P’s: Pakistan Oilfields Limited

## REPORT 121
Title: AHL Alert: ILP EPS arrived at PKR 4.47/share in 1HFY26
Created At: 2026-02-04T10:44:25.366Z
Tickers: ILP
Sectors: 0829
Item ID: 698323092c2a6a5d49ee6b1c
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 04-Feb-2026
Textile Composite: Interloop Limited
Result Review: EPS arrived at PKR 4.47/share in 1HFY26
1HFY26: EPS at PKR 4.47, DPS of PKR 2.00/share announced
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced its financial results for 1HFY26, wherein the
company reported a 357% YoY jump in Profit after Tax (PAT) to PKR 6,268mn,
compared to PKR 1,373mn in 1HFY25. This translates into an EPS of PKR 4.47
versus PKR 0.98 in the SPLY. The sharp recovery in earnings was driven by improved
gross margins, significantly higher other income, decline in finance costs, and a lower
effective tax rate.
Result Highlights

During 1HFY26, net revenue reached PKR 87,418mn, up 4% YoY, while 2QFY26
sales stood at PKR 43,644mn, reflecting a 3% YoY increase. Overall, topline
growth remained modest.

Gross profit increased 27% YoY to PKR 20,618mn in 1HFY26, with gross margins
expanding to 23.6% from 19.4% in SPLY, supported by lower yarn prices and, as
per sources, a significant reduction in losses within the apparel segment. On a
quarterly basis, 2QFY26 gross margins improved to 23.9%, compared to 20.1% in
2QFY25.

Distribution expenses declined 22% YoY to PKR 2,706mn in 1HFY26, while
administrative expenses rose 9% YoY to PKR 5,037mn. Meanwhile, other income
surged 281% YoY to PKR 1,713mn, primarily driven by forward bookings
(derivative-related
income),
which
provided
a
meaningful
uplift
to
overall
profitability.

As a result, Profit after Tax surged 357% YoY to PKR 6,268mn in 1HFY26, while
on a quarterly basis, PAT rose 202% YoY to PKR 3,471mn in 2QFY26.

The company reported an effective tax rate of 39.6% in 1HFY26, compared to
45.5% in 1HFY25.

The company announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 2.0 per share, implying
a payout ratio of 44.7% for 1HFY26 (80.8% for 2QFY26).
Leena Abid

leena.abid@arifhabibltd.com

## REPORT 122
Title: AHL Alert: ENGRO - Result Review: CY23 EPS @ PKR 39.17 (After re-measurement)
Created At: 2024-02-26T10:35:15.602Z
Tickers: ENGRO
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 65dc6963c954d41aba01e35f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65dc6963c954d41aba01e35f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability declines by 14% YoY during CY23
Engro Corporation Limited (ENGRO) announced its CY23 financial result with a
consolidated profit after tax of PKR 21,020mn (EPS: PKR 39.17), depicting a decline of
14% YoY. If we exclude re-measurement loss, the earnings clocks in at PKR 34,316mn
(EPS: PKR 63.95), witnessing a surge of 41% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the company
booked consolidated loss of PKR 1,727mn (LPS: PKR 3.22) compared to net profit of PKR
8,901mn (EPS: PKR 16.59) in SPLY, given company booked a loss on Thermal Assets re-
measurement of PKR 29,950mn (ENGRO’s share: PKR 13,295mn or PKR 24.78/share). It
is pertinent to note that this loss on re-measurement will not result in cash outflow and
when the transaction of sale of Thermal assets concludes, the difference between the
agreed sale price and the cost of investment (gain on divestment) will be reflected in
standalone accounts of ENGRO. Alongside the result, the company announced a final
cash dividend of PKR 2.00/share in 4QCY23 (PKR 48.00/share in CY23).
Result Highlights
•
On the fertilizer business front, EFERT’s bottom-line clocked in at PKR 11,147mn
(EPS: PKR 8.35), surging by 74% YoY during 4QCY23 owed to i) growth in urea and
DAP sales by 45% and 15% YoY, respectively, ii) hike in urea price, and iii) decline in
finance cost amid reduction in short term borrowings.
•
Net profit of Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) in 4QCY23 arrived at PKR
3,760mn (EPS: PKR 4.14), swelling up by 59% YoY on account of i) higher gross
margins and ii) lower finance cost.
•
Engro Powergen Qadirpur Pakistan Limited (EPQL) posted a Profit after Tax of PKR
69mn (EPS: PKR 0.21) in 4QCY23, down by 27% YoY which is owed to lower period
weighting factor applied to capacity payments.

Title: AHL Alert: OGDC - Result Review: Earnings clocked in at PKR 30.13 in 9MFY25
Created At: 2025-04-30T07:23:07.482Z
Tickers: OGDC
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 6811cfdb87a2884a3235446f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6811cfdb87a2884a3235446f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 30-Apr-2025 
E&P: Oil & Gas Development Company Limited
Result Review: Earnings clocked in at PKR 30.13 in 9MFY25

Oil & Gas Development Company Limited (OGDC) announced its financial result
today, posting earnings of PKR 129,606mn (EPS: PKR 30.13) during 9MFY25, down
24% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the company’s profitability arrived at PKR 47,149mn
(EPS: PKR 10.96), down 1% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced a
cash dividend of PKR 3.00/share (PKR 10.05/share in 1HFY25).

Topline in 9MFY25 contracted by 11% YoY, clocking in at PKR 310,907mn compared
to PKR 348,164mn in SPLY, owing to i) 4% and 8% YoY reduction in oil and gas
production, respectively, ii) 10% YoY decline in oil prices, and iii) depreciation of the
USD against the PKR. During 3QFY25, the net sales decreased by 7% YoY, settling at
PKR 104,484mn, amid i) 4% and 13% YoY reduction in oil and gas production,
respectively, and ii) 6% YoY fall in oil prices.

The exploration costs surged by 73% YoY to PKR 17,356mn in 9MFY25. Whereas, the
exploration costs during 3QFY25 swelled by 68% YoY, which is on account of dry well
(Chak 202-2) along with higher seismic activity.

Other income clocked in at PKR 64,692mn in 9MFY25, up 45% YoY. In 3QFY25, the
other income arrived at PKR 18,110mn, depicting a jump 5% YoY owing to income
from cash and cash equivalent.

The cash and cash equivalent arrived at PKR 203bn in Mar’25 compared to PKR
263bn in Dec’24.

The company’s trade debts in Mar’25 stood at PKR 620bn vs. PKR 603bn in Dec’24.

The company booked effective taxation at 30% during 3QFY25 vis-à-vis 41% in
3QFY24.

The pay-out ratio has reached 33.4% in 9MFY25 compared 15.3% in SPLY.

We have a “BUY” stance on the scrip, with Dec’25 target price of PKR 286.6/share.
The scrip is trading at forward P/E of 5.1x for FY26E

## REPORT 124
Title: AHL Alert: PSO - Result Review: EPS arrived at PKR 8.46 in 1QFY25
Created At: 2024-10-25T14:39:16.195Z
Tickers: PSO
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 671bad948f84bab98c93ecbe
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671bad948f84bab98c93ecbe.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 October 2024
Profitability dents on YoY basis amid inventory loss
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited (PSO) announced the financial result for 1QFY25,
whereby the company has posted a net profit of PKR 3,971mn (EPS: PKR 8.46)
compared to PKR 21,888mn (EPS: PKR 46.62) in 1QFY24, down 82% YoY. Whereas,
the bottom-line surged by 61% on QoQ basis.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales during 1QFY25 contracted by 14% YoY, settling at PKR 787,589mn, which
comes on the back of i) lower average retail price of petroleum products, and ii) 15%
YoY reduction in the sales volume of petroleum products (MS, HSD, and FO
dispatches down 15%, 19%, and 22% YoY, respectively). Although we await disclosure
of detailed accounts, we estimate the revenue contribution from RLNG segment to be
PKR 302bn (up 18% YoY) given higher DES price (up by 4% YoY) coupled with
handling of 30 cargos in 1QFY25 versus 26 cargos in SPLY. On a sequential basis, the
topline tumbled by 13% QoQ owing to 13% QoQ decline in overall petroleum sales
volumes.
•
Gross margins of the company arrived at 3.3% in 1QFY25 compared to 6.4% in
1QFY24 on account of inventory losses during the quarter compared to hefty inventory
gains during SPLY. On a QoQ basis, the gross margins in 1QFY25 increased by
134bps amid lower inventory losses incurred during the quarter.
•
Finance cost increased by 1% YoY to PKR 10,424mn in 1QFY25 due to higher late
payment surcharge during the period.
•
The company recorded effective taxation at 66% in 1QFY25 vis-à-vis 49% in 1QFY24.

Pakistan State Oil Company Limited

Title: AHL Alert: Lucky Core Ventures Terminated the SPA
Created At: 2024-01-12T10:56:48.199Z
Tickers: LCI, LOTCHEM
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 65a11af015fdcdc42f86321d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
According to a notice at the Pakistan Stock Exchange, Lucky Core Ventures (Pvt) Limited (LCV)
has decided to terminate the share purchase agreement (SPA) for the acquisition of 1,136mn
shares (75.01%) of Lotte Chemical Pakistan Limited (LOTCHEM). To recall, LCV initially entered
into an SPA with Lotte Chemical Corporation on Jan 26, 2023.
As a result of this termination, LCV will be withdrawing the public announcement of its intention
to acquire the aforementioned shares of LOTCHEM. LCV is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lucky
Core Industries Limited (LCI).
Recommendations
•
Currently, we have a “HOLD” call on LOTCHEM with a Dec’24 TP of PKR 27.9/share, while we
have a “BUY” call on LCI with a Dec’24 TP of PKR 937.1/share.

## REPORT 126
Title: AHL Alert: APL - Result Review: Profitability arrives at PKR 111.09 in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-02T08:40:59.92Z
Tickers: APL
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 66d57a1b311a3f7dcc1c8057
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66d57a1b311a3f7dcc1c8057.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2 September 2024
Earnings increase by 16% YoY to PKR 24.45/share in 4QFY24
Attock Petroleum Limited (APL) announced the financial result for FY24, whereby the
company has posted a net profit of PKR 13,822mn (EPS: PKR 111.09) against PKR
12,461mn (EPS: PKR 100.15) in FY23, up by 11% YoY. During 4QFY24, the bottom-line
settled at PKR 3,041mn (EPS: PKR 24.45), up by 16% YoY. Alongside the result, the
company announced a final cash dividend of PKR 17.50/share (PKR 27.50 in FY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales during FY24 climbed up by 11% YoY, clocking in at PKR 526,317mn, which
is attributable to i) higher average retail price of petroleum products, and ii) jump in MS
and HSD offtake by 4% and 5% YoY, respectively. On a quarterly basis, the topline
settled at PKR 130,638mn, up by 6% YoY owing to an increase in petroleum prices
coupled with a growth in MS and HSD dispatches by 6% and 12% YoY, respectively.
•
Gross margins of the company reduced by 131bps YoY to 4.5% in FY24. On the other
hand, the gross margins in 4QFY24 arrived at 3.1% vis-à-vis 5.1% in SPLY owed to
higher inventory losses (~PKR 3bn) during the quarter.
•
The operating expenses during FY24 declined by 19% YoY, arriving at PKR 7,592mn
owing to lower exchange loss during the period.
•
Finance income in FY24 depicted a surge of 75% YoY to PKR 9,634mn on the back of
higher income from cash and cash balances. Meanwhile, on a quarterly basis, the
finance income climbed up by 51% YoY due to aforementioned reason.
•
Finance cost reduced by 29% YoY to PKR 1,619mn in FY24 given a fall in mark-up
charged on late payments during the period. The finance cost during 4QFY24 clocked
in at PKR 454mn, down by 31% YoY due to the reason stated above.
•
The company recorded effective taxation at 42.32% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 53.31% in
4QFY23.

## REPORT 127
Title: AHL Alert: MARI - Result Review: Highest ever yearly earnings and dividend
Created At: 2024-08-08T11:54:50.947Z
Tickers: MARI
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 66b4b20a311a3f7dccc1bc0f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66b4b20a311a3f7dccc1bc0f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
8 August 2024
Profitability surged by 62% YoY to PKR 192.34/share in 4QFY24
Mari Petroleum Company Limited (MARI) announced its financial result today, posting
a bottom line of PKR 77,288mn (EPS: PKR 579.36) during FY24 against PKR
56,129mn (EPS: PKR 420.75) in FY23, up by 38% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the
company’s profitability arrived at PKR 25,659mn (EPS: PKR 192.34), up by 62% YoY).
In addition to the result the company announced final cash dividend of PKR 134/share
(PKR 232/share in FY24) and bonus of 800%.
Result Highlights
•
Topline in FY24 increased by 25% YoY, settling at PKR 181,829mn compared to
PKR 145,770mn in SPLY amid i) 9% and 12% YoY jump in gas and oil production,
respectively, ii) 19% YoY hike in the wellhead price of Mari Gas Field, and iii) 12%
YoY devaluation of PKR against USD. During 4QFY24, the net sales declined by
15% YoY, arriving at PKR 39,836mn given i) gas production from Mari HRL below
the threshold level of 577.5 mmcfd during 4QFY24 and ii) 3% YoY Pak Rupee
appreciation against greenback.
•
The exploration cost declined by 19% YoY to PKR 12,924mn in FY24. Meanwhile,
the company booked a reversal of PKR 5,859mn in exploration cost in 4QFY24
against exploration cost of PKR 6,478mn in SPLY, despite incurring dry well (Halini-
2) during the quarter. We await disclosure of detail financial accounts for clarity in
this regard.
•
The finance income arrived at PKR 9,209mn in FY24, up by 1% YoY. Whereas,
finance income during 4QFY24 settled at PKR 3,104mn, depicting a decline of 40%
YoY on account of higher income on cash and cash balances.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 20% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 37% in 4QFY23.
REP-300
Mari Petroleum Company Limited
Result Review: Highest ever yearly earnings and dividend

8 August 2024
Other Information
•
The government has extended Mari D&P lease by 5-Yr, renewing the MARI’s rights on the lease until Nov’29 with an additional payment of 15% wellhead
value. Moreover, the new amendments in the PP12 allows the company to extend the lease until the field production remains commercially viable.
•
The construction of early production facilities in Waziristan Block and SNGP gas transmission pipeline are completed. At present, pipeline hydro testing is
ongoing after which production is expected to commence.
•
During FY24, the company made discoveries at Shawal-1, Shewa-2, Maiwand X-1, and Jhim East X-1.
•
Furthermore, MARI has increased its exploration acreage by acquiring working interests in Nareli (27.55%), Zarghun South (40%), and Kalchas South (44%)
blocks.
•
Towards the end of Jun’24, MARI’s 2P reserves stood at 704 mnBOE (Reserves Replacement Ratio of 423%).
•
The appraisal program of Ghazij gas discovery was concluded in FY24, in which 4 appraisal wells were drilled, tested, and commenced test production.
•
During FY24, 4 development wells were drilled and connected to production line such as MD-20, HRL-124H, Adam-2 and Togh-2.
•
MARI’s subsidiary Mari Mining Co. (Pvt) Ltd was awarded two mineral exploration licenses in Chaghi district of Balochistan.

## REPORT 128
Title: AHL Alert - MTL 9MFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 30.25
Created At: 2026-04-29T06:57:44.494Z
Tickers: MTL
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 69f1abe8973030436086b497
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1abe8973030436086b497.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Result Highlights
•
Millat Tractors Limited (MTL) announced its financial results for 9MFY26, posting a Profit
After Tax (PAT) of PKR 6,036mn (EPS: PKR 30.25), compared to PKR 5,049mn (EPS:
PKR 25.30) in the same period last year, reflecting an increase of 20% YoY. Cumulative
dividends for 9MFY26 stand at PKR 20/share, declared earlier in the year.
•
MTL's Board has proposed a 2-for-1 stock split, reducing face value from PKR 10 to PKR
5/share. Upon shareholder approval at the EOGM on June 5, 2026, shares outstanding will
increase from ~199.5mn to ~399.0mn.
•
Net revenue for 9MFY26 stood at PKR 45,566mn, reflecting a 14% YoY increase
compared to PKR 39,986mn in the same period last year. On a quarterly basis, net
revenue for 3QFY26 rose 37% YoY to PKR 17,129mn, up from PKR 12,492mn in 3QFY25,
though declined 18% QoQ. Volumes grew 11% YoY to 4,878 units (SPLY: 4,411 units),
supported by disbursements under Phase 3 of the Green Tractor Scheme, which focused
on 50–60 horsepower variants.
•
Gross margins expanded significantly to 34.6% in 9MFY26, compared to 27.1% in the
SPLY. On a quarterly basis, gross margins stood at 35.1% in 3QFY26, up from 28.0% in
the same quarter last year, driven by higher volumes.
•
Other income increased to PKR 151mn in 3QFY26 (+125% YoY) against PKR 67mn last
year, due to higher long term investments and cash.
•
Meanwhile, finance cost fell 30% YoY to PKR 284mn (vs PKR 408mn in SPLY) owing to a
decline in overall borrowings and interest rates.
•
The effective tax rate rose to 45.4% in 9MFY26 versus 18.4% in the same period last year.
In 3QFY26, the effective tax rate stood at 55.1%, up from 38.0% in 3QFY25.
•
The scrip is trading at FY26E and FY27F P/E multiples of 13.7x and 12.8x, respectively.

## REPORT 129
Title: Result Review: INDU - 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 63.07
Created At: 2024-02-22T07:05:23.88Z
Tickers: INDU
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 65d6f233c954d41aba663f02
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65d6f233c954d41aba663f02.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY24: Profitability arrived at PKR 22.15/share
Indus Motors Company Limited (INDU) announced its financial result for 1HFY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 4,957mn (EPS: PKR 63.07), up by 89% YoY. During 2QFY24 the 
profitability of the company was recorded at PKR 1,741mn (EPS: PKR 22.15) compared to a 
profit of PKR 1,331mn (EPS: PKR 16.93) in 2QFY23, up by 31% YoY. Alongside the result, 
the company announced a cash dividend of PKR 13.2/share (PKR 37.70/share in 1HFY24).
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales during 1HFY24 clocked in at PKR 50,910mn in contrast to PKR 86,834mn in 
SPLY, plummeting by 41% YoY. During 2QFY24, net sales declined by 63% YoY | 44% 
QoQ, due to lower volumetric sales (-72% YoY) with major reduction coming in sales of 
Fortuner and Hilux (-83% YoY).
•
During 1HFY24 gross margin arrived at 9.3%, in contrast to -3.3% during SPLY mainly 
due to a stable PKR USD parity and a decline in raw material cost i.e.; steel. On a 
quarterly basis, the gross margins settled at 7.8% during 2QFY24 compared to -1.0% in 
SPLY, due to the aforementioned reason.
•
Distribution expense rose by 94% YoY settling at 654mn during 2QFY24, This rise can be 
attributed to an upsurge in marketing expenses associated with the Corolla Cross.
•
Other income declined by 28% YoY to clock in at PKR 2,494mn in 2QFY24, mainly due 
to a decline in short-term investments, resulting in lower interest income.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 28% in 2QFY24 vis-à-vis 31% in 2QFY23.
Other Information:
•
The company’s board has approved an investment of PKR 3bn for the purpose of 
incremental localization of parts and components of CKDs being assembled by the 
company. Furthermore, the company plans to complete the investment by 3QCY25. 
1

## REPORT 130
Title: AHL Alert: FFBL - Result Review: Bottom line arrives at PKR 3.33/share
Created At: 2024-04-25T12:36:24.329Z
Tickers: FFBL
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 662a4e48615439187e022341
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662a4e48615439187e022341.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 April 2024
Profit amid absence of hefty exchange losses during the quarter
Fauji Fertilizer Bin Qasim Limited (FFBL) announced financial result for 1QCY24 today 
where the company registered a net profit of PKR 4,306mn (EPS: PKR 3.33) against a loss 
of PKR 5,429mn (LPS: PKR 4.20) in 1QCY23 which is primarily due to absence of hefty 
exchange loss during the quarter. 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales jumped up by 48% YoY to PKR 46,524mn during 1QCY24 owed to i) 33% YoY 
growth in DAP sales, and ii) higher urea and DAP prices. However, urea offtake 
plummeted by 27% YoY in 1QCY24. On QoQ basis, the topline reduced by 17% amid a 
decline in urea and DAP offtake by 23% and 25%, respectively. 
•
Gross margins clocked in at 18.9% in 1QCY24 vis-à-vis 6.8% in SPLY owed to a decline 
in phosphoric acid prices.   
•
Other income surged by 5x YoY to PKR 4,025mn in 1QCY24 on account of higher income 
from cash and cash equivalents tagged with a dividend income from subsidiaries and 
associates (AKBL).
•
Financial charges plummeted by 59% YoY to clock in at PKR 1,020mn during 1QCY24 
amid lower short-term borrowings.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 47% in 1QCY24.

Title: AHL Alert: UBL - Result Review: CY24 EPS arrives at PKR 61.4
Created At: 2025-02-19T09:53:15.753Z
Tickers: UBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 67b5aa0b881e05effa525532

--- Page 1 ---
19 February 2025
UBL posted highest ever profitability in CY24
UBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY24 at PKR 26.0bn (EPS: PKR 21.3), depicting
a jump of 93% YoY | 42% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 11.0). This took the CY24 earnings to
PKR 75.2bn (EPS: PKR 61.4) up by 36% YoY. The jump in overall earnings was mainly on the
back of growth in NFI. Along with the result, UBL announced a final dividend of PKR 11/share
in 4QCY24, taking full year, CY24, dividend to PKR 44/share (CY23: PKR 44/share)
Result Highlights

Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 64.7bn during 4QCY24, up by 74% YoY and
25% QoQ. With this, the total NII for CY24 went up to PKR 173.5bn, marking a 16% YoY
jump. Interest earned surged by 103% YoY during CY24, however, interest expenses also
rose significantly, increasing by 136% YoY.

The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 122% YoY during 4QCY24, pushing the
total NFI for CY24 to PKR 83.7bn, a remarkable 132% YoY increase. This exceptional
growth was primarily fueled by a massive gain on the sale of securities, which soared 85x
YoY to PKR 45.5bn. Additionally, fee income rose by 9% YoY to PKR 21.5bn, while
dividend income increased by PKR 1.7bn (8% YoY).

Provisioning charge for the bank clocked in at PKR 14.2bn during 4QCY24 (4QCY23:
provisioning reversal PKR 1,040mn). This takes total provisioning charge to PKR 12.8bn
during CY24 (+13x YoY).

The bank’s OPEX increased 70% YoY in 4QCY24 clocking-in at PKR 36.1bn (4QCY23:
PKR 21.3bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 39% in CY24 against 39% during same
period last year.

Effective tax rate was set at 40% during 4QCY24 compared to 57% in SPLY. With this, the
effective tax for CY24 stood at 48% (CY23: 49%)
REP-300
United Bank Limited
Result Review: CY24 EPS arrives at PKR 61.4

## REPORT 132
Title: AHL Alert - DGKC Highest Ever PAT After 4QFY18
Created At: 2026-02-23T10:49:21.7Z
Tickers: DGKC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 699c30b1d33a93d06bdeea37
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699c30b1d33a93d06bdeea37.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
1HFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 13.36/share; up by 66% YoY.
•
DGKC posted Profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 5.85bn (66.1% YoY); EPS at PKR
13.36/share in 1HFY26. The earnings growth is largely driven by improved gross margins
and reduction in finance cost. To note, the company has announced the highest ever PAT
after 4QFY18.
Result Highlights
•
Net Revenue for 1HFY26 was recorded at PKR 40.5bn (+9.72% YoY). Higher revenue is
mainly attributable to increase in their domestic dispatches by 6%.
•
Gross margins stood at 26.9% in 1HFY26, up from 22.8% in 1HFY25 and 21.7% in
1QFY26. In our view, the expansion was driven by lower international coal prices and
uptick in domestic dispatches.
•
Other income fell 2% YoY to PKR 2.1bn in 1HFY26, largely due to the lower interest rate
environment.
•
The cash and equivalents rose to PKR 38.4bn (PKR 88/share) in 2QFY26, up from PKR
29.4bn (PKR 67/share) in 1QFY26.
•
Finance costs declined 72% YoY and 29% QoQ to PKR 735mn in 1HFY26, primarily
reflecting the impact of lower interest rates.
•
Effective tax rate in 1HFY26 stood at 35.2% compared to 32.3% in 1HFY25 and 37.0% in
1QFY26.
Recommendation
We maintain a BUY stance on DGKC with a Dec’26 Target price of PKR 289/share. Currently,
the stock is trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 8.58x and 7.66x, respectively

## REPORT 133
Title: AHL Alert: PPL - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 25.28 in 1HFY24
Created At: 2024-02-26T10:33:34.908Z
Tickers: PPL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 65dc68fec954d41aba01bc21
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65dc68fec954d41aba01bc21.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY24: Highest-ever quarterly earnings
As per latest financial result announcement, Pakistan Petroleum Limited (PPL)’s profit after tax
arrived at PKR 68,777mn (EPS: PKR 25.28) during 1HFY24 compared to PKR 48,936mn (EPS:
PKR 17.98) in 1HFY23, climbing up by 41% YoY. During 2QFY24, the company posted highest
ever quarterly earnings of PKR 39,150mn (EPS: PKR 14.92), depicting significant jump of 75%
YoY. In addition to the result, the company announced an interim cash dividend of PKR
2.50/share in 2QFY24 (PKR 1.00/share in 2QFY23).
Result Highlights
•
Topline during 1HFY24 witnessed a growth of 9% YoY, settling at PKR 149,755mn on the
back of a devaluation of the Pak Rupee against the greenback. Meanwhile, the Sui wellhead
price declined by 5% YoY while oil and gas production reduced by 3% and 14% YoY,
respectively. On a quarterly basis, net sales ascended by 11% YoY, clocking in at PKR
72,307mn in 2QFY24 which is attributable to i) 2% YoY uptick in oil production, and ii) 21%
YoY PKR depreciation against USD.
•
Exploration costs reduced by 5% YoY, settling at PKR 8,589mn in 1HFY24. Meanwhile,
exploration cost in 2QFY24 plummeted by 12% YoY, settling at PKR 6,590mn given one dry
well (Murad X-1) incurred during the quarter compared to two dry wells (Hatim X-1 and Faiz
X-1) in SPLY.
•
Other income clocked-in at PKR 7,732mn in 1HFY24, showcasing a growth of 16% YoY
owing to higher income from cash and cash balances during the period. In 2QFY24, other
income settled at PKR 3,978mn, up by 119% YoY due to aforementioned reason.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 1% in 2QFY24 vis-à-vis 31% in 2QFY23. During
2QFY24, the company secured favourable verdict from the court related to depletion on
wellhead allowance. Hence, the company booked a hefty tax reversal in 2QFY24.
1
AHL Alert: E&P

AHL Alert | 24-Apr-2025 
Cement: Fauji Cement Company Limited
Result Review: Earnings for 9MFY25 registered a 34% YoY growth
3QFY25: Earnings arrived at PKR 0.87/share
Fauji Cement Company Ltd (FCCL) announced its financial results for 9MFY25, posting a
profit of PKR 9.4bn (EPS: PKR 3.84), marking a 34% YoY increase from PKR 7.0bn (EPS:
PKR 2.87). During 3QFY25, the company recorded earnings of PKR 2.1bn (EPS: PKR
0.87), reflecting a 21% YoY rise but a 47% decline QoQ.
Result Highlights
•
In 9MFY25, company reported sales of PKR 67.2bn, reflecting a 13% YoY increase,
driven by a 6% YoY rise in dispatches. For 3QFY25, revenue edged up to PKR 19.3bn,
a modest 1% YoY growth from PKR 19.0bn, primarily supported by higher selling
prices. However, on a QoQ basis, sales dropped by 22%, mainly due to a 2% decline in
dispatches and a fall in local prices.
•
Gross margins improved to 34.3% in 9MFY25, up from 30.7% in 9MFY24, mainly due to
higher retention prices. In 3QFY25, gross margins increased by 437bps YoY, reaching
32.5%, supported by the same favorable pricing factors.
•
Selling and distribution expenses declined by 12% YoY in 9MFY25, amounting to PKR
2,250mn. These expenses also fell by 8% YoY during 3QFY25.
•
Finance costs for 9MFY25 rose by 8% YoY to PKR 3,867mn. However, in 3QFY25,
finance costs dropped by 7% YoY but surged 39% on a QoQ basis.
•
The company reported an effective tax rate of 38.7% in 3QFY25, compared to 33.1% in
the same period last year.
Outlook and Recommendation: We forecast an increase in local dispatches in FY26, and
expect FCCL to benefit from this growth, supported by its timely expansion. We maintain a
BUY rating on the stock with a target price of Dec’25 ending at PKR 53.6/share

Title: AHL Alert:  BAFL - Result Review: 3QCY24 EPS arrives at PKR 8.42
Created At: 2024-10-17T09:55:07.642Z
Tickers: BAFL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 6710defb8f84bab98c5f7bce

--- Page 1 ---
17 October 2024
3QCY24: EPS supported by higher non funded income and lower provisioning
BAFL reported its 3QCY24 earnings at PKR 13.3bn (EPS: PKR 8.42), surging up by
50% YoY | 11% QoQ. For 9MCY24, total earnings amounted to PKR 35.2bn, marking
a 28% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled by a jump in total
income and decline in provisioning. The bank also announced a cash dividend of PKR
2.0/share in 3QCY24 along with the result taking total payout for 9MCY24 to PKR
6.0/share.
Result Highlights

Interest earned by the bank settled at PKR 131.4bn during 3QCY24, surging by 20%
YoY and 3% QoQ while interest expense was up by 25% YoY | 1% QoQ reaching
PKR 97.6bn. With this, Net Interest Income of the bank clocked in at PKR 33.8bn in
3QCY24, up by 6% YoY | 10%QoQ (9MCY24: PKR 95.1bn, +5% YoY).

The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 120% YoY during 3QCY24,
bringing the total for 9MCY24 to PKR 33.8bn, a 70% YoY increase. The increase is
primarily fueled by a 23% YoY rise in fee income, reaching PKR 13.6bn, and a jump
in foreign exchange income, which climbed to PKR 7.8bn, up by 3% YoY.
Additionally, other income saw a 14% YoY surge to PKR 2.7bn in 9MCY24.
Furthermore, the bank posted a gain of PKR 8.6bn from the sale of securities,
compared to a loss of PKR 1.8bn in the same period last year.

A provisioning charge was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 440mn,
this took the provisioning charge for the 9MCY24 to PKR 208mn compared to a
provisioning charge during 9MCY23 of PKR 9.2bn.

The bank’s OPEX increased by 27% YoY | 2% QoQ in 3QCY24 clocking in at PKR
20.7bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 43.9% in 3QCY24 against 43.3% SPLY.

Effective tax rate during 3QCY24 stood at 49.0% compared to 49.0% SPLY.

## REPORT 136
Title: AHL Alert - MLCF 9MFY26 Earnings Per Share Stood at PKR 7.44
Created At: 2026-04-22T07:00:13.055Z
Tickers: MLCF
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 69e871fd1f328d6e651c7f67
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69e871fd1f328d6e651c7f67.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
9MFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 7.44/share
Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited (MLCF) announced its financial results for 9MFY26, reporting
consolidated earnings of PKR 7.79bn (EPS: PKR 7.44), reflecting a marginal decline of 1% YoY. The
slight dip in profitability is primarily attributed to higher cost of sales, increased finance costs, and a
rise in the effective tax rate.
Result Highlights
•
During 9MFY26, revenue increased by 11% YoY to PKR 56.9bn, compared to PKR 51.3bn in
9MFY25. The growth was primarily driven by a 20% YoY increase in total dispatches by PIOC,
supporting overall revenue performance, while MLCF dispatches declined by 2% YoY.
•
Gross margins declined to 34% in 9MFY26 (from 36% in 9MFY25), primarily due to a 14%
increase in cost of sales. The rise in costs was driven by higher fuel expenses due to Afghan
border closure. While margins compressed, the impact was partially offset by increased dispatch
volumes.
•
Selling and distribution expenses declined by 27% YoY in 9MFY26, settling at PKR 2.3bn. The
decrease is primarily attributable to the company’s shift away from its previous direct distribution
model.
•
Finance costs declined by 7% YoY to PKR 2.3bn in 9MFY26, primarily due to lower interest
rates. However, the company’s leverage increased significantly during the period. Long-term
borrowings rose to PKR 83.4bn in 3QFY26, compared to PKR 7.8bn in 2QFY26, mainly due to
the acquisition of PIOC, financed through debt of PKR 75bn. Additionally, short-term borrowings
stood at PKR 4.0bn in 3QFY26, compared to no short-term borrowings in 2QFY26.
•
Other income, including finance income, declined by 3% to PKR 1.2bn in 9MFY26. The decrease
was primarily driven by lower interest income compared to SPLY.
•
The company booked ETR at 36% in 9MFY26 vs 28.1% in 9MFY25.
We maintain a BUY stance on MLCF with a Dec’26 Target price of PKR 148/share. Currently, the
stock is trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 8.3x and 6.5x, respectively.
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 22-Apr-2026 
Cement: Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Engineering 
Agha Steel Industries Limited 
September 29, 2022 
 
Agha Steel Industries Limited (AGHA) announced the financial result for FY22 today, posting a profit 
after tax (PAT) of PKR 1,855mn (EPS: PKR 3.07) compared to PKR 2,036mn (EPS: PKR 3.37) in SPLY. 
During the last quarter of FY22, the company posted earnings of PKR 115mn (EPS: PKR 0.19) while 
profitability for 4QFY21 arrived at PKR 438mn (EPS: PKR 0.72).  
    Result Highlights 
 
 During FY22, topline of the company underwent a growth of 29% YoY primarily aided by a nearly 
40% jump in prices of rebars and specialized billets, whereas we believe volumes posted a mild 
decline on a YoY basis. A similar trend was witnessed in the last quarter.  
 Gross margins during FY22 settled at 21.4% vs. 22.7% in FY21 as the company sold a lower 
quantum of wire rods / specialized billets (high margin products) coupled with higher scrap prices, 
PKR depreciation, and a higher energy tariff. During 4QFY22, dip in margins was much sharper 
(17.6% against 30.6% in SPLY and 23.2% in 3QFY22) as the price hike remained insufficient due 
to existing long term contracts to pass on the impact of higher scrap prices, PKR depreciation, and 
a higher energy tariff. 
 Finance costs displayed a jump of 52% YoY in FY22 to PKR 2,136bn attributable to a sharp hike 
in the benchmark policy rate by the SBP during the year as well as augmented borrowing to meet 
the higher working capital requirement. Similarly, the expense rose by 45% YoY in 4Q due to 
aforementioned reasons. 
 The company booked effective taxation at 45% during 4QFY22 on account of super tax. 
 
Result Review: FY22 EPS @ PKR 3.07

## REPORT 138
Title: AHL Alert: MEBL - Result Review: Highest ever PAT recorded in 2Q
Created At: 2024-08-08T11:00:36.231Z
Tickers: MEBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 66b4a554311a3f7dccbf849f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66b4a554311a3f7dccbf849f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
8 August 2024
▪
MEBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 2QCY24 at PKR 26.7bn (EPS: PKR 14.9), 
depicting an increase of 54% YoY | 5% QoQ (2QCY23 EPS: PKR 9.66). This jump in 
overall QoQ earnings was mainly on the back of higher total income and provisioning 
reversal. The bank announced a dividend of PKR 7/share along with 2Q result.
▪
With this, total earnings for 1HCY24 increased to PKR 52.1bn, marking a 59% YoY 
increase. 
•
Net Profit Earned of the bank settled at PKR 70.4bn during 2QCY24, increasing 42% 
YoY and 4% QoQ. With this, total NPE for 1HCY24 went up to PKR 137.9bn, marking 
a 52% YoY jump. Profit earned surged by 29% YoY | 9% QoQ while profit expense was 
up by 16% YoY | 16% QoQ. 
•
NFI depicted an increase of 46% YoY during 1HCY24 (2QCY24: +31 YoY) primarily 
due to a jump in fee and commission income which posted a 32% YoY increase owed 
to higher trade volumes. 
•
In addition, FX income surged by 7% YoY to reach at PKR 579mn during 1HCY24. 
•
Furthermore, the bank recorded gain on sale of securities of PKR 1,028mn in 1HCY24 , 
as compared to a loss on sale of securities of PKR 341mn in SPLY.
•
During 2QCY24, bank booked provisioning reversal of PKR 328mn vs a provisioning 
charge of PKR 1,504mn in 2QCY23. With this, the total provisioning reversal during 
1HCY24 stood at PKR 672mn as compared to a charge of PKR 3,634mn SPLY.
•
The bank’s OPEX increased 38% YoY | 4% QoQ during 2QCY24 clocking-in at PKR 
22.7bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 29.3% in 2QCY24 against 29.9% same period 
last year
•
Effective tax rate was 50.8% during 2QCY24 compared to 53.2% recorded in SPLY.
REP-300
Meezan Bank Limited

## REPORT 139
Title: AHL Alert: KOHC -  Result Review: 9MFY24 EPS @ PKR 33.25
Created At: 2024-04-25T12:58:29.731Z
Tickers: KOHC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 662a5375b3858eb2c783361d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662a5375b3858eb2c783361d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 April 2024
3QFY24: Profitability arrived at PKR 10.51/share
Kohat Cement Company Limited (KOHC) announced its financial result for 9MFY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 6,512mn (EPS: PKR 33.25) compared to a PAT of PKR 5,363 (EPS: 
PKR 26.91) in SPLY, up by 21% YoY. During 3QFY24 earnings clocked in at PKR 2,058mn 
(EPS: PKR 10.51) against PKR 1,624mn (EPS: 8.29), showcasing a jump of 27% YoY.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 9MFY24 remained stable at PKR 29,972mn. During 3QFY24 net sales 
plummeted by 15% YoY to settle at PKR 8,510mn in contrast to PKR 10,010mn in SPLY.
•
Gross margins for 9MFY24 arrived at 28% vis-à-vis 27% in SPLY. During 3QFY24, the 
gross margins climbed by 754bps to 30% owing to higher cement prices in tandem with a 
fall in coal prices.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in 9MFY24 increased by 19% YoY to clock in at PKR 
138mn, which is attributable to the introduction of the axle load factor, we view. In 3QFY24, 
selling and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 53mn vis-à-vis PKR 42mn, up by 26% 
YoY.
•
Other income surged by 2x YoY in 9MFY24 to clock in at PKR 3,145mn, due to higher 
income from short-term investments. In 3QFY24, the other income arrived at PKR 
1,078mn, depicting a massive growth of 99% YoY due to the aforementioned reason.
•
Finance costs in 9MFY24 increased by 5% YoY to PKR 535mn on the back of higher 
interest rates. In 3QFY24, the finance cost decline by 10% YoY amid fall in the long term 
finances.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 34% in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis 30% in 3QFY23.
REP-300
Kohat Cement Company Limited
Result Review: 9MFY24 EPS @ PKR 33.25

Created At: 2025-04-25T12:09:35.819Z
Tickers: NBP
Sectors: 0807

Page 07
AHL Alert | 25-Apr-2025 
Commercial Bank: National Bank of Pakistan
Result Review: EPS @ PKR 10.3 in 1QCY25
REP-300

NBP announced its earnings (Profit After Tax) today for 1QCY25 at PKR 21.8bn, with
an EPS of PKR 10.3. This reflects a jump of 2.3x YoY but a decline of 2% QoQ
(4QCY24 EPS: PKR 10.6).

On a YoY basis, higher net interest income enhanced the overall profitability.

During 1QCY25, the bank reported its Net Interest Income at PKR 69.8bn, marking an
uptick of 139% compared to the same period last year and a 6% increase from the
previous quarter.

The bank experienced a decline in interest earned, which dipped by 22% YoY and 17%
QoQ. Moreover, the bank also faced a decline in interest expenses, which contracted
by 42% YoY and 24% compared to the last quarter.

During the outgoing quarter, the bank’s non-funded income witnessed a downturn of
11% YoY, while declined by 56% QoQ.

The downturn on YoY basis was primarily due to loss on sale of securities, which was
recorded at PKR 100mn compared to gain of PKR 4.4bn in SPLY.

Dividend income also declined by 1.1% YoY, settling at PKR 1.7bn. Moreover, other
income declined by 66.5% YoY to PKR 89mn

However, FX income depicted a jump of 13% YoY, while fee income increased by 47%
YoY to record at PKR 9.2bn.

The bank recorded a provisioning charge of PKR 6.4bn for 1QCY25 against
a
provisioning charge of PKR 727mn recorded in 1QCY24,

The bank’s OPEX increased by 30% YoY | -71% QoQ in 1QCY25 clocking in at PKR
29.2bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 35% in 1QCY25 against 52% SPLY

The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 53.1% during 1QCY25, showing a jump
from 51.9% in SPLY.

## REPORT 141
Title: Result Review:  ACPL - 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 14.85
Created At: 2024-02-06T07:13:52.202Z
Tickers: ACPL
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 65c1dc306c1b4b36b6ea44dc
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65c1dc306c1b4b36b6ea44dc.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY24: Earnings settled at PKR 3.62/share
Attock Cement Pakistan Limited (ACPL) announced its financial result for 1HFY24
today, posting a PAT of PKR 2,040 mn (EPS: PKR 14.85) compared to a PAT of PKR
560 mn (EPS: PKR 4.08) in SPLY, a growth of 264% YoY. On the other hand, the
profitability in 2QFY24 swelled up by 12% YoY to PKR 497 mn (EPS: PKR 3.62)
compared to SPLY. Alongside the result, ACPL announced an interim cash dividend of
PKR 2.5/share in 2QFY24.
Result Highlights
•
Topline during 1HFY24 clocked-in at PKR 14,638 mn, displaying a jump of 41% YoY
in contrast to PKR 10,373 mn in SPLY, amid higher volumetric sales. On a quarterly
basis, net sales rose by 33% YoY to settle at PKR 7,978 mn, primarily led by surge in
exports. Local dispatches a depicted decline in 2QFY24 on the back of drop in
domestic demand.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY24 arrived at 19% compared to 20% in SPLY. Whereas,
gross margins in 2QFY24 settled at 21%, down by 200bps compared to SPLY, due to
lower margins on exports.
•
The company recorded a one off gain of 2,197 mn during 1HFY24 on the back of sale
of the subsidiary ‘Saqr AL Keetan for Cement Production Company Limited’.
•
The other income in 1HFY24 fell by 44% YoY to settled at PKR 83 mn. Whereby, in
2QFY24 other income depicted a drop of 18% YoY to record at 56 mn.
•
Finance costs fell by 33% YoY to PKR 53mn in 2QFY24.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 4% in 2QFY24 vs. 45% in 2QFY23, amid
rise in exports.
1

## REPORT 142
Title: AHL Alert: HBL - Result Review: 3QCY24 EPS arrives at PKR 9.85
Created At: 2024-10-16T10:29:36.847Z
Tickers: HBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 670f95908f84bab98c1ba25b
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/670f95908f84bab98c1ba25b.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
16 October 2024
3QCY24: EPS supported by higher non funded income
HBL reported its 3QCY24 earnings at PKR 14.5bn (EPS: PKR 9.85), depicting a
decline of 13% YoY while up by 1% QoQ. For 9MCY24, total earnings amounted to
PKR 44.0bn, marking a 3% YoY increase. The rise in earnings on YoY basis is fueled
by a jump in total income. The bank also announced a cash dividend of PKR 4/share in
3QCY24 along with the result taking total payout for 9MCY24 to PKR 12.0/share.
Result Highlights

Interest earned by the bank settled at PKR 218bn during 3QCY24, increasing 14%
YoY and 4% QoQ while interest expense was up by 23% YoY | 4% QoQ reaching
PKR 154bn. With this, Net Interest Income of the bank clocked in at PKR 64bn in
3QCY24, down by 2% YoY | +4%QoQ (9MCY24: PKR 185.3bn, +4% YoY).

The bank's Non-Funded Income (NFI) surged by 63% YoY during 3QCY24, bringing
the total for 9MCY24 to PKR 60.7bn, a 59% YoY increase. The increase is primarily
fueled by a 20% YoY rise in fee income, reaching PKR 36.6bn, and a significant
boost in foreign exchange income, which climbed to PKR 5.6bn from PKR 1.6bn in
the same period last year. Additionally, other income saw a remarkable 110% YoY
surge to PKR 9.3bn in 9MCY24. Furthermore, the bank posted a gain of PKR 6.6bn
from the sale of securities, compared to a loss of PKR 307mn in the same period
last year.

A provisioning charge was recorded in the outgoing quarter, settling at PKR 8.9bn,
this took the provisioning charge for the 9MCY24 to PKR 19.0bn compared to a
provisioning charge during 9MCY23 of PKR 7.4bn.

The bank’s OPEX increased by 8% YoY | 4% QoQ in 3QCY24 clocking in at PKR
47.9bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 56.5% in 3QCY24 against 57.1% SPLY.

Effective tax rate during 3QCY24 stood at 49.3% compared to 48.1% SPLY.
REP-300
Habib Bank Limited

## REPORT 143
Title: AHL Alert - KOHC: Result Review: 1QFY24 EPS @ PKR 11.39
Created At: 2023-10-30T14:43:39Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 6540b2c76156e406d25df1f2
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

--- Page 1 ---
Profitability climbed up by 25% YoY to PKR/share 11.39
Kohat Cement Company Limited (KOHC) announced its financial result for 1QFY24 
today, posting a PAT of PKR 2,230mn (EPS: PKR 11.39) compared to a PAT of PKR 
1,785mn (EPS: PKR 8.89) in SPLY, up by 25% YoY. 
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 1QFY24 clocked-in at PKR 11,064mn, displaying a jump of 25% YoY 
in contrast to PKR 8,853mn in SPLY. This growth in 1QFY24 is attributable to 
increase in the retention prices coupled with jump in dispatches clocking in at 0.76mn 
tons vis-à-vis 0.69mn tons (up 11% YoY). 
•
Gross margins in 1QFY24 reduced by 210bps clocking at 29.2% as compared to 
31.3% during same period last year. The decline in margins is mainly due to rise in 
the cost of packing materials consumed.
•
Other income in 1QFY24 surged by 136% to arrive at PKR 866mn, amid rise in 
interest income.
•
Finance costs rose by 18% YoY to clock in at PKR 203mn in 1QFY24, on the back of 
higher interest rates.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 36% in 1QFY24.
Additional information
•
The Company has commissioned a 10MW Solar Power Project at its Kohat site in 
the month of August 2023 and is in the process of installation of another 15MW Solar 
Power Project

## REPORT 144
Title: AHL Alert: PSO - EPS of PKR 183.66 in FY22
Created At: 2022-08-26T09:44:27Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 649170427cf4c3f30fc34221
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=500&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/649170427cf4c3f30fc34221.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
 P a g e  1 | 2 
 
AHL Alert 
Oil and Gas Marketing Companies 
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited 
August 26, 2022 
4QFY22: Profitability jumped up by 97% YoY  
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited (PSO) announced its financial result for FY22 posting a 
profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 86.2bn (EPS: PKR 183.66), depicting a massive growth of 196% 
YoY. On a quarterly basis, the earnings climbed up by 97% YoY, clocking-in at PKR 21.5bn 
(EPS: PKR 45.70) in 4QFY22. Alongside the result, the company announced a final cash 
dividend of PKR 10.00/share.  
 
Result Highlights 
 
 Net sales of the company arrived at PKR 2,451bn in FY22, up by 104% YoY given 
the increase in sales volumes of MS, HSD, and FO by 15%, 26%, and 62% YoY, 
respectively tagged with higher average selling prices of petroleum products. 
Whereas, the topline in 4QFY22 ascended by 157% YoY given growth in overall 
volumes by 42% YoY (MS, HSD, and FO volumes went up by 11%, 24%, and 167% 
YoY, respectively).  
 The company posted a gross profit of PKR 161bn with gross margins set at 6.57% in 
FY22 against 4.53% in SPLY, which in our view is a result of significant changes in 
ex-refinery prices leading to substantial inventory gains in FY22. During 4QFY22, the 
gross margins arrived at 7.58% (up by 277bps YoY) due to volumetric growth along 
with hefty inventory gains of ~PKR 41bn. 
 Other income boosted by 32% YoY to PKR 24,813mn owed to higher interest 
received on delayed payments. Meanwhile, in 4QFY22, the other income witnessed 
a drastic decline of 82% YoY given the absence of significant penal income from the 
power sector booked last year.  
 The company booked provisioning of impairment on financial assets related to trade 
debts of PKR 4,737mn during the 4QFY22, taking the total provisioning to PKR 
5,104mn in FY22 compared to PKR 898mn in FY21.  
 The finance costs dropped by 54% YoY to PKR 4,721mn in FY22 given lower late 
payment surcharge. Similarly, the finance cost plunged by 74% YoY in 4QFY22 owed 
to the aforementioned reason. 
 The company recorded effective taxation at 61% in 4QFY22 compared to 35% in 
4QFY21. The taxation during this quarter includes super tax imposed on profit before 
tax of FY22. 
Recommendation 
Currently, we have a ‘BUY’ call on the scrip with Jun’23 Target Price of PKR 271.91/share.  
 
Result Review: EPS of PKR 183.66 in FY22

## REPORT 145
Title: AHL Alert - FABL Earnings in line with expectations @ PKR 3.51/sh in 1QCY26
Created At: 2026-04-23T10:33:25.802Z
Tickers: FABL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 69e9f5751f328d6e65f32d39
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69e9f5751f328d6e65f32d39.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 23-Apr-2026 
Commercial Banks: Faysal Bank Limited
Result Review: Earnings in line with expectations @ PKR 3.51/sh
REP-300

FABL posted earnings of PKR 5.3bn (EPS: PKR 3.51) for 1QCY26, down 2% YoY and
18% QoQ. Alongside the results, the bank announced an interim dividend of PKR
1.5/share, marking a payout of 43%.

Earnings remained mostly muted due to contraction in Net Profit Earned, while support
was provided by growing Non-funded income and provisioning reversals.

Net profit earned stood at PKR 15.6bn, down 9% YoY and 11% QoQ, with both profit
earned and profit expensed declining by 9% YoY to reach PKR 38bn and PKR 23bn
respectively.

Non-funded income grew significantly by 70% YoY, recorded at PKR 9.9bn, with major
contributions by fee income (PKR 4.5bn, +13% YoY), higher capital gains (PKR 3.3bn,
+72x YoY) and growth in FX income by 14% YoY, at PKR 1.9bn.

The bank further recorded a provisioning reversal of PKR 361mn in 1QCY26,
compared to a reversal of PKR 2.3bn in 1QCY25 and PKR 671mn in 4QCY25.

OPEX grew by 7% YoY, taking the overall cost-to-income ratio to 58% for the quarter,
compared to 60% in 1QCY25 and 53% in 4QCY25.

Effective tax rate reduced slightly to reach 51.7% for the quarter (1QCY25: 52.9%).

On the balance sheet front, deposits grew 19% YoY to reach PKR 1.3trn, while
contracting by 7% QoQ. Advances grew by 20% YoY and declined 11% QoQ to reach
PKR 872bn which brought the bank’s ADR to 58% (vs 57% in 1QCY25 and 61% in
4QCY25). Meanwhile the IDR was recorded at 50% on the back of 5% QoQ growth in
investments, as well as the decline in deposits.

The stock is currently trading at a P/B multiple of 1.2x and a Dividend Yield of 7.2% for
CY26e, based on LDCP.

Title: AHL Alert - PPL : Result Review - Highest-ever profitability of PKR 35.99/share in FY23
Created At: 2023-09-20T14:50:48Z

Disclaimer: This document has been prepared by Research analysts at Arif Habib Limited (AHL).  This document does not constitute an offer or solicitation for the purchase or sale of any security. This publication is intended only for

AHL Alert 
Oil & Gas Exploration Companies 
Pakistan Petroleum Limited 
September 20, 2023 
4QFY23: Earnings surge by 13x YoY to PKR 5.92/share  
As per latest financial result announcement, Pakistan Petroleum Limited (PPL) posted a profit after 
tax of PKR 97,937mn (EPS: PKR 35.99) during FY23 compared to PKR 53,546mn (EPS: PKR 19.68) 
in FY22, swelling up by 83% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the bottom-line arrived at PKR 16,103mn 
(EPS: PKR 5.92), depicting a massive growth of 13x YoY. 
 
Result Highlights 
 
 Topline during FY23 jumped up by 42% YoY, clocking-in at PKR 286,480mn given i) 16% YoY 
hike in Sui wellhead price, and ii) stable oil production while 2% YoY growth in gas production, 
and iii) a devaluation of the Pak Rupee against the greenback. On a quarterly basis, net sales 
ascended by 17% YoY, settling at PKR 72,305mn in 4QFY23 on account of i) PKR depreciation 
against USD, and iii) increase in Sui wellhead price (up by 14% YoY). 
 Exploration costs reduced by 9% YoY, settling at PKR 21,628mn in FY23 compared to PKR 
23,735mn in FY22 due to lower cost of dry wells during the year. Meanwhile, exploration cost in 
4QFY23 declined by 38% YoY, settling at PKR 8,679mn owed to absence of dry wells (Khipro 
East X 01 and Pandran X-1 incurred in 4QFY22). 
 Other income clocked-in at PKR 17,539mn in FY23 against PKR 14,144mn in SPLY, showcasing 
a growth of 24% YoY higher exchange gains during the period. In 4QFY23, other income settled 
at PKR 2,640mn, decreasing by 29% YoY. 
 The company booked effective taxation at 56% in 4QFY23 vis-à-vis 94% in 4QFY22. The taxation 
during the quarter includes an additional 6% super tax levied on the profit before tax of FY23.  
 
Result Review: Highest-ever profitability of PKR 35.99/share in FY23

## REPORT 147
Title: AHL Alert: Result review: ABL - EPS @ PKR 10.14 in 1QCY24
Created At: 2024-04-24T09:26:25.084Z
Tickers: ABL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 6628d041a85d383ee3ec5f2d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6628d041a85d383ee3ec5f2d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
24 April 2024

ABL announced earnings (PAT) today for 1QCY24 at PKR 11.6bn (EPS: PKR 10.14),
depicting an increase of 51% YoY, however down 5% QoQ (4QCY23 EPS: PKR 10.7).

The decrease in earnings QoQ was mainly attributed to reduced markup and non-markup
income. However, on a YoY basis, higher income and provisioning reversals contributed to an
increase in overall profitability.

Along with the result, ABL announced a cash dividend of PKR 4.0/share.

In the 1QCY24, the bank reported a Net Interest Income of PKR 29.2bn, marking a significant
43% rise from the same period in the previous year. However, this figure represents a 10%
decline from the preceding quarter.

Interest earned surged by 28% compared to the previous year and saw a modest 1% increase
from the previous quarter. On the other hand, interest expenses witnessed a 22% increase
compared to the same period last year and a 7% uptick compared to the prior quarter.

In the outgoing quarter, non-markup income continued its downward trend, experiencing a 4%
YoY decline and a more significant 16% QoQ decrease. This decline was primarily driven by a
substantial drop in FX income, which plummeted by 61% YoY and 62% QoQ. Additionally,
gains on securities were down by 28% QoQ, totaling PKR 303mn in 1QCY24. However, there
was a contrasting positive trend in Fee Income, which showed robust growth. Fee Income
surged by 45% YoY and 20% QoQ, reaching PKR 4bn in 1QCY24.

In the 1QCY24, the bank reported a reversal in provisioning of PKR 163mn, marking a
significant turnaround from the provisioning charge of PKR 2.3bn recorded in the same period
last year.

The operating expense of the bank clocked-in at PKR 13.6bn in 1QCY24, up 16% YoY and
10% QoQ. With this, the Cost/Income ratio stood at 39.9% for 1QCY24, down from 42.9% in
the same period last year.

The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 49% during 1QCY24, showing an increase from
43% in the same period last year.
REP-300
Allied Bank Limited
Result review: EPS @ PKR 10.14 in 1QCY24

## REPORT 148
Title: Result Review: MCB - Highest-ever profitability and dividend recorded during CY23
Created At: 2024-02-06T09:22:47.013Z
Tickers: MCB
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 65c1fa676c1b4b36b6f190b1
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65c1fa676c1b4b36b6f190b1.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Historic high profitability recorded in CY23
MCB announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY23 at PKR 16.9bn (EPS: PKR 14.24), 
depicting an increase of 23% YoY while down 14% QoQ (4QCY22 EPS: PKR 11.61). 
This jump in overall earnings was mainly on the back of growth in total income. Along 
with the result, MCB announced a final dividend of PKR 9/share (CY23: PKR 30/share). 
Result Highlights 
•
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 46.3bn during 4QCY23, increasing 
61% YoY and 3% QoQ. With this, the total NII for CY23 went up to PKR 165.4bn, 
marking a 72% YoY jump. Interest earned saw a jump of 66% during CY23 while 
interest expense was up by 61%.
•
NFI depicted an increase of 126% YoY | 18% QoQ during 4QCY23, taking CY23’s 
total to PKR 35.4bn (+37% YoY). This jump in NFI during CY23 was primarily due to 
higher fee and commission income which posted a jump of 44% YoY, clocking in at 
PKR 22.4bn. Moreover, dividend income too was up 18% in CY23, reaching PKR 
2.5bn. In addition, MCB posted gains on securities of PKR 837mn as compared to a 
loss recorded in SPLY of PKR 1.7bn.
•
MCB booked provisioning reversals of PKR 721mn during 4QCY23 (4QCY22 
reversals: PKR 1.3bn) depicting a decline of 46% on a YoY basis. This takes total 
provisioning to PKR 1.08bn during CY23 (CY22 reversals: PKR 2.6bn).
•
The bank’s OPEX increased 31% YoY in 4QCY23 clocking-in at PKR 17.7bn 
(4QCY22: PKR 13.5bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 30.9% in 4QCY23 against 
40.2% same period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 58% during 4QCY23 compared to 36% SPLY (4QCY22: 
49%). With this, the effective tax for CY23 stood at 53% (CY22: 54%).
1

## REPORT 149
Title: AHL Alert - DGKC : Result Review: FY23 LPS @ PKR 8.30
Created At: 2023-08-31T16:47:01Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 64f1a65456f0b360f1455ebe
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=300&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae

P a g e  1 | 1 
 
AHL Alert 
Cement 
D.G. Khan Cement Company Limited 
August 31, 2023 
 
D.G. Khan Cement Company Limited (DGKC) announced its financial result for FY23 today, posting a 
loss of PKR 3,636mn (LPS: PKR 8.30) compared to a profit of PKR 2,972mn (EPS: PKR 6.78) in SPLY, 
primarily owed to unprecedented tax amount charged of PKR 6,799mn. On Quarterly basis, loss arrived 
at PKR 5,748mn (LPS: PKR 13.12) in contrast to a loss of 647mn (LPS: PKR 1.48) SPLY.   
Result Highlights 
 
• Topline during FY23 clocked-in PKR 64,984mn, up by 12% YoY owed to a nearly 40% jump in 
retention prices which offset the impact of a 22% YoY decline in dispatches to 5,049k tons. In 
4QFY23, revenue rose by 15% YoY as robust growth in cement prices coupled with a rise in volumes 
to 1,340k tons (up by 3%) was witnessed. Rise in dispatches was mainly attributable to a massive 
surge in exports by 546% YoY. 
• Gross margins declined during FY23 by 326bps to settle at 14.7% on the back of higher electricity 
tariff and PKR depreciation. Margins during 4QFY23 clocked in at 10.7% vs 17.8%, the drop in 
margins was due to higher quantum of exports.  
• During 4QFY23 other income rose by 46% YoY to PKR 1,098mn vis-à-vis PKR 755mn SPLY. 
• Finance cost grew by 89% YoY to PKR 6,742mn in FY23 under review amid higher interest rates. 
• The massive increase in tax was due to super tax along with deferred tax adjustment.

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
Habib Bank Limited 
July 26, 2023 
Higher NII and lower taxation led to earnings jump in 2Q on YoY basis 
HBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 2QCY23 at PKR 13bn (EPS: PKR 8.86), depicting an 
increase of 281% YoY, while a decline of 2% QoQ (1QCY23 EPS: PKR 9.00). This jump in 
earnings during the out-going quarter was mainly led by higher net interest income. The bank 
announced DPS of PKR 2.0 for the quarter taking the half yearly payout to PKR 3.5. 
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 58.1bn during 2QCY23, increasing 54% YoY 
and 4% QoQ. While interest expense of the bank in 2QCY23 was up 77% YoY, interest 
income too remained high recording a 69% YoY depicting asset-repricing impact in the out-
going quarter. 
• 
NFI depicted a jump of 16% YoY | 81% QoQ to PKR 15.5bn during 2QCY23. This upsurge 
in NFI was primarily due to a huge gain on dividend income of PKR 976mn against a gain 
of PKR 155mn recorded SPLY. Moreover, the bank’s fee and commission income also 
recorded a jump of 35% YoY to record at PKR 10.4bn, further the bank also booked a gain 
on sale of securities of PKR 854mn during the out-going quarter. On the other hand, foreign 
exchange income posted a decline of 86% YoY, clocking-in at PKR 755mn during 2QCY23. 
• 
Provisioning for the bank jumped on YoY basis, up 81% while posting a decline of 11% QoQ 
to PKR 2.8bn during 2QCY23. 
• 
The bank’s OPEX increased 39% YoY | 3% QoQ in 2QCY23 clocking-in at PKR 40.7bn 
(2QCY22: PKR 29.3bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 55.4% in 2QCY23 against 57.7% 
same period last year.  
• 
Effective tax rate was set at 56.4% during 2QCY23 compared to 82.5% SPLY. 
 
 
Result review: 2QCY23 EPS @ PKR 8.86

P a g e  1 | 1 
 
AHL Alert 
Cement 
Lucky Cement Limited 
May 18, 2023 
 
  
 Capacity of Najmah-Al-Samawah to go up to 3.13mn tons  
Lucky Cement Limited (LUCK) has announced an expansion with its joint venture partner in Samawah, 
Iraq of 1.82mn tons, taking the total capacity under its clinker production company Najmah Al Samawah 
to 3.13mn tons.  
This is expected to take the total cement capacity of the group (including both local and foreign 
operations), to 21.48mn tons, whereby local capacity is set at 15mn tons, capacity at DR Congo is 1.31mn 
tons, and capacity in Basra, Iraq is 1.74mn tons while Samawah, Iraq will have a grinding capacity of 
3.13mn tons.  
Pertinently, this project will allow the company to secure clinker supply for its cement grinding facility in 
Basra, Iraq. We have attached the potential impact of the expansion on consolidated earnings of LUCK in 
a table based on the current parity. The management expects the plant to be completed in 18 months as 
per PSX notice, after commencement of construction in 1QFY24. We have assumed full year earnings 
will become visible in FY26. 
The company has a 50% stake in this JV, and based on last published accounts (FY22) whereby the 
company posted margins of 27%, and improving demand dynamics of Iraq, where we have seen margins 
as high as nearly 30% in the past, we estimate an impact of PKR 10.42 to 12.88/share on consolidated 
bottom-line of LUCK, augmenting PAT by 7-8% per annum against current year’s expected profitability. 
Please check the table on the right.

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Oil & Gas Exploration Companies 
Mari Petroleum Company Limited 
April 28, 2023 
3QFY23: Bottom-line ascended by 51% YoY to PKR 123.16/share 
Mari Petroleum Company Limited (MARI) announced its financial result today, posting a net profit 
of PKR 40,291mn (EPS: PKR 302.03) during 9MFY23 compared to PKR 27,459mn (EPS: PKR 
205.84) in 9MFY22, up by 47% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the company recorded a net profit of 
PKR 16,430mn (EPS: PKR 123.16), up by 51% YoY. 
 
Result Highlights 
 
 Net sales in 9MFY23 expanded by 46% YoY, settling at PKR 98,840mn compared to PKR 
67,617mn in SPLY amid i) 61% YoY surge in the wellhead price of Mari Gas Field, and ii) 27% 
YoY Pak Rupee depreciation against the greenback. During 3QFY23, the topline augmented 
by 50% YoY, arriving at PKR 37,838mn given i) 56% YoY higher wellhead gas price of Mari 
Gas Field, ii) 32% YoY devaluation of PKR against USD, and iii) 18% and 6% YoY growth in oil 
and gas production, respectively.  
 The exploration cost ascended by 111% YoY to PKR 9,539mn in 9MFY23 given three dry wells 
(Bazil X-1, Sundha Thal-1 and Shahpurabad-1) incurred during the period. Meanwhile, the 
exploration cost during 3QFY23 arrived at PKR 3,816mn, up by 104% YoY owing to higher 
seismic activity during the quarter. 
 The finance income arrived at PKR 6,858mn in 9MFY23 versus PKR 2,565mn in SPLY, up by 
167% YoY on account of higher income on cash and cash balances coupled with exchange gain 
on foreign currency. Whereas, finance income during 3QFY23 settled at PKR 4,644mn, depicting 
a massive growth of 5.5x YoY owing to immense exchange gain of ~PKR 2.5bn along with 
interest income during the quarter. 
 The company booked effective taxation at 33% in 3QFY23 vis-à-vis 27% in 3QFY22.  
 
Result Review: EPS of PKR 302.03 in 9MFY23

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
Bank Alfalah Limited 
July 29, 2022 
2QCY22: Profitability up YoY despite higher taxation  
BAFL announced earnings (PAT) today for 2QCY22 at PKR 3.7bn (EPS: PKR 2.10), depicting an 
increase of 4% YoY | -26% QoQ (2QCY21 EPS: PKR 2.02). However, the profit before tax was 
significantly up 55% YoY | 18% QoQ during the quarter. The bank also announced a DPS of PKR 
2.50.  
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 17.8bn during 2QCY22, increasing 52% YoY and 
25% QoQ. With this, total NII for 1HCY22 went up to PKR 32.1bn, marking a 45% YoY jump. 
Interest expense saw significant jump of 124% YoY depicting impact of policy rate hikes, in the 
out-going quarter.  
• 
NFI depicted an increase of 61% YoY during 2QCY22 taking 1HCY22’s total to PKR 11.4bn 
(38% YoY). This jump in 1HCY22 is primarily due to a huge FX gains of PKR 3.4bn (+209% 
YoY) on the back of PKR depreciation. In addition, fee and commission income posted a 29% 
YoY, clocking-in at PKR 2.8bn during 1HCY22. On the other hand, bank booked loss on 
securities in 1HCY22 of PKR 95mn against a gain of PKR 768mn recorded in same period last 
year.  
• 
Provisioning for the bank clocked-in at PKR 4.04bn during 1HCY22 (2QCY22: PKR 3.7bn) 
depicting a significant jump of 251% on YoY basis. 
• 
The bank’s OPEX increased 26% YoY in 1HCY22 clocking-in at PKR 22.3bn (2QCY22: PKR 
11.8bn, 29% YoY | 12% QoQ). With this, Cost/Income stood at 47.6% in 2QCY22 against 56.8% 
same period last year.  
• 
Effective tax rate was set at 60% during 2QCY22 compared to 36% last quarter. This higher 
taxation is on account of revised corporate and super tax. 
 
 
Result Review: 2QCY22 EPS @ PKR 2.10

## REPORT 154
Title: AHL Alert: MLCF - Earnings for 1QFY26 registered a 103% YoY growth
Created At: 2025-10-17T13:21:49.117Z
Tickers: AHL
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 68f242edd5d1fae29bbadc40
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68f242edd5d1fae29bbadc40.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
1QFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 2.60/share
Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited (MLCF) announced its financial results for 1QFY26
today, reporting consolidated earnings of PKR 2.7bn (EPS: PKR 2.60), showcasing a jump of
103% YoY. Higher earnings are due to higher Net Revenue, improved margins, lower
distribution and finance costs.
Result Highlights
•
During 1QFY26, revenue increased by 5% YoY to PKR 16.5bn compared to PKR 15.7bn
in 1QFY25. The improvement was driven primarily by higher domestic dispatches which
increased by 22% YoY to 0.95mn tons. However, sales fell 5% QoQ amid a drop in local
retention prices.
•
Gross margins improved to 33.9% in 1QFY26 (up from 31.6% last year), aided by a better
fuel and power mix and improved domestic dispatches. However, gross margins were
lower compared to 40.5% recorded in 4QFY25. Lower margins are due to company’s
newer and more efficient production lines facing turnaround and higher production taking
place at older lines.
•
Selling and distribution expenses decreased by 43% YoY in 1QFY26, settling at PKR
774mn. Meanwhile, expenses are down by 20% on a QoQ basis. Decline in distribution
costs is due to company switching from prior direct distribution model.
•
Finance costs declined sharply, down 50% YoY in 1QFY26. This is attributed to lower
interest rates and the company not having any short term borrowings on its balance sheet
compared to PKR 4.7bn in 1QFY25.
•
Other income increased by 9x to PKR 498mn in 1QFY26. Increase is due to higher cash
and short term investments of PKR 14.6bn compared to PKR 6.8bn in 1QFY25.
•
The company book effective taxation at 32.9% in 1QFY26 vis-à-vis 36.1% in 1QFY25.
REP-300
1
Nasheed Malik
D: +92 21 32461106
UAN: +92 21 111 245 111, Ext: 242
nasheed.malik@arifhabibltd.com
AHL Alert | 17-Oct-2025

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Automobile Assembler 
Pak Suzuki Motor Company Limited 
March 20, 2023 
 
 Bottom-line turns red 
  Pak Suzuki Motor Company Limited (PSMC) announced its financial result for 4QCY22 whereby the 
company posted a loss after tax (LAT) of PKR 3,830mn (LPS: PKR 46.5) against a profit after tax (PAT) 
of PKR 489mn (EPS: PKR 5.9) during 4QCY21, mainly due to an enormous jump (+13x YoY) in the 
finance cost along with huge tax charge (+15x YoY). This took the loss in CY22 to PKR 6,337mn (LPS: 
PKR 77) vs. a PAT of PKR 2,679mn (EPS: PKR 32.6) during CY21. 
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net sales jumped up by 37% YoY to PKR 60bn in 4QCY22, mainly due to higher car prices which 
offset the impact of slowdown in offtake. In addition, the PSMC plant was shut down in the month 
of Oct’22 due to lower availability of the raw material on the back of SBP’s restrictive measures. 
• 
Gross margins arrived at 9.85% during 4QCY22 vis-à-vis 3.55% in SPLY, given strong topline 
growth as increase in car prices managed to counter the impact of hike in other costs and 
volumetric decline.  
• 
Other income declined by 37% YoY compared to SPLY, majorly due to declining cash and cash 
equivalents.  
• 
During 4QCY22, finance cost increased by 13x YoY to PKR 4.9bn, whereby the company has 
potentially recognized an exchange loss of PKR 3.55bn on outstanding foreign liabilities of USD 
184mn (up to 31st Dec’22). In addition, PSMC booked markup paid on account of late delivery of 
vehicles. 
• 
Company charged a massive tax of PKR 3.4bn in CY22; we await guidance from the management 
for this massive increase.  
• 
In another notice today to the PSX, PSMC has mentioned that it has an outstanding foreign 
liability of USD 218mn as of now, and the company has unrealized loss of ~PKR 9bn, which may 
possibility hurt the profitability, and hence the equity of the company during CY23. 
 
 
Result Review: 4QCY22 LPS @ PKR 46.5

AHL Alert 
Oil & Gas Exploration Companies 
Pakistan Oilfields Limited 
February 28, 2023 
2QFY23: Bottom-line up by 5% YoY  
Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after tax of PKR 
14,353mn (EPS: PKR 50.57) during 1HFY23 compared to PKR 10,923mn (EPS: PKR 38.48) in 
1HFY22, up by 31% YoY. On a quarterly basis, earnings during 2QFY23 settled at PKR 5,954mn 
(EPS: PKR 20.98), up by 5% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced an interim cash 
dividend of PKR 20.00/share in 2QFY23 (PKR 20.00/share in 2QFY22). 
 
Result Highlights 
 
• Net sales in 1HFY23 climbed up by 27% YoY, clocking-in at PKR 29,998mn in contrast to PKR 
23,687mn during SPLY on account of i) 18% YoY increase in average realized oil prices, and ii) 
24% YoY weak Pak Rupee. The topline in 2QFY23 arrived at PKR 14,021mn, witnessing a 
growth of 5% YoY amid 8% YoY higher realized oil prices coupled with 22% YoY appreciation of 
PKR against USD. Meanwhile, oil and gas production depicted a decline of 9.8% and 8.9% YoY, 
respectively. 
• The exploration costs surged by 9.8x YoY in 1HFY23, arriving at PKR 5,480mn against PKR 
559mn in SPLY, amid two dry wells incurred during the period. Meanwhile, the exploration costs 
during 2QFY23 reached PKR 954mn, up by a massive 8.5x YoY owing to dry well Tarnol-01 
incurred during the quarter. 
• The other income registered a growth of 85% YoY, settling at PKR 5,480mn during 1HFY23 on 
account of exchange gain on foreign currency. Whereas, other income during 2QFY23 arrived 
at PKR 2,104mn, up by 4% YoY owed to higher income from cash and cash equivalents. 
• The company’s effective taxation remained arrived at 36% in 2QFY23, similar to 2QFY22.

AHL Alert 
Automobile Assembler 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited 
January 26, 2023 
 
Improved gross margins led to jump in profitability YoY 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for 3QMY23 on 26th Jan’23 
whereby the company posted a Profit After Tax (PAT) of PKR 811mn (EPS: PKR 5.68), representing 
a YoY increase of 82%. The significant surge in profit was attributed to improved margins, which were 
recorded up by 440bps on a sequential bases. This brought the profit in 9MMY23 to PKR 1,084mn 
(EPS: PKR 7.59) as compared to a PAT of PKR 2,313mn (EPS: PKR 16.2) during 9MMY22. 
Result Highlights 
 
 
Net sales declined to PKR 22,949mn during 3QMY23, down by 22% YoY, owing to lower 
volumetric sales (-47% YoY) and the impact of import restrictions imposed by the SBP, which 
was partially offset by higher car prices.  
 
Gross margins stood at 7.8% during 3QMY23, as compared to 2.7% in SPLY, depicting an 
improvement of 550bps. This was attributed to augmented car prices coupled with lower 
commodity prices, especially steel. For similar reasons, margins jumped up significantly over last 
quarter (2QMY23: 3.4%).  
 
Other income depicted a decline of 39% YoY during 3QMY23, as the cash and short-term 
investments held by the company declined during the period under review.  
 
The company booked effective taxation at 31% in 3QMY23. 
Result Highlights 
Currently, we have a ‘Sell’ stance on the stock with Dec’23 target price of PKR 109/share.

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
Bank Alfalah Limited 
November 10, 2022 
BAFL decides to buy back shares up to 200mn 
BAFL announced today its decision to buy back, up to 200mn of ordinary shares which is equivalent 
to 11.25% of paid-up capital and 32% of free-float.  
Purpose of Buy back: As per the notice, the purpose of buying back is cancellation of shares. 
Source of Funds: Distributable profit 
Buyback dates: December 14, 2022 to June 02, 2023 
Impact of buy back: 
 
 
The probable impact of repurchasing of shares includes: i) upward revision of financial ratios 
primarily the ones based on number of shares outstanding (e.g. EPS, ROE, and BVPS) - same has 
been stated by the management in their notice published today, ii) help improve valuation of the 
stock and, iii) from sponsor’s standpoint, increase their stake in the bank. 
 
We have worked out a sensitivity (refer table on the right) covering major financial multiples and the 
potential impact of buy back on them.  
 
As evident in the table, financial metrics are likely to improve with PKR 10 impact expected on the 
BVPS, ROE to hover in the range of 17-18% and EPS to remain north of 14%. Moreover, on the 
capital front, BAFL seems to be comfortably above the regulatory requirement of 11.5% given the 
assumptions stated on the right. To recall, in its Analyst Briefing post 3QCY22 results, the 
management mentioned that the Bank will be issuing PKR 7bn worth Additional Tier-1 instrument in 
4QCY22 which, we believe, would help improve capital position of the bank. 
 
With share trading at a discount of 40% from its 5-year P/B average of 0.7x, BAFL’s stock is currently 
undervalued. This decision to repurchase its shares should help improve the valuation of the bank.
