## REPORT 160
Title: AHL Alert - SYS EPS clocked-in at PKR 7.49/share in CY25
Created At: 2026-04-07T06:31:31.982Z
Tickers: SYS
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 69d4a4c3b56b444d53d157ba
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69d4a4c3b56b444d53d157ba.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 07-Apr-2026 
Systems Limited
Result Review: CY25 EPS clocked-in at PKR 7.49/share
REP-300
Result Highlights
•
Systems Limited (SYS) announced the financial result for CY25 whereby the
company posted a PAT of PKR 11,041mn (EPS: PKR 7.49) against a PAT of
PKR 7,460mn (EPS: 5.06) during CY24, up by 48% YoY. Whereas, earnings in
4QCY25 clocked in at PKR 3,073mn (EPS: PKR 2.09), up by 60% YoY. In
addition to the result, the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR
2.00/share (PKR 2.00/share in CY25).
•
Net sales for CY25 reached PKR 80,392mn, marking a 19% YoY increase, driven
by strong growth in Telecommunications (+31% YoY), Technology (+18% YoY),
BFSI (+17% YoY). Revenue contribution by region was led by the Middle East at
59%, followed by North America at 18%, Pakistan at 14%, Europe at 5%, and
APAC at 4%. On a quarterly basis, 4QCY25 topline surged 20% YoY to PKR
22,973mn. In our view, the growth in 4QCY25 also reflects the impact of the
strategic partnership with British American Tobacco (BAT), as the company
confirmed in its latest CBS that operations commenced in Nov’25.
•
Gross margins in CY25 arrived at 28% vis-à-vis 24% in CY24. This improvement
in gross margins was due to operational efficiencies. The gross margins in
4QCY25 arrived at 30% (up by 718bps YoY).
•
Other income in 4QCY25 declined by 65% YoY to PKR 148mn, largely
attributable to unfavorable currency movements. However during CY25, other
income improved by 17% YoY mainly due to higher exchange gains.
•
Finance cost increased by 13% YoY, clocking in at PKR 91mn during 4QCY25
due to increase in short term borrowings.
•
The company booked an effective taxation at 15% in 4QCY25 vs 2% in SPLY.
•
We have a “BUY” stance on SYS, the stock is trading at CY26e and CY27f
multiple of 11.5 and 8.7x.
Menka Kirpalani
UAN: (021) 3828 0256
menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
National Bank of Pakistan  
October 28, 2022 
3QCY22: Higher taxation and lower NFI dented profitability 
NBP announced earnings (PAT) today for 3QCY22 at PKR 7bn (EPS: PKR 3.29), depicting a 
decrease of 7% YoY, while an increase of 179% QoQ (3QCY21 EPS: PKR 3.55). In addition, the 
profit before tax was up by 19% YoY, however it declined by 19% QoQ, during the quarter.   
Result Highlights 
 
 
Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 152bn during 3QCY22, increasing 161% YoY and 
52% QoQ. With this, the total interest income for 9MCY22 went up to PKR 332bn, marking 
a 99% YoY jump. Interest expense registered a significant increase of 274% YoY in the out-
going quarter.  
 
Non-Interest income went down during the quarter, 17% YoY | 26% QoQ, taking 9MCY22’s 
total to PKR 27.4bn. The downfall in the out-going quarter was majorly contributed by FX 
income, which went down by 52% YoY; however, Fee income and Dividend income went up 
by 9% YoY and 14% YoY, respectively. 
 
During 3QCY22, provisioning significantly declined 70% YoY, while on QoQ basis, it went up 
by 164%. This takes the overall provisioning of the bank to PKR 3.5bn in 9MCY22, -70% 
YoY. 
 
The bank’s OPEX increased 15% YoY in 3QCY22 clocking-in at PKR 19.3bn (9MCY22: PKR 
55.8bn, 16% YoY). With this, Cost/Income stood at 54.5% in 3QCY22 against 48.4% same 
period last year.  
 
Effective tax rate was significantly down during 3QCY22 at 51.8% compared to 86% last 
quarter.

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Oil & Gas Marketing Companies 
Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited 
September 28, 2022 
 
Sui Norther Gas Pipelines Limited (SNGP) announced the financial result for 9MFY22 today, posting a 
profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 9,437mn (EPS: PKR 14.88) compared to PKR 8,939mn (EPS: PKR 14.09) 
in SPLY. During the second quarter of FY22, the company posted earnings of PKR 3,854mn (EPS: PKR 
6.08) while profitability for 3QFY22 arrived at PKR 2,551mn (EPS: PKR 4.02). In addition, SNGP 
announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 2.50/share vs. PKR 2.00/share last year.  
    Result Highlights 
 
 During 9MFY22 sales underwent a noteworthy growth of 59% YoY primarily aided by a nearly 80% 
jump in RLNG prices in lieu of rising international Brent Oil price (up by 67% YoY) to USD 83.48/bbl 
vs. USD 49.84/bbl in SPLY. This offset the impact of a 4% decline in RLNG imports. Moreover, 
domestic volumes also posted a decline in our view amid declining local gas reserves, but we await 
full year accounts to quantify the same.  
 Operating profit of the company settled at PKR 49.8bn in 9MFY22 vis-à-vis PKR 41.6bn in SPLY 
given lower charge of unaccounted for gas losses (UFG) led by effective UFG control plan, and 
shrunk domestic volumes which countered the impact of a higher weighted average cost of gas.  
 Finance costs displayed a jump of 24% YoY in FY22 to PKR 36bn primarily owed to a sharp hike 
in the benchmark policy rate by the SBP during the year.  
 The company booked effective taxation at 33% during 9MFY22 (9MFY21: 30%). 
Other highlights 
 One auditor note has been highlighted, that is Note 14.1 of the financial statements, which explains 
that the settlement of the circular debt including tariff adjustment relies on resolution of outstanding 
balances owed by the Government of Pakistan, and increase in gas prices / subsidy by the 
government. The report however, is not modified.

## REPORT 163
Title: AHL Alert: HUBC - Result Review: 9MFY24 EPS @ PKR 38.20
Created At: 2024-04-29T12:47:12.846Z
Tickers: HUBC
Sectors: 0824
Item ID: 662f96d0b3858eb2c732927d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662f96d0b3858eb2c732927d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
29 April 2024
HUBC: 3QFY24 earnings clock in at PKR 13.25/share, surge of 53% YoY
The Hub Power Company Limited (HUBC) announced its 9MFY24 financial result today where 
the company posted consolidated earnings of PKR 49,547mn (EPS: PKR 38.20), up by 47% 
YoY compared to PKR 33,647mn (EPS: PKR 25.94) during 9MFY23. In 3QFY24 profitability 
increased by 53% YoY arriving at PKR 17,186mn (EPS: PKR 13.25) compared to SPLY. 
Along with the result, the company also announced a cash dividend of PKR 2.50/share 
(9MFY24: PKR 11.50/share).
Result Highlights 
•
During 9MFY24, net sales witnessed an increase of 16% YoY to PKR 95,434mn 
attributable to higher dispatches from TEL (+76% YoY) as compared to SPLY and the 
devaluation of PKR against USD. Moreover, in 3QFY24 the topline rose by 17% YoY, due 
aforementioned reason, to settle at PKR 31,791mn as compared to PKR 27,231mn in 
SPLY.
•
During 9QFY24, the company’s gross margins increased by 866bps YoY to 52%, mainly 
attributable to the zero load factor of the Hub base plant during the period as compared to 
196 GwH dispatched during SPLY. In 3QFY24 the gross margins arrived at 52% vis-à-vis 
48% in SPLY.
•
The company recognized share of profit from associate and joint venture of PKR 34,703mn 
during 9MFY24 compared to PKR 19,553mn during 9QFY23, a jump of 77% YoY, on the 
back of devaluation of PKR against the greenback and profitability coming from TNPTL, 
which achieved CoD on 17th Feb’23. In 3QFY24 share of profit surged by 87% YoY to 
settle at PKR 12,104mn, amid aforementioned reasons.
•
Finance cost during 9MFY24 rose by 74% YoY to PKR 20,643mn due to higher interest 
rates and the addition of TEL’s finance cost. In 3QFY24 finance cost jumped by 40% YoY, 
amid higher borrowing costs.
REP-300
The Hub Power Company Limited
Result Review: 9MFY24 EPS @ PKR 38.20

P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Commercial Banks 
Faysal Bank Limited 
August 25, 2022 
FABL earnings up by 9% YoY in 2QCY22 
FABL announced earnings today for 2QCY22 at PKR 2.2bn (EPS: PKR 1.41), depicting an uptick of 
10% YoY| -3% QoQ (1QCY22 EPS: PKR 1.46). The rise in YoY earnings is primarily due to a jump in 
total income as well as reversal in provisioning charge. The bank also announced a DPS of PKR 0.5 
along with the result.  
Result Highlights 
 
• 
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 9.1bn during 2QCY22, increasing 40% YoY and 
26% QoQ. Interest expense was up 141% YoY as most likely impact of the interest rate hikes 
were accounted for in the out-going quarter while interest income was up 89% YoY. With this, 
NII during 1HCY22 posted a jump of 36% YoY. 
• 
NFI depicted an increase of 13% YoY in 2QCY22 primarily due to increase in Fee, Dividend and 
FX income by 31%, 119% and 67% (all on YoY basis), respectively. In addition, the bank also 
booked income from derivatives of PKR 45mn as against a loss in SPLY of PKR 13mn. On the 
contrary, loss on securities was posted by the bank this quarter of PKR 640mn against a gain 
booked SPLY of PKR 47mn.  
• 
FABL booked a reversal in provisioning during 2QCY22 of PKR 259mn against a charge of PKR 
521mn recorded in SPLY. With this, the total reversals in provisioning charge for 1HCY22 stands 
at PKR 406mn (+34x YoY).  
• 
The banks OPEX increased 30% YoY | 9% QoQ during 2QCY22. With this, Cost/Income stood 
at 57% in 2QCY22 against 58% SPLY.  
• 
Effective tax rate was set at ~58% during 2QCY22 compared to 37% SPLY. 
 
Result Review: 2QCY22 EPS @ PKR 1.41

## REPORT 165
Title: AHL Alert: EFERT - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 7.06 in 1HCY24
Created At: 2024-07-31T05:04:33.001Z
Tickers: EFERT
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 66a9c5e16a3cb5cde9345aed
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a9c5e16a3cb5cde9345aed.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
31 July 2024
2QCY24: Bottom-line witnessed a growth of 57% YoY
Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) today unveiled the financial result for 1HCY24 posting a 
consolidated profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 9,426mn (EPS: PKR 7.06) against PKR 5,464mn 
(EPS: PKR 4.09) in 1HCY23, up by 73% YoY. Whereas, consolidated earnings in 2QCY24 
clocked in at PKR 1,666mn (EPS: PKR 1.25), augmenting by 57% YoY. Alongside the result, 
the EFERT announced a cash dividend of PKR 3.00/share (PKR 11.00/share in 1HCY24). 
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales in 1HCY24 clocked in at PKR 113,204mn, up by 37% YoY which is on account of 
62% and 19% YoY hike in urea and DAP price tagged with 54% YoY jump in DAP offtake. 
On a quarterly basis, the topline during 2QCY24 witnessed an uptick of 3% YoY, settling at 
PKR 39,421mn, which is due to 59% and 15% YoY higher urea and DAP prices, respectively 
coupled with 16% YoY jump in DAP sales. Meanwhile, urea offtake contracted by 36% YoY 
due to 58 days maintenance at EnVen plant.
•
Gross margins in 1HCY24 arrived at 21.49% vis-à-vis 26.95% in 1HCY23. Whereas, gross 
margins in 2QCY24 arrived at 18.10% (down by 1,167bps YoY) owed to lower urea offtake.
•
Other income swelled up by 75% YoY settling at PKR 2,032mn in 1HCY24 given higher 
income from cash and cash balances. Meanwhile, in 2QCY24, the other income came out to 
be PKR 745mn, up by 67% YoY due to the aforementioned reason. 
•
Finance cost climbed up by 22% YoY, clocking in at PKR 1,386mn during 1HCY24 owed to 
higher interest rates. Whereas, the finance cost in 2QCY24 swelled up by 74% YoY, 
reaching PKR 1,225mn.    
•
The company booked effective taxation at 35% in 2QCY24 vis-à-vis 85% in 2QCY23. To 
recall, during 2QCY23 an additional 6% super tax was levied on the profit before tax of CY22 
and 1HCY23, respectively.
REP-300
Engro Fertilizers Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 7.06 in 1HCY24

## REPORT 166
Title: Brief Note: EFERT - 1HCY22 Result Review and Analyst Briefing Takeaways
Created At: 2022-07-28T15:16:44Z
Tickers: Not stated
Sectors: Not stated
Item ID: 6491e96d54aca318bf8b2d5d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=300&category=648c11940ffa16ff648002ad
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/6491e96d54aca318bf8b2d5d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
 
 
 
 
 
 
P a g e  1 | 2 
 
July 28, 2022 
AHL Brief Note 
Fertilizer 
Engro Fertilizers Limited 
 
 
2QCY22: EFERT books a loss of PKR 0.07/share 
The management of Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) announced the financial result for 2QCY22 and held an 
analyst briefing session today. The company posted a consolidated loss after Tax (LAT) of PKR 98mn (LPS: PKR 
0.07) compared to PKR 4,768mn (EPS: PKR 3.56) in SPLY on account of imposition of super tax during the quarter 
tagged with higher other operating expenses. On a cumulative basis, consolidated earnings in 1HCY22 settle at 
PKR 5,413 (EPS: PKR 4.05), down by 48% YoY. 
 
Results Review and Brief Takeaways 
 
Net Sales in 2QCY22 clocked-in PKR 38,323mn, up by 48% YoY amid surge in urea and DAP prices by 
22% and 79% YoY, respectively alongside a growth in DAP offtake by 30% YoY. Meanwhile, urea offtake 
went down by 3% YoY. Consequently, the topline during 1HCY22 arrived at PKR 75,136mn, up by 36% 
YoY which is attributable to a 16% jump in DAP offtake couple with an 18% and 87% YoY hike urea and 
DAP prices, respectively. 
 
Gross margins arrived at 30.51% (down by 750bps YoY) in 2QCY22 as the company accrued industrial 
feed gas rate at the EnVen plant since concessionary gas period ended on 30th Jun’21. 
 
Other income showcased an increase of 20% YoY arriving at PKR 588mn in 2QCY22 owed to higher 
income from cash and cash balances. 
 
Other expenses witnessed a whopping 211% YoY jump in 2QCY22 to PKR 1,404mn on account of an 
exchange loss on imports (which according to the company management is around PKR 750mn).  
 
Finance cost ascended by 2% YoY to PKR 583mn during 2QCY22 given higher interest rates. 
 
The company booked effective taxation at 101% in 2QCY22 vis-à-vis 28% in 2QCY21.The taxation during 
the quarter includes super tax levied on the profit before tax of CY21 and 1HCY22, coupled the with 
deferred tax liability adjustment. As per the management, deferred taxation amount the quarter was PKR 
1.6bn.   
 
The management told that capital gain is now taxed at the corporate tax of 29%.  
 
The management told that exemption of output sales tax by the government has increased burden on cost, 
since input sales tax was adjusted against it. 
 
The management re-iterated that increase price of urea bag of PKR 350/bag was primarily because of i) output 
sales tax exemption, ii) escalating financing cost and iii) higher PP12 gas prices.  
 
Regarding BMR on base plant, the management told that it is expected in Sep’22 or Oct’22. The management 
further added that BMR will go on for two months’ maximum and during this time the base plant will be closed. 
 
The management informed that the EnVen plant witnessed an unforeseen breakdown which has now been 
resolved. Due to this, the management incurred a production loss of ~111 KT.  
 
 
 
1HCY22 Result Review and Analyst Briefing Takeaways

 P a g e  1 | 1 
AHL Alert 
Oil and Gas Marketing Companies 
Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited 
June 17, 2022 
                                                     
As per the latest notice disseminated to the stock exchange by Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited (SNGP), the company’s Board has approved to lay a 295 KMs gas pipeline to add newly 
discovered 50-70 MMCFD gas in the vicinity of North Waziristan and Lakki Marwat area to the system. Estimated cost of the pipeline is set at PKR 23.4bn and assuming annual depreciation 
at 7%, ROA at 16.6%, UFG at 8% (with ~7% approved by OGRA upon meeting KPIs), finance cost at 15% of the assumed 70% debt component, this project will generate a post-tax return 
of PKR 820mn. This translates to a per share impact of PKR 1.06. The project is subject to approval of the regulatory authority (OGRA). 
Apart from SNGP, local steel pipe manufacturers, such as INIL or CSAP, can also benefit from the construction of this pipeline.

## REPORT 168
Title: AHL Alert: ILP - Result Review: Lowest profitability since 2QFY23
Created At: 2024-10-24T12:54:11.369Z
Tickers: ILP
Sectors: 0829
Item ID: 671a43738f84bab98c35a240
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671a43738f84bab98c35a240.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
24 October 2024
1QFY25: Profitability arrives at PKR 0.16/share
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced the financial result for 1QFY25 today, where the 
company posted a PAT of PKR 222mn (EPS: PKR 0.16) against PKR 6,042mn (EPS: 
PKR 4.31) in 1QFY24, down by 96% YoY. 
Result Highlights 
•
The company posted net sales of PKR 41,635mn in 1QFY25, reflecting an 8% 
YoY growth compared to PKR 38,500mn in 1QFY24. This increase was mainly 
driven by enhanced production capacities at the Hosiery and Apparel plants. 
However, the 5% YoY appreciation of the PKR tempered growth, partially 
offsetting the impact of higher production.
•
Gross margins for 1QFY25 dropped to 18.6% from 33.1% in SPLY, mainly due to 
higher depreciation from the newly commissioned Apparel plant and higher 
energy tariff which when up to PKR 34/unit as compared to PKR 28/unit in SPLY.
•
Distribution costs surged by 33% YoY to PKR 1,831mn in 1QFY25, compared to 
PKR 1,416mn in 1QFY24. The rise in expenses can be attributed to higher 
shipping costs linked to improved sales of the Apparel and Hosiery segment. 
•
Other income exhibited a significant rise of 112% YoY, reaching PKR 277mn 
during 1QFY25 compared to PKR 131mn in the SPLY. 
•
Finance costs in 1QFY25 settled at PKR 2,851mn vis-à-vis PKR 2,206mn in 
SPLY, an increase of 29% YoY due to elevated short-term borrowings
•
The company recorded an effective tax rate of 74.4% in 1QFY25, a sharp 
increase compared to 7.8% in the same period last year. This significant jump is 
primarily due to a change in the tax regime, effective from July 1, 2024, which 
shifted the company from the final tax regime to a normal tax regime (29% 
corporate tax + 10% super tax). 
REP-300
Interloop Limited
Result Review: Lowest profitability since 2QFY23

## REPORT 169
Title: AHL Alert: LCI - Result Review: EPS settled at PKR 68.2 in 1HFY25
Created At: 2025-01-28T05:35:07.508Z
Tickers: LCI
Sectors: 0805
Item ID: 67986c8b6ae6aa2b532c6512
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67986c8b6ae6aa2b532c6512.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 January 2025
Profitability surged by 47% YoY to PKR 39.82/share in 2QFY25
Lucky Core Industries (LCI) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after tax
of PKR 6,301mn (EPS: PKR 68.23) during 1HFY25, augmenting by 25% YoY amid
higher gross margins and elevated other income. On a quarterly basis, net profit during
2QFY25 arrived at PKR 3,677mn (EPS: PKR 39.82), up by 47% YoY. In addition to the
result, the company announced an interim cash dividend of PKR 34.00/share in 2QFY25
(PKR 27.00/share in 2QFY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 1HFY25 witnessed an increase of 4% YoY, settling at PKR 62,246mn
compared to PKR 60,058mn during SPLY. Moreover, topline in 2QFY25 arrived at PKR
31,517mn, up by 1% YoY
•
Gross margins increased by 150bps YoY to reach 22.9% in 1HFY25. This improvement
was primarily driven by a 5.3% YoY decline in coal prices. Additionally, the deregulation
of non-essential medicines contributed positively to the gross margin growth in the
pharma
sector.
Notably,
gross
margins
in
2QFY25
saw
a
more
significant
improvement, rising by 270bps, largely due to these factors.
•
Other income grew by 28% YoY, reaching PKR 2,444mn in 1HFY25, driven by higher
earnings from cash and cash equivalents, reflecting improved short-term investments.
Moreover, other income during 2QFY25 arrived at PKR 1,617mn, portraying a surge of
62% YoY.
•
Finance costs decreased by 35% YoY to PKR 1,300mn in 1HFY25, primarily due to the
favorable decline in interest rates.
•
The company’s effective taxation arrived at 36.7% in 2QFY25 vis-à-vis 35.1% in
2QFY24.

## REPORT 170
Title: AHL Alert: OGDC - Result Review: Earnings clocked in at PKR 48.59 in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-23T10:30:21.269Z
Tickers: OGDC
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 66f1433d0cbf3cea06cf8d4f
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66f1433d0cbf3cea06cf8d4f.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
23 September 2024
Profitability declined by 42% YoY to PKR 8.81/share in 4QFY24
Oil & Gas Development Company Limited (OGDC) announced its financial result today,
posting earnings of PKR 208,976mn (EPS: PKR 48.59) during FY24, down by 7% YoY.
On a quarterly basis, the company’s profitability arrived at PKR 37,872mn (EPS: PKR
8.81), plummeting by 42% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced a final
cash dividend of PKR 4.00/share (PKR 10.10/share in FY24).
Result Highlights
•
Topline in FY24 climbed up by 12% YoY, clocking in at PKR 463,698mn compared to
PKR 413,594mn in SPLY, owing to i) 2% YoY increase in oil production, and ii) PKR
depreciation against USD by 12% YoY. During 4QFY24, the net sales increased by
11% YoY, settling at PKR 115,534mn, amid i) 5% YoY jump in oil production, and ii)
9% YoY hike in oil prices.
•
The exploration costs declined by 34% YoY to PKR 12,561mn in FY24. Whereas, the
exploration costs during 4QFY24 dwindled by 50% YoY, which is on account of lower
cost of dry well “Badhra Dir-16” compared to dry well “Chak 20-01” in SPLY.
•
Other income clocked in at PKR 41,344mn in FY24, down by 73% YoY. Whereas, the
company recorded other expense of PKR 3,197mn in 4QFY24 compared to other
income of PKR 89,508mn in SPLY, owing to i) exchange loss on Pak Rupee
appreciation, and ii) one-off gain on modification of finance lease of Uch Power Limited
I in 4QFY23.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 30% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 50% in 4QFY23.
REP-300
Oil & Gas Development Company Limited

## REPORT 171
Title: AHL Alert: MTL 1HFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 14.63
Created At: 2026-02-17T12:01:12.976Z
Tickers: MTL
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 699458882c2a6a5d49818f84
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699458882c2a6a5d49818f84.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Result Highlights
•
Millat Tractors Limited (MTL) announced its financial results for 1HFY26, posting a Profit
After Tax (PAT) of PKR 2,920mn (EPS: PKR 14.63), compared to PKR 3,645mn (EPS: PKR
18.27) in the same period last year, reflecting a decline of 20% YoY and 368% increase
QoQ. The company declared an interim dividend of PKR 20/share during the period.

Net revenue for 1HFY26 stood at PKR 28,437mn, reflecting a 3% YoY increase compared to
PKR 27,494mn in the same period last year. On a quarterly basis, net revenue for 2QFY26
rose 7% YoY to PKR 20,891mn, up from PKR 19,497mn in 2QFY25. Despite a 16% YoY
decline in sales volumes to 6,335 units in 2QFY26, the topline posted growth, driven by a
favorable product mix, with higher distribution of medium- and high-horsepower tractors under
the Punjab government’s tractor scheme.

Gross margins expanded to 33.0% in 1HFY26, compared to 26.5% in the SPLY. On a
quarterly basis, gross margins stood at 27.2% in 2QFY26, up from 25.0% in the same quarter
last year due to stable PKR-USD parity and increased number of high priced variants sold
under Punjab government tractor scheme.

Other income declined to PKR 85mn in 1HFY26 (–68% YoY) against PKR 266mn last year,
due to lower interest rates.

Meanwhile, finance cost fell 26% YoY to PKR 872mn (vs PKR 1,173mn in SPLY) due to
decline in overall borrowings and decline in interest rates.

Ultimately, PAT declined 20% YoY to PKR 2,920mn in 1HFY26. On a quarterly basis, PAT
decreased 21% YoY to PKR 2,406mn, compared to PKR 3,041mn in the same period last
year.

The company’s effective tax rate stood at 52.5% in 1HFY26 versus 7.5% in the same period
last year. In 2QFY26, the company reported an effective taxation rate of 35.0%.

The scrip is trading at FY26E and FY27F P/E multiples of 21.0x and 16.2x, respectively.

## REPORT 172
Title: AHL Alert: PSO - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 33.79 in FY24
Created At: 2024-08-27T09:35:16.86Z
Tickers: PSO
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 66cd9dd4311a3f7dccf022d6
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66cd9dd4311a3f7dccf022d6.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
27 August 2024
Profitability reports profitability at PKR 5.25/share in 4QFY24
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited (PSO) announced its financial result for FY24
posting a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 15.9bn (EPS: PKR 33.79) compared to PKR 5.7bn
(EPS: PKR 12.06) in SPLY, surging by 3x YoY. On a quarterly basis, the profitability
arrived at
PKR 2.5bn (EPS: PKR 5.25) in 4QFY24 compared to a loss of PKR 4.6bn
(LPS: PKR 9.85) in SPLY. Alongside the result, the company announced a final cash
dividend of PKR 10.00/share.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales arrived at PKR 3,572bn in FY24, up by 5% YoY given higher average selling
prices of petroleum products. However, the sales volume of petroleum products
declined by 9% YoY. Whereas, the topline in 4QFY24 climbed up by 3% YoY on the
back of hike in price of MS and HSD coupled with an increase in sales of MS and FO
by 1% and 32% YoY, respectively. Although we await disclosure detailed accounts, we
believe the revenue contribution from RLNG segment to be PKR 297bn (up 17% YoY)
given higher DES price (up by 5% YoY) tagged with handling of 29 cargos in 4QFY24
versus 25 cargos in SPLY.
•
The gross profit margin in FY24 arrived at 2.72% vis-à-vis 2.21% in SPLY amid
inventory gains during the period. During 4QFY24, the company posted gross profit
margins at 1.93% compared to 1.90% in SPLY due lower inventory gains.
•
Other income increased by 74% YoY in FY24 to PKR 23.6bn in contrast to PKR 13.5bn
in FY23. Whereas, in 4QFY24, the other income augmented by 5x YoY arriving at PKR
10.3bn given significant interest received on delayed payments.
•
The finance costs increased by 30% YoY to PKR 52.3bn in FY24 given higher short-
term borrowings.
•
The company booked a effective taxation at 74% in 4QFY24.
REP-300
Pakistan State Oil Company Limited

## REPORT 173
Title: AHL Alert: POL - Result Review: Earnings clocked in at PKR 137.93 in FY24
Created At: 2024-09-02T08:40:16.977Z
Tickers: POL
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 66d579f0311a3f7dcc1c6fd7
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66d579f0311a3f7dcc1c6fd7.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2 September 2024
Profitability surge by 57% YoY to PKR 32.45/share in 4QFY24
Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after
tax of PKR 39,152mn (EPS: PKR 137.93) during FY24 compared to PKR 36,453mn
(EPS: PKR 128.42) in FY23, up by 7% YoY. On a quarterly basis, net profit during
4QFY24 arrived at PKR 9,212mn (EPS: PKR 32.45), up by 57% YoY. In addition to the
result, the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR 70.00/share (PKR
95.00/share in FY24).
Result Highlights
•
Topline in FY24 witnessed a growth of 7% YoY, settling at PKR 65,290mn compared to
PKR 60,952mn during SPLY amid Pak Rupee depreciation by 12% YoY against
greenback. Whereas, oil and gas production witnessed a decrease of 5% YoY, each in
FY24. The net sales in 4QFY24 arrived at PKR 14,956mn, down by 1% YoY on the
back of i) 6% and 15% YoY decline in oil and gas production, respectively, and ii) Pak
Rupee appreciation against the greenback. Meanwhile, realized average oil prices
witnessed an uptick of 4% YoY during the quarter.
•
The exploration costs plummeted by 76% YoY in FY24, arriving at PKR 1,606mn, owed
to the absence of a dry well during the period. Meanwhile, the exploration costs during
4QFY24 reached PKR 160mn, down by 59% YoY due to a lower geological and
geophysical costs during the period.
•
The other income registered a decline of 39% YoY, settling at PKR 16,574mn during
FY24 given absence of exchange gain during the period. Whereas, other income during
4QFY24 arrived at PKR 4,821mn, up by 21% YoY amid higher income from cash and
cash balances.
•
The company’s effective taxation arrived at 28% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 52% in 4QFY23.

## REPORT 174
Title: AHL Alert: FFC - Result Review: EPS settled at PKR 9.33 in 1QCY25
Created At: 2025-04-28T13:35:17.769Z
Tickers: FFC
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 680f841583dc41f4304b5c41
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/680f841583dc41f4304b5c41.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 April 2025
REP-300
1
AHL Alert | 28-Apr-2025 
Fertilizer: Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited
Result Review: EPS settled at PKR 9.33 in 1QCY25

Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited (FFC) disclosed its financial result today, posting a
net profit of PKR 13,278mn (EPS: PKR 9.33) during 1QCY25, depicting a decline of
10% YoY. On a sequential basis, the profitability reduced by 6% QoQ. The
company announced a interim cash dividend of PKR 7.00/share in 1QCY25 (PKR
4.92/share in 1QCY24).

Net sales plummeted by 39% YoY during 1QCY25, which is mainly attributable to a
fall in offtake of prilled urea, granular urea, local DAP, and imported DAP by 24%,
46%, 62%, and 35% YoY, respectively. Similarly, the revenue on a QoQ basis
dwindled by 58% primarily due to a 27%, 77%, 71%, and 78% QoQ reduction in
dispatches
of
prilled
urea,
granular
urea,
local
DAP,
and
imported
DAP,
respectively.

The gross margins arrived at 35.6% in 1QCY25 compared 24.8% in SPLY
supported by i) absence of higher cost of imported urea, ii) higher urea prices, and
iii) lower DAP offtake.

Financial charges decreased by 22% YoY, clocking in at PKR 1,699mn in 1QCY25
amid lower interest rates.

Other income contracted by 48% YoY, given lower income from cash and cash
equivalents and dividend income from associates, and subsidiaries.

The company booked effective taxation at 35% in 1QCY25 vis-à-vis 44% in
1QCY24.

As of Mar’25, the company’s cash position stood at PKR 147bn compared to PKR
216bn in Dec’24.
Recommendation
•
We have “BUY” call on the scrip with a Dec’25 target price of PKR 414.5/share,
trading at a forward P/E multiples of 6.7x for CY25 and 6.5x for CY26

## REPORT 177
Title: AHL Alert - SAZEW posts highest ever quarterly PAT in 3QFY26
Created At: 2026-04-20T09:17:32.89Z
Tickers: SAZEW
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 69e5ef2c1f328d6e65029938
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69e5ef2c1f328d6e65029938.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 20-Apr-2026 
Sazgar Engineering Works Limited
Result Review: SAZEW posts highest ever quarterly PAT in 3QFY26
REP-300
3QFY26: Earnings arrived at PKR 106.5/share; payout of PKR 20.0/share declared
•
Sazgar Engineering Works Limited (SAZEW) announced its financial results for 3QFY26,
reporting highest ever quarterly Profit After Tax (PAT) of PKR 6,438mn (EPS: PKR
106.5), reflecting a 3% YoY increase. Cumulatively, 9MFY26 earnings stood at PKR
14,879mn (EPS: PKR 246.2), marking a 16% YoY growth.
•
The company declared a cash dividend of PKR 20.0/share in 3QFY26, taking the total
payout to PKR 50.0/share in 9MFY26.
•
On a 9MFY26 basis, revenue clocked in at PKR 115,202mn, up 41% YoY. Net revenue
for 3QFY26 amounted to PKR 47,356mn, up 29% YoY, primarily driven by a sharp
increase in Haval volumes to 5,420 units (+47% YoY).
•
On a cumulative basis, gross margins for 9MFY26 declined to 25.6% from 30.5% in
9MFY25. In 3QFY26, margins stood at 26.8% compared to 32.6% in the same period last
year. The contraction is primarily driven by the absence of concessionary duties, impact
of PKR depreciation, and a shift in product mix toward higher contribution from petrol
variants.
•
On a 9MFY26 basis, distribution expense increased 62% YoY to PKR 4,171mn.
Distribution expense rose 62% YoY in 3QFY26, driven by higher deliveries due to
increased volumes.
•
Other income clocked in at PKR 761mn in 3QFY26, up 155% YoY, supported by higher
cash and cash equivalents.
•
Finance cost in 3QFY26 stood at PKR 102mn, up 98% YoY. For 9MFY26, finance cost
rose 50% YoY to PKR 301mn.
•
The effective tax rate for 3QFY26 came in at 39.1%, broadly in line with 39.0% for
9MFY26 vs. 38.9% in 9MFY25.
•
We have a BUY stance on SAZEW with Dec'26 target price of PKR 2,670. The scrip is
trading at FY26/FY27 P/E of 6.7x and 5.9x.
Menka Kirpalani | Leena Abid 
UAN: (021) 3828 0256
menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com | leena.abid@arifhabibltd.com

## REPORT 178
Title: AHL Alert: ENGRO - Result Review: Net Profit arrived at PKR 10.66/share in 1QCY24
Created At: 2024-04-25T12:32:09.182Z
Tickers: ENGRO
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 662a4d49615439187e020cd7
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662a4d49615439187e020cd7.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 April 2024
Profitability climbs up by 23% YoY
Engro Corporation Limited (ENGRO) announced its 1QCY24 financial result, posting a 
consolidated net profit of PKR 5,719mn (EPS: PKR 10.66) compared to PKR 4,647mn (EPS: 
PKR 8.66) in 1QCY23, up by 23% YoY. Alongside the result, the company announced an 
interim cash dividend of PKR 11.00/share in 1QCY24 (PKR 40.00/share in 1QCY23). 
Result Highlights 
•
On the fertilizer business front, EFERT’s earnings settled at PKR 10,784mn (EPS: PKR 
8.08), surging by 2x YoY during 1QCY24 owed to i) a hike in urea and DAP prices by 66% 
and 22% YoY, respectively, ii) a massive jump in DAP offtake by 85% YoY, and iii) fall in 
finance cost amid reduction in short term borrowings. 
•
Engro Polymer & Chemicals Limited (EPCL) registered a loss of PKR 901mn (LPS: PKR 
0.99) compared to net profit of PKR 1,183mn (EPS: PKR 1.30) due to higher gas prices 
and subdued PVC margins.
•
Although we await disclosure, we believe that the contribution Elengy business during 
1QCY24 stood at PKR 1,441mn.
•
The other income of ENGRO depicted a decline of 9% YoY during 1QCY24, which is 
attributable to a fall in income from cash and cash balances. 
•
The finance cost reduced by 7% YoY in 1QCY24 on account of a decrease in short term 
borrowings.
•
The company booked a reversal of the loss allowance on subsidy receivable from Govt. 
amounting PKR 58mn in 1QCY24 against the loss allowance on subsidiary receivable 
from Govt. of PKR 432mn in 1QCY23. 
•
The company’s booked effective taxation at 46% in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis 44% in 3QFY23
REP-300
Engro Corporation Limited

## REPORT 179
Title: AHL Alert: DGKC - Result Review: FY24 EPS @ PKR 1.24
Created At: 2024-09-16T10:56:24.667Z
Tickers: DGKC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 66e80ed80cbf3cea068db1ed
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66e80ed80cbf3cea068db1ed.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
16 September 2024
4QFY24: The company posted a loss of PKR 3.86/share
D.G. Khan Cement Company Limited (DGKC) announced its financial result for FY24
today, posting a profit of PKR 542mn (EPS: PKR 1.24) compared to a loss of PKR
3,636mn (LPS: PKR 8.30) in SPLY. During 4QFY24 the company reported a loss of PKR
1,692mn (LPS: PKR 3.86).
Result Highlights
•
Topline during FY24 arrived at PKR 66,039mn, depicting an uptick of 2% YoY in
contrast to PKR 64,984mn in SPLY, on the back of higher retention prices. However,
during 4QFY24 net sales remained flat at PKR 16,988mn.
•
Gross margins for FY24 climbed up by 124bps arriving at 16% owed to higher cement
prices in tandem with a fall in coal prices during the period. In 4QFY24 gross margins
came at 8% vis-à-vis 11% in SPLY. This was primarily attributable to increase in
exports arriving at 453k tons (up 5% YoY).
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in FY24 surged by 43% YoY to settle at PKR
2,609mn, on the back of elevated freight charges given higher export sales, we view.
In 4QFY24, selling and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 992mn vis-à-vis PKR
770mn, jump of 29% due to aforementioned reason.
•
Other income for FY24 ascended by 30% YoY, which is mainly due to higher dividend
income from MCB.
•
Finance costs in FY24 increased by 19% YoY to clock in at PKR 8,001mn on the back
of higher interest rate. In 4QFY24, the finance cost arrived at PKR 1,928mn,
displaying a rise of 3% YoY due to the reason stated above.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 81% in FY24 amid amendment made in tax
laws where normal tax is charged on the sale of exports (compared to presumptive
tax). During SPLY effective taxation arrived at 215% due to the imposition of super tax
which was retrospective
REP-300
D.G. Khan Cement Company Limited
Result Review: FY24 EPS @ PKR 1.24

## REPORT 180
Title: AHL Alert - MARI Earnings up 33% YoY to PKR 17.6/sh in 3QFY26
Created At: 2026-04-22T10:53:57.949Z
Tickers: MARI
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 69e8a8c51f328d6e6553174a
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69e8a8c51f328d6e6553174a.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
3QFY26: Bottomline arrived at PKR 17.6/share
Mari Energies Limited (MARI) announced its financial results today, posting earnings of
PKR 21.1bn (EPS: PKR 17.6/share) for 3QFY26, up 33% YoY. The increase in
profitability was driven by a 6% rise in net sales, and a lower effective tax rate booked
during the quarter.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 3QFY26 increased by 6% YoY to PKR 48.1bn, supported by a 13% YoY
rise in oil production to 1,282 bopd and a 4% YoY increase in gas production to 946
mmcfd. Within MARI’s key gas fields, HRL production grew by 2% YoY, while Shewa
production surged significantly by 11x YoY.
•
Exploration costs increased by 68% YoY to PKR 4.9bn in 3QFY26 and nearly 3x QoQ,
primarily driven by the dry well expense of Pario-1 recorded during the quarter. In
contrast, no dry well expenses were booked in 3QFY25 and 2QFY26.
•
Finance income declined by 29% YoY to PKR 1.3bn, primarily due to a lower interest
rate environment compared to SPLY.
•
The company recorded an tax of PKR 0.2bn in 3QFY26, translating into an effective
tax rate of 1%, compared to 29% in SPLY. We await further clarity from management
regarding the unusually low tax rate.
•
MARI’s cash position declined to PKR 58.6bn in 3QFY26 (2QFY26: PKR 60.8bn),
while trade receivables increased up to PKR 92.0bn (2QFY26: PKR 88.7bn).
MARI is currently trading at a forward FY26/FY27 multiples of 14x/9x P/E, offering a
dividend yield of 3%/5%.

## REPORT 181
Title: AHL Alert - UBL Highest PAT and DPS in CY25
Created At: 2026-02-25T10:08:07.679Z
Tickers: UBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 699eca07d33a93d06bd496db
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699eca07d33a93d06bd496db.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 25-Feb-2026 
Commercial Bank: United Bank Limited
Result Review: Highest PAT and DPS in CY25
REP-300

UBL has announced its CY25 earnings, posting its highest ever profit of PKR 130bn
(EPS: PKR 51.9), marking a remarkable growth of 73% YoY. On a quarterly basis,
the profit was recorded at PKR 29.9bn (EPS: PKR 11.95), +17% YoY and -15%
QoQ. The bank also announced its highest annual dividend of PKR 29.5/share,
compared to PKR 22/share in CY24.

The earnings growth was primarily driven by higher net interest income and
provisioning reversals recorded during the year.

The bank’s NII was recorded at PKR 362bn in CY25, growing significantly by 108%
YoY. The growth was due to growth in interest income and decline in interest
expensed. Interest income was reported at PKR 1.2trn (+9% YoY) while interest
expensed settled at PKR 823bn (-10% YoY).

Non-funded income contracted by 25% YoY to reach PKR 62.7bn in CY25. This
decline was attributable to lower gains booked on sale of securities (PKR 9.9bn, -78%
YoY). Meanwhile, the bank recorded improvements in fee income by 48% YoY at
PKR 32bn, FX income by 43% YoY at PKR 18bn, and dividend income by 34% YoY
at PKR 2.3bn.

On the provisioning front, the bank recognized a provisioning reversal of PKR 4.7bn
in CY25, compared to a provisioning expense of PKR 12.7bn in CY24.

OPEX grew by 38% to reach PKR 139bn, however strong income growth led to an
overall decline in cost-to-income, which settled at 32.7% in CY25 vs 39% in CY24.

The Effective Tax Rate was 55% for CY25 compared to 48% in CY24.

In terms of balance sheet dynamics, the bank’s deposits surpassed PKR 5trn in
CY25, reaching its highest ever recorded level, achieving a remarkable growth of
96% YoY. Meanwhile, investments also reached record high at PKR 9.9trn (+69%
YoY) while advances dropped to PKR 1.4trn (-5% YoY).

The stock is currently trading at a P/B multiple of 3x and a DY of 6.6% for CY26e
based on LDCP.

## REPORT 182
Title: AHL Alert: FCCL - Result Review: Highest ever quarterly profit
Created At: 2024-10-25T14:38:39.603Z
Tickers: FCCL
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 671bad6f8f84bab98c93ecbd
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671bad6f8f84bab98c93ecbd.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 October 2024
1QFY25: Profitability jumped by 24% YoY
Fauji Cement Company Limited (FCCL) announced its financial result for 1QFY25 today,
posting a highest ever quarterly profitability of PKR 3,247mn (EPS: PKR 1.32) compared to
earnings of PKR 2,614mn (EPS: PKR 1.07) in SPLY, an increase of 24% YoY. Furthermore,
on QoQ basis, earnings went up by 175% QoQ, due to the company recording additional
taxation in 4QFY24 on account of amendments made in tax laws, where normal tax is charged
on sales of exports compared to presumptive tax.
Result Highlights
•
Topline during 1QFY25 arrived at PKR 22,956mn, depicting an uptick of 13% YoY in
contrast to PKR 20,313mn in SPLY, on the back of higher retention prices along with
improved local dispatches. On a quarterly basis net sales grew by 11% QoQ, due to
aforementioned reasons.
•
Gross margins for 1QFY25 arrived at 34.3% against 31.1% SPLY, owing to i) higher
cement prices, ii) use of local coal and other cheap alternative fuel and iii) increased use of
captive generation. On a quarterly basis, gross margins declined by 190bps.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in 1QFY25 remained stable at PKR 693mn,down by 1%
YoY. Whereas, on QoQ basis selling and distribution expenses, declined by 5% QoQ,
•
Finance costs in 1QFY25 increased by 27% YoY to clock in at PKR 1,355mn. However, on
QoQ basis finance cost declined by 18% QoQ, amid fall in interest rates in tandem with
decline in borrowings.
•
The company booked effective taxation of 38% in 1QFY25 as compared to 35% during
SPLY.
REP-300
Fauji Cement Company Limited
Result Review: Highest ever quarterly profit

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 09-Feb-2026 
Commercial Banks: Meezan Bank Limited
Result Review: EPS clocked-in at PKR 50.4 in CY25
REP-300

MEBL reported profit after tax of PKR 21.4bn (EPS: PKR 11.88) for 4QCY25, reflecting
a 14% YoY decline. This takes CY25 profitability to PKR 90.7bn, down by 12% YoY.
Moreover, the bank announced a cash dividend of PKR 7.0/share, bringing the
cumulative payout for the year to PKR 28.0/share.

The earnings decline was driven by a 15% contraction in profit earned, resulting in
lower Net Profit Earned of PKR 252.4bn (-12% YoY).

Key support was provided by non-funded income (+20% YoY), limiting the total income
decline to 9% YoY.

Major contributors to non-funded income include FX Income (+561% YoY at PKR
5.5bn), fees and commission income (+15% YoY at PKR 29.8bn). Meanwhile lower
gains on sale of securities (PKR 989.0mn, -72% YoY) and mild dividend income (PKR
380mn, -39% YoY) acted as drags.

In terms of provisioning, the bank recognized a relatively lower provisioning expense of
PKR 3.9bn in CY25, down 58% YoY from PKR 9.2bn in CY24. OPEX grew by a
modest 3% YoY to PKR 89.8bn, bringing the cost-to-income ratio up to 31% in CY25
vs 29% in CY24.

The effective tax rate remained in line with prior levels at 54% for CY25 (vs 54.2% in
CY24).

On the balance sheet front, deposits hit an all-time high of PKR 3.3trn, increasing by
28% YoY (+4% QoQ). Bank’s Investments also surged by 39% YoY, reaching an all
time high of PKR 2.6trn, while advances rose by 8% YoY to reach PKR 1.6trn.
Valuation

The stock is currently trading at a P/B of 2.7x and a Dividend yield of 6.0% for CY26e.

## REPORT 184
Title: AHL Alert: KOHC - Result Review: 1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 22.74
Created At: 2024-02-14T12:07:28.509Z
Tickers: KOHC
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 65ccad0099cade535826a965

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY24: Earnings settled at PKR 11.35/share
Kohat Cement Company Limited (KOHC) announced its financial result for 1HFY24 
today, posting a PAT of PKR 4,453mn (EPS: PKR 22.74) compared to a PAT of PKR 
3,578mn (EPS: PKR 18.27) in SPLY, a growth of 24% YoY. On the other hand, the 
profitability in 2QFY24 swelled up by 14% YoY to PKR 2,224mn (EPS: PKR 11.35) 
compared to SPLY. 
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 1HFY24 clocked-in at PKR 21,462mn, slightly increasing by 2% YoY 
in contrast to PKR 20,972mn in SPLY, amid higher retention prices. On a quarterly 
basis, net sales fell by 5% YoY to settle at PKR 10,398mn primarily led by lower 
dispatches, due to drop in domestic demand.
•
Gross margins for 1HFY24 arrived at 28% compared to 25% in SPLY, on the back of 
higher cement price in tandem with decline in coal prices. Whereas, gross margins in 
2QFY24 settled at 26%, down by 100bps compared to SPLY, this is attributable to 
hike in national grid charges (currently at PKR 45/ unit).
•
Other income in 1HFY24 surged by 112% YoY to settled at PKR 2,066mn, due to  
increase in short term investments. Whereby in 2QFY24 other income settled at 
1,202mn (up 177% YoY). 
•
Finance costs arrived in 2QFY24 settled at 180mn vis-à-vis 170mn in SPLY, rise of 
6% YoY.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 34% in 2QFY24.

AHL Alert | 25-Apr-2025 
Commercial Bank: National Bank of Pakistan
Result Review: EPS @ PKR 10.3 in 1QCY25
REP-300

NBP announced its earnings (Profit After Tax) today for 1QCY25 at PKR 21.8bn, with
an EPS of PKR 10.3. This reflects a jump of 2.3x YoY but a decline of 2% QoQ
(4QCY24 EPS: PKR 10.6).

On a YoY basis, higher net interest income enhanced the overall profitability.

During 1QCY25, the bank reported its Net Interest Income at PKR 69.8bn, marking an
uptick of 139% compared to the same period last year and a 6% increase from the
previous quarter.

The bank experienced a decline in interest earned, which dipped by 22% YoY and 17%
QoQ. Moreover, the bank also faced a decline in interest expenses, which contracted
by 42% YoY and 24% compared to the last quarter.

During the outgoing quarter, the bank’s non-funded income witnessed a downturn of
11% YoY, while declined by 56% QoQ.

The downturn on YoY basis was primarily due to loss on sale of securities, which was
recorded at PKR 100mn compared to gain of PKR 4.4bn in SPLY.

Dividend income also declined by 1.1% YoY, settling at PKR 1.7bn. Moreover, other
income declined by 66.5% YoY to PKR 89mn

However, FX income depicted a jump of 13% YoY, while fee income increased by 47%
YoY to record at PKR 9.2bn.

The bank recorded a provisioning charge of PKR 6.4bn for 1QCY25 against
a
provisioning charge of PKR 727mn recorded in 1QCY24,

The bank’s OPEX increased by 30% YoY | -71% QoQ in 1QCY25 clocking in at PKR
29.2bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 35% in 1QCY25 against 52% SPLY

The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 53.1% during 1QCY25, showing a jump
from 51.9% in SPLY.

## REPORT 186
Title: AHL Alert: UBL - 2nd highest ever quarterly EPS
Created At: 2025-10-15T13:10:01.729Z
Tickers: AHL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 68ef9d29d5d1fae29bd20262
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/68ef9d29d5d1fae29bd20262.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Page 07
AHL Alert | 15-Oct-2025 
Commercial Bank: United Bank Limited
Result Review: 2nd highest ever quarterly EPS
REP-300

UBL has announced its 3QCY25 earnings, posting a quarterly profit of PKR 35.4bn
(EPS: PKR 14.12), marking a remarkable growth of 93% YoY, and 24% QoQ.

For 9MCY25, total earnings amounted to PKR 100.0bn, marking a 101% YoY increase.

Alongside the result, the bank announced a dividend of PKR 8.0/share, taking the total
payout for 9MCY25 to PKR 21.5/share. Moreover, on the balance sheet front, UBL’s
deposits reached a record high, surpassing PKR 4.7trn as of Sep’25.

During 9M, the bank’s earnings were driven mainly by a 154% YoY surge in Net Interest
Income and a provisioning reversal of PKR 4.7bn.

In the outgoing quarter of 3QCY25, the bank reported its Net Interest Income at PKR
91.9bn, marking a jump of 78% compared to the same period last year and a 1%
increase from the previous quarter.

The bank experienced an decrease in interest earned, which declined by 4% YoY and 2%
QoQ. Similarly, the bank faced a decline in interest expenses, which contracted by 21%
YoY and 3% QoQ.

During the outgoing quarter, the bank’s non-funded income declined by 12% QoQ and
13% YoY, mainly due to a 85% YoY drop in capital gains to PKR 901mn.

On a positive note, FX income climbed 46% YoY to PKR 5.0bn, while fee income
recorded a solid 20% YoY growth, reaching PKR 7.8bn in 3QCY25.

The bank recorded a provisioning reversal of PKR 822mn for 3QCY25 against
provisioning of PKR 894mn recorded in 3QCY24.

The bank’s OPEX increased by 36% YoY, while declined 9% QoQ in 3QCY25 clocking in
at PKR 32.5bn. With this, Cost/Income stood at 30.64% in 3QCY25 against 35.34%
SPLY.

The effective tax rate for the bank normalized to 52.6% during 3QCY25, showing an
decrease from 61.4% in the last quarter.

UBL is currently trading at a P/B of 2.0x.

## REPORT 187
Title: AHL Alert: MLCF -  Result Review: 9MFY24 EPS @ PKR 5.01
Created At: 2024-04-25T13:11:10.042Z
Tickers: MLCF
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 662a566eb3858eb2c7838361
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/662a566eb3858eb2c7838361.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 April 2024
3QFY24: Profitability declined by 20% YoY, settling at PKR 1.40/share
Maple Leaf Cement Factory Limited (MLCF) announced its financial result for 9MFY24 today, 
posting a PAT of PKR 5,374mn (EPS: PKR 5.01) compared to a PAT of PKR 6,185mn (EPS: 
PKR 5.76) in SPLY, down by 13% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the earnings settled at PKR 
1,505mn (EPS: PKR 1.40) in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis PKR 1,876mn (EPS: 1.75) in SPLY, registering 
a decline of 20% YoY.
Result Highlights 
•
Topline during 9MFY24 arrived at PKR 50,729mn in contrast to PKR 47,090mn in SPLY, 
depicting an uptick of 8% YoY. The growth in revenue comes on the back of higher 
retention prices. However, during 3QFY24 net sales declined by 6% YoY to settle at PKR 
15,980mn, which is primarily due to lower volumetric sales.
•
Gross margins for 9MFY24 remained unchanged at 32%. Likewise, in 3QFY24 gross 
margins remained stable at 30%.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in 9MFY24 witnessed a growth of 39% YoY to settle at 
PKR 4,112mn amid elevated freight charges given higher exports of 113k tons ( increasing 
by 31%) coupled with the implementation of axle load factor, we view. In 3QFY24, selling 
and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 1,316mn vis-à-vis PKR 1,305mn, a meagre 
uptick of 1%. 
•
Finance costs in 9MFY24 surged by 80% YoY to clock in at PKR 2,687mn due to higher 
policy rate. In 3QFY24, the finance cost arrived at PKR 825mn, displaying a jump of 102% 
YoY, due to aforementioned reason.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 35% in 3QFY24 vis-à-vis 30% in 3QFY23.
REP-300
AHL Alert: Cement

Page 07
AHL Alert | 24-Apr-2025 
Commercial Bank: Faysal Bank Limited
Result Review: EPS @ PKR 3.6 in 1QCY25
REP-300

FABL announced earnings (PAT) today for 1QCY25 at PKR 5.4bn (EPS: PKR 3.6), depicting a
decline of 18% YoY while a jump of 11% QoQ (4QCY24 EPS: PKR 2.34).

The bank's earnings contracted by a YoY dip in NII and higher OPEX.

Along with the result, FABL announced a cash dividend of PKR 1.5/share.

In the 1QCY25, the bank reported a Net Profit Earned of PKR 17.2bn, reflecting a 9% decline
YoY, while declined by 16% on QoQ. The profit earned by the bank saw a 30% dip YoY and
experienced a 14% decline QoQ. At the same time, profit expensed declined by 40% YoY, while
contracted by 16% compared to the previous quarter.

In the outgoing quarter, the bank experienced a 15% increase in Non-Funded Income YoY,
driven primarily by higher Fee income, which rose by 31.6% YoY, and FX income, which
increased by 8.1% YoY. However, on a QoQ basis, the Non-Funded Income was impacted
negatively by 3% due to lower dividend income, which fell by 65% QoQ, and a decrease in Gain
on Securities by 83% QoQ.

The bank posted provisioning reversal amounting to PKR 2.3bn in 1QCY25, compared to a
charge of PKR 36mn in the same period last year and a charge of PKR 36mn in the same
period last year.

OPEX witnessed an uptick of 24% YoY while declined by 11% QoQ in 1QCY25, clocking-in at
PKR 13.9bn for the bank. With this, the Cost/Income ratio stood at 60.3% for 1QCY25, up from
47.02% in the same period last year.

The effective tax rate for the bank was set at 52.9% during 1QCY25, showing an increase from
48.0% in the same period last year.
Recommendation
We maintain our Buy recommendation on FABL, with a Dec'25 target price of PKR 60.6/share. The 
stock is currently trading at forward valuations, with a P/E multiple of 3.8x for CY25 and 3.7x for 
CY26, while the forward P/B stands at 0.62x for CY25 and 0.58x for CY26.

## REPORT 189
Title: AHL Alert: HBL -Result Review: 4QCY23 EPS @ PKR 10.12
Created At: 2024-02-23T11:04:46.164Z
Tickers: HBL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 65d87bcec954d41abab4232a
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65d87bcec954d41abab4232a.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Historic high profitability recorded in CY23
HBL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY23 at PKR 14.8bn (EPS: PKR 10.12),
depicting an increase of 39% YoY | -11% QoQ (4QCY22 EPS: PKR 7.27). This jump in
overall earnings was mainly on the back of growth in total revenue during the year. Along
with the result, the bank announced a final DPS of PKR 4, taking the total dividend for
the year to PKR 9.75/share (CY22: PKR 6.75/share).
Result Highlights
•
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 64.2bn during 4QCY23, increasing
30% YoY | -1% QoQ basis. With this, the total NII for CY23 went up to PKR 242bn,
marking a 46% YoY jump. Interest earned saw a jump of 63% during CY23 while
interest expense was up by 74%.
•
NFI depicted an increase of 83% YoY and whopping 56% QoQ during 4QCY23, taking
CY23’s total to PKR 57.4bn (+23% YoY). This jump in CY23 is primarily due to 34%
and 53% (on a YoY basis) in fee and dividend income, respectively. On the contrary,
the FX income came down 61% YoY during CY23, clocking-in at PKR 4.6bn.
•
Provisioning for the bank clocked-in at PKR 5.8bn during 4QCY23 (4QCY22: PKR
5.0bn) depicting an increase of 19% on a YoY basis. This takes total provisioning to
PKR 13.3bn during CY23 (+56% YoY).
•
The bank’s OPEX increased 39% YoY in 4QCY23 clocking-in at PKR 47.7bn
(4QCY22: PKR 34.3bn). With this, the Cost/Income ratio remained unchanged at
56.3% in 4QCY23 compared to the same period previous year.
•
Effective tax rate was set at 49% during 4QCY23 compared to 48% last quarter. With
this, the effective tax for CY23 stood at 49% (CY22: 55%).
1

## REPORT 190
Title: HCAR - Result Review: 1QMY25 EPS clocked-in at PKR 1.42
Created At: 2024-07-24T09:11:39.43Z
Tickers: HCAR
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 66a0c54bfb2c98ca2ef998dc
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66a0c54bfb2c98ca2ef998dc.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
24 July 2024
1QMY25: Profitability increased by 40% YoY, settling at 1.42/share. 
Honda Atlas Cars (Pakistan) Limited (HCAR) announced its financial result for 1QMY25 
today, posting a PAT of PKR 203mn (EPS: PKR 1.42), representing YoY increase of 40%. 
The YoY increase is attributed to better gross margins which were recorded significantly up 
by 1,020bps. On a sequential basis, the earnings declined by 85% QoQ.
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales during 1QMY25 clocked in at PKR 15,970mn in contrast to PKR 3,771mn in 
SPLY, depicting an increase of 4.2x YoY primarily due to high customer purchasing power 
and low base effect caused by the plant shutdown in SPLY. On a sequential basis, the 
revenue declined  by 36% QoQ amid decline in sales volumes by 35% QoQ to 3,285 units
vis-à-vis 5,044 units.
•
Gross margins in 1QMY25 arrived at 6.3% vis-à-vis -3.9% in 1QMY24, depicting a 
massive surge of 1020bps, mainly due to a stable PKR-USD parity.
•
Other income declined by 62% YoY to clock in at PKR 344mn in 1QMY25, which is mainly 
because of reduction in cash and cash equivalent during the period under review.
•
Finance cost surged by 6.6x YoY to clock in at PKR 287mn in 1QMY25 in contrast to PKR 
44mn during SPLY, primarily due to increase in short term borrowings.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 47% in 1QMY25 vis-à-vis  46% in SPLY.

## REPORT 191
Title: AHL Alert: SYS - Result Review: Earnings arrived at PKR 5.39/share in 1QCY24
Created At: 2024-04-30T06:53:27.351Z
Tickers: SYS
Sectors: 0828
Item ID: 66309567b3858eb2c75b5aac
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66309567b3858eb2c75b5aac.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
30 April 2024
Profitability declined by 59% during the quarter
Systems Limited (SYS) announced the financial result for 1QCY24 whereby the company
posted a profit after tax (PAT) of PKR 1,571mn (EPS: PKR 5.39) in contrast to PKR
3,879mn (EPS: 13.32) during 1QCY23, down by 59% YoY due to exchange loss during the
quarter compared to massive exchange gain in SPLY. On a sequential basis, the earnings
witnessed an uptick of 2% QoQ.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in 1QCY24 clocked in at PKR 15,194mn, up by 42% YoY which is on account of
32%, 35%, and 49% YoY growth in technology-related solutions, Banking Financial
Services & Insurance (BFSI), and telecommunications services, respectively.
•
Gross margins in 1QCY24 arrived at 23.06% vis-à-vis 26.08% in 1QCY23, which is due
to higher energy costs and inflationary adjustments during the quarter.
•
The company booked an expense of PKR 57mn in 1QCY24 compared to other income of
PKR 2,552mn in SPLY amid exchange losses during the quarter against significant
exchange gains in SPLY.
•
Finance cost plummeted by 6% YoY, clocking in at PKR 148mn during 1QCY24 owed to
reduction in the short-term borrowings.
•
Administrative and Distribution expenses augmented by 36%YoY, which is due to
inflationary pressure.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 11% in 1QCY24 vis-à-vis 3% in 1QCY23.

## REPORT 192
Title: AHL Alert: BAFL - Result Review: 4QCY23 EPS @ PKR 5.74
Created At: 2024-02-01T10:13:34.124Z
Tickers: BAFL
Sectors: 0807
Item ID: 65bb6ece17c89d20e25529b3
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65bb6ece17c89d20e25529b3.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
Achieving historic high profitability through higher income and lower provisioning
BAFL announced earnings (PAT) today for 4QCY23 at PKR 9.1bn (EPS: PKR 5.74), 
depicting an increase of 120% YoY | 2% QoQ (4QCY22 EPS: PKR 2.61). This jump in 
overall earnings was mainly on the back of growth in total income. Along with the result, 
BAFL announced a final dividend of PKR 5/share (CY23: PKR 8/share). 
Result Highlights 
•
Net Interest Income of the bank settled at PKR 35.1bn during 4QCY23, increasing 
51% YoY and 10% QoQ. With this, the total NII for CY23 went up to PKR 126bn, 
marking a 63% YoY jump. Interest earned saw a jump of 92% during CY23 while 
interest expense was up by 109%.
•
NFI depicted an increase of 128% YoY | 69% QoQ during 4QCY23, taking CY23’s 
total to PKR 30.1bn (+32% YoY). This jump in 4QCY23 is primarily due to higher FX 
gains, which were recorded at PKR 2.0bn (+130% YoY) on the back of volatile 
PKR/USD parity. In addition, the bank fee and commission income posted a jump of 
49% YoY, clocking in at PKR 4.5bn during the out-going quarter. Moreover, during 
4QCY23, BAFL also posted gains on securities to the tune of PKR 1.7bn as compared 
to a loss recorded in SPLY of PKR 46mn.
•
Provisioning for the bank clocked in at PKR 1.2bn during 4QCY23 (4QCY22: PKR 
4.3bn) depicting a decline of 73% on a YoY basis. This takes total provisioning to PKR 
10.3bn during CY23 (-17% YoY).
•
The bank’s OPEX increased 28% YoY in 4QCY23 clocking-in at PKR 19.1bn 
(4QCY22: PKR 14.9bn). With this, Cost/Income stood at 42% in 4QCY23 against 
53.7% same period last year. 
•
Effective tax rate was set at 66% during 4QCY23 compared to 51% last quarter. With 
this, the effective tax for CY23 stood at 54% (CY22: 50%).
1

## REPORT 193
Title: AHL Alert: EFERT - Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 21.16 in CY24
Created At: 2025-02-10T07:16:01.999Z
Tickers: EFERT
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 67a9a7b103d71c41374cfc5e
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/67a9a7b103d71c41374cfc5e.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
10 February 2025
4QCY24: Bottom-line plummeted by 8% YoY
Engro Fertilizers Limited (EFERT) today unveiled the financial result for CY24 posting a
consolidated profit after Tax (PAT) of PKR 28,260mn (EPS: PKR 21.16) against PKR
26,191mn (EPS: PKR 19.61) in CY23, up by 8% YoY. Whereas, consolidated earnings in
4QCY24 clocked in at PKR 10,280mn (EPS: PKR 7.70), down 8% YoY. Alongside the result,
the company announced a final cash dividend of PKR 8.00/share (PKR 21.50/share in CY24).
Result Highlights
•
Net sales in CY24 clocked in at PKR 256,675mn, up 15% YoY which is on account of 39%
and 9% YoY hike in urea and DAP price, respectively tagged with 10% YoY jump in DAP
offtake. Meanwhile, urea sales declined by 13% YoY on the back of 54 days closure of
EnVen plant for maintenance. On a quarterly basis, the topline during 4QCY24 increased by
13% YoY, settling at PKR 84,830mn, which is due to 17% jump in urea offtake. Whereas,
DAP sales reduced by 5% YoY, respectively.
•
Gross margins in CY24 arrived at 28.2% vis-à-vis 32.3% in CY23. Whereas, gross margins
plummeted by 378bps, settling at 34.9% amid higher gas prices.
•
The distribution cost in CY24 clocked in at PKR 17,855mn, up by 37% YoY. Meanwhile,
during 4QCY24, the distribution cost of the company surged by 2x YoY due to higher
volumetric sales.
•
Other income declined by 21% YoY settling at PKR 2,925mn in CY24 given dip in income
from cash and cash balances. Meanwhile, in 4QCY24, the other income came out to be PKR
511mn, down 72% YoY owing to lower interest income.
•
Finance cost climbed up by 2x YoY, clocking in at PKR 4,129mn during CY24 owed to
higher interest rates. Whereas, the finance cost in 4QCY24 swelled up by 5x YoY, reaching
PKR 1,467mn due to higher borrowings.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 38% in 4QCY24 vs 44% in 4QCY23.
REP-300
Engro Fertilizers Limited
Result Review: EPS arrives at PKR 21.16 in CY24
AHL Alert: Fertilizer

## REPORT 194
Title: Result Review: ILP -1HFY24 EPS @ PKR 7.03, up 2.15x YoY
Created At: 2024-02-20T15:01:52.613Z
Tickers: ILP
Sectors: 0829
Item ID: 65d4bee0c954d41aba1efa7d
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/65d4bee0c954d41aba1efa7d.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY24: Profitability arrive at PKR 2.72/share
Interloop Limited (ILP) announced the financial result for 1HFY24 today, where the company
posted a PAT of PKR 9,857mn (EPS: PKR 7.03) against PKR 4,585mn (EPS: PKR 3.27) in
1HFY23, up by 115% YoY. Meanwhile, the consolidated earnings in 2QFY24 clocked in at
PKR 3,814mn (EPS: PKR 2.72), in contrast to a loss after tax of PKR 376mn (LPS: PKR
0.27) in 2QFY23. In addition to the result, the company announced an interim cash dividend
of PKR 2.00/share (PKR 2.00/share in 1HFY24).
Result Highlights

Net sales during 1HFY24 clocked in at PKR 73,984mn, climbing up by 40% YoY amid
higher hosiery pricing and volumetric growth. On quarterly basis, the topline ascended
by 58% YoY, settling at PKR 35,484mn on account of PKR depreciation against the
greenback.

Gross margins for 1HFY24 arrived at 30.6% compared to 25.8% in SPLY, owed to
higher hosiery and yarn prices in tandem with the decline in cotton prices. Whereas
gross margins in 2QFY24 reached 27.9% (up by 1202bps YoY), which is attributable
to the absence of export rebate during the same period.

Other income in 1HFY24 surged by 81x YoY to settle at PKR 1,210mn. Whereas, in
2QFY24 other income settled at 1,079mn (up 28x YoY) mainly due to surplus on the
acquisition of subsidiaries of PKR 857mn.

Finance costs in arrive 2QFY24 settled at PKR 2,595mn vis-à-vis PKR 1,414mn in
SPLY, an increase of 84% YoY due to higher interest charged on elevated short-term
borrowings

The company booked effective taxation at 11.8% in 2QFY24.
1
AHL Alert: Textile Composite

## REPORT 195
Title: AHL Alert: POL- Result Review: Earnings clocked in at PKR 9.05 in 1QFY25
Created At: 2024-10-18T11:28:08.045Z
Tickers: POL
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 671246488f84bab98cb31baa
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671246488f84bab98cb31baa.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
18 October 2024
Higher dry well cost takes toll on the bottom-line
Pakistan Oilfields Limited (POL) announced its financial result today, posting a profit after
tax of PKR 2,569mn (EPS: PKR 9.05) during 1QFY25, which is the lowest quarterly
earnings since COVID-19 (4QFY20), compared to PKR 9,708mn (EPS: PKR 34.20) in
1QFY24, significantly down 74% YoY. Similarly, the net profit on QoQ basis contracted by
72%.
Result Highlights
•
Topline in 1QFY25 witnessed a decline of 7% YoY, settling at PKR 15,451mn
compared to PKR 16,682mn during SPLY amid i) reduction in oil and gas production by
6% and 4% YoY, respectively, ii) 6% YoY decline in average realized oil prices, and iii)
appreciation of Pak Rupee by 5% YoY against greenback. On a sequential basis, the
net sales witnessed a 3% QoQ growth given 8% and 13% QoQ growth in oil and gas
production, respectively. Meanwhile, the realized oil prices dwindled by 3% QoQ.
•
The exploration costs depicted a massive surge of 11x YoY | 48x QoQ in 1QFY25,
arriving at PKR 7,735mn or PKR 27.25/share owing to higher cost of dry well Balkassar
Deep 1A.
•
The other income registered a decline of 23% YoY | 22% QoQ, settling at PKR
3,746mn during 1QFY25 given absence of exchange gains and lower income from
cash and cash balances.
•
The company’s effective taxation arrived at 45% in 1QFY25 vis-à-vis 30% in 1QFY24.
REP-300
Pakistan Oilfields Limited

## REPORT 196
Title: AHL Alert - OGDC Highest-ever dividend per share for 1HFY26
Created At: 2026-02-23T07:23:37.088Z
Tickers: OGDC
Sectors: 0820
Item ID: 699c0079d33a93d06bc45d14
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/699c0079d33a93d06bc45d14.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
2QFY26 earnings arrived at PKR 8.07/share, down by 16% YoY I DPS at PKR 4.25
Oil and Gas Development Company (OGDC) reported a 16% YoY decline in 2QFY26 earnings to
PKR 34.7bn (EPS: PKR 8.07) from PKR 41.4bn (EPS: PKR 9.63), mainly due to a 13% drop in oil
prices, lower gas output, higher exploration costs, and reduced other income. The company
declared a dividend of PKR 4.25/share, raising the payout ratio to 53% (vs. 42% in 2QFY25) and
taking 1HFY26 DPS to a record PKR 7.8/share, supported by improved liquidity from receivable
recoveries, including TFCs.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales declined by 4% YoY to PKR 97bn, reflecting weaker gas output and lower oil prices,
while oil production improved. Gas production declined across key fields, including Uch (-11.7%
YoY due to ATA and weak power demand), Qadirpur (-26.6% YoY), TAL (-5.0% YoY), and KPD
(-2.2% YoY). On the oil side, Nashpa output rose 2.1% YoY, while TAL and KPD blocks
recorded strong growth of 14.2% YoY (13,331 bopd – not stake adjusted) and 19.3% YoY
(10,240 bopd), respectively.
•
Exploration costs rose by 119% YoY in 2QFY26, amounting to PKR 8.8bn, following the plug
and abandonment of the Jakhro North-1 well and Khatian-1.
•
Other income declined by 29.2% YoY to PKR 14.8bn in 2QFY26, primarily due to lower finance
income amid a declining interest rate environment. The impact was partially offset by the
remaining unwinding of TFC-related losses.
•
In 2QFY26, OGDC posted a strong 130% recovery ratio, likely supported by Uch receivables,
reducing trade receivables to PKR 583.8bn from PKR 612.8bn in 1QFY26. Meanwhile, cash and
short-term investments rose to PKR 256bn (PKR 56/share) in Dec’25, up from PKR 222bn (PKR
52/share) in Sep’25.
•
The effective tax rate in 2QFY26 stood at 29.0%, compared to 43.0% in 2QFY25.
We maintain a Buy stance on OGDC, with a Dec’26 target price of PKR 347/share. The stock is
trading at FY26e and FY27f P/E multiples of 8.2x and 7.2x, respectively.
REP-300
Zayan Babar Khan | Muhammad Anas Habib
D: +92 21 38280256
zayan.babar@arifhabibltd.com | anas.habib@arifhabibltd.com 
1
AHL Alert | 23-Feb-2026

## REPORT 197
Title: AHL Alert: FFC - Result Review: EPS clocked in at PKR 33.45 in 9MCY24
Created At: 2024-10-28T11:36:53.161Z
Tickers: FFC
Sectors: 0809
Item ID: 671f77558f84bab98c0fd5a8
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/671f77558f84bab98c0fd5a8.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
28 October 2024
All time high quarterly profit posted during the 3QCY24
Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited (FFC) disclosed its financial result today where the 
company posted net profit of PKR 42,552mn (EPS: PKR 33.45) during 9MCY24 against 
PKR 22,214mn (EPS: PKR 17.46) in 9MCY23, up 92% YoY. On the other hand, the 
profitability in 3QCY24 augmented by 80% YoY to PKR 16,481mn (EPS: PKR 12.95). The 
company has skipped quarterly dividend for the first time (based on reports available till 
2002), due to the ongoing amalgamation process. The company is likely to resume dividend 
post merger with FFBL, as per notice issued by the company.  
Result Highlights 
•
Net sales climbed up by 43% YoY during 9MCY24, settling at PKR 165,914mn on 
account of a jump in urea and DAP price by 51% and 15% YoY, respectively. Similarly, 
the revenue on a quarterly basis increased by 14% YoY in 3QCY24 amid hike in urea 
and DAP price by 32% and 9% YoY, respectively. Meanwhile, the urea and DAP offtake 
declined by 12% and 58% YoY, respectively.
•
The gross margins during 9MCY24 arrived at 44.91% compared to 38.66% in SPLY amid 
better urea and DAP margins. Whereas, gross margins in 3QCY24 arrived at 51.84% (up 
by 2,091bps YoY) due to aforementioned reason.  
•
Financial charges increased by 5% YoY, clocking in at PKR 4,189mn in 9MCY24 amid 
higher interest rates. Meanwhile, the finance charges during 3QCY24 decreased by 3% 
YoY to PKR 1,304mn on account of lower interest rates.   
•
Other income ascended by 98% YoY, clocking in at PKR 24,841mn in 9MCY24, given  
higher dividend income from associates, and subsidiaries. Meanwhile, the other income 
during 3QCY24 ascended by 46% YoY on account of i) dividend income from wind power 
segment, and ii) increase in income from cash and cash balances.
•
The company booked effective taxation at 36% in 3QCY24 vis-à-vis 34% in 3QCY23. 
REP-300
Fauji Fertilizer Company Limited
Result Review: EPS clocked in at PKR 33.45 in 9MCY24

## REPORT 198
Title: AHL Alert: LUCK - Result Review: FY24 EPS @ PKR 95.93
Created At: 2024-08-09T07:52:23.516Z
Tickers: LUCK
Sectors: 0804
Item ID: 66b5cab7311a3f7dcced42ce
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=200&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/66b5cab7311a3f7dcced42ce.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
9 August 2024
4QFY24: Earnings surged by 265% clocking at PKR 32.3/share
•
Lucky Cement Limited (LUCK) announced its financial result for FY24 today, posting an
unconsolidated earnings of PKR 28,107mn (EPS: PKR 95.93), showcasing a hefty jump of
105% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the unconsolidated net profit arrived at PKR 9,458mn
(EPS: PKR 32.3) compared to PKR 2,592mn (EPS: PKR 8.85) in SPLY, depicting a rise of
265% YoY. That said, consolidated earnings ascended by 34% YoY, settling at PKR
65,556mn (EPS: PKR 223.7) in FY24. In 4QFY24 profitability arrived at PKR 14,630mn
(EPS: PKR 49.9) vis-à-vis PKR 11,814mn (EPS: PKR 40.32), an increase of 24% YoY due
to i) rise in both local and foreign cement operations, ii) increase in profitability from LEPCL
and iii) jump in other income. In addition to the result, the company announced final cash
dividend of PKR 15.0/share
Result Highlights
•
Topline during FY24 arrived at PKR 115,325mn in contrast to PKR 95,832mn in SPLY,
depicting a jump of 20% YoY. The growth in the revenue comes on the back of higher
retention prices in tandem with an increase in dispatches (up 16% YoY). Moreover, during
4QFY24 net sales climbed up by 10% YoY to settle at PKR 27,921mn, amid higher cement
prices and jump in offtakes to 2,156k tons (up 12% YoY).
•
Gross margins for FY24 arrived at 34% as compared to 27% in SPLY, on the back of higher
retention prices coupled with better inventory management. Furthermore, in 4QFY24 gross
margins ascended by 469bps as compared to SPLY arriving at 32%, due to lower coal
prices in tandem with higher cement prices.
•
Selling and Distribution expenses in FY24 witnessed a growth of 46% YoY to settle at PKR
7,774mn as compared to SPLY, owing to the implementation of axle load factor. In
4QFY24, selling and distribution expenses arrived at PKR 2,129mn vis-à-vis PKR 1,408mn,
a surge of 51%, due to the reason stated above.

The company booked effective taxation at 25% in 4QFY24 vis-à-vis 54% in 4QFY23, amid the imposition of a super tax in last year.

## REPORT 199
Title: AHL Alert - PSO 3QFY26 EPS clocked-in at PKR 55.4
Created At: 2026-04-29T10:23:48.907Z
Tickers: PSO
Sectors: 0821
Item ID: 69f1dc349730304360c1af24
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=0&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/69f1dc349730304360c1af24.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
3QFY26 earnings arrived at PKR 55.4/share, up by 6.4x YoY and 9.5x QoQ
Pakistan State Oil Limited (PSO) reported 9MFY26 earnings of PKR 38bn which translates
into 81.2/share, up by 150% YoY. In 3QFY26 earnings of PKR 26bn (EPS: PKR 55.4) were
recorded, which were up by 6.4x YoY.
Result Highlights
•
In 9MFY26, PSO’s sales declined by 4% YoY to PKR 2,241bn. In 3QFY26, the topline
was recorded at PKR 742bn, up by 4% YoY. The increase is mainly due to higher
average prices amid high international oil prices. MS and HSD prices increased YoY by
PKR 30/ltr to PKR 285ltr and PKR 37/ltr to PKR 298/ltr, respectively, alongside volume
growth of 9% YoY in MS and 20% YoY in HSD during the quarter.
•
However, RLNG topline remained under pressure as PSO’s average DES price declined
to USD 7.55/mmbtu from USD 8.93/mmbtu in the SPLY. During the quarter, the
company handled 21 cargoes (only 2 in Mar’26 due to supply disruptions amid global
tensions) versus 27 cargoes in the same period last year.
•
Gross margin in 9MFY26 rose to 5.96% vs 3.14% in SPLY. In 3QFY26, it increased to
11.65% vs 3.17%, driven by inventory gains amid a sharp rise in international oil prices.
Estimated inventory gains stood at ~PKR 64bn during the quarter.
•
Other expenses increased by 10.4x YoY mainly due to one-offs recorded in WPPF and
WWF during the period, we view.
•
Finance costs declined 23% YoY to PKR 5.9bn in 3QFY26 from PKR 7.7bn, mainly due
to lower short-term borrowings of PKR 288bn as of 3QFY26 (PKR 361bn SPLY) and
reduced interest rates.
•
The company booked an effective taxation at 59.9% in 3QFY26 compared to 64.3% in
3QFY25.
•
PSO’s receivables increased by PKR 43bn QoQ as of Mar’26. Since Dec’23 (when gas
price were increased), they have declined by PKR 93bn, (PKR 455bn as of Mar’26).
•
We have a “BUY” call on PSO. The stock is trading at FY26 and FY27 P/E of 3.7 and
5.8x.
REP-300
Zayan Babar Khan | Menka Kirpalani
D: +92 21 38280256
zayan.babar@arifhabibltd.com | menka.kumari@arifhabibltd.com

## REPORT 200
Title: AHL Alert: MTL - Result Review: 9MFY25 EPS clocked-in at PKR 26.32
Created At: 2025-04-25T12:12:18.162Z
Tickers: MTL
Sectors: 0801
Item ID: 680b7c22238b86d3f8eb4963
Source URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/list/res?count=100&offset=100&category=648c11950ffa16ff648002ae
PDF URL: https://www.arifhabibltd.com/api/research/open?path=178/680b7c22238b86d3f8eb4963.pdf

--- Page 1 ---
25 April 2025
3QFY25 earnings arrived at PKR 7.24/share, down by 56% YoY I 54% QoQ
Millat tractors Limited (MTL) announced its financial result for 9MFY25 today whereby the
company posted a Profit After Tax (PAT) of PKR 5,049mn (EPS: PKR 26.32), decreasing
by 40% YoY. During 3QFY25, the profitability of the company amounted to PKR 1,388mn
(EPS: PKR 7.24) decreasing by 56% YoY I 54% QoQ.
Result Highlights
•
Net sales arrived at PKR 39,986mn during 9MFY25, down by 43% YoY. The decline is
attributed to lower volumetric sales to 14,518 units in 9MFY25 vis-à-vis 23,093 units in
9MFY24, down by 37% YoY. On a quarterly basis, the revenue declined by 50% YoY I
36% QoQ amid decline in sales volumes by 45% YoY (3QFY25: 4,411units vs 3QFY24:
7,994 units). On QoQ basis, the volumes also declined by 42%.
•
In 3QFY25, gross margins were recorded at 28.3%, compared to 24.3% in the same
period last year and 25.5% in the previous quarter. The improvement in margins was
mainly due to higher sales of high-priced tractors, we view. Additionally, stable parity
also contributed to the improvement in margins.
•
Other income declined by 82% YoY to clock in at PKR 67mn in 3QFY25, which is mainly
because of reduction in cash and cash equivalent and decline in interest rates during the
period under review.
•
Finance cost in 3QFY25 increased by 46% YoY amid increase in short-term borrowings.
•
The company recorded an effective taxation of 37.9% in 3QFY25 vis-à-vis 33.7% in the
same period last year.
REP-300
Millat Tractors Limited
